translations.source: Mnamo 1996, katika Mkutano wa 49 wa Afya Ulimwenguni, Nchi Wanachama ziliamua kuharibu hisa mnamo Juni 1999, na kuruhusu tu sampuli ndogo kuhifadhiwa kwa madhumuni ya utafiti katika maabara mbili zilizowekwa salama - moja nchini Merika na moja katika Urusi. Uhifadhi wa muda wa hifadhi zilizopo za virusi vya variola hutumiwa kwa utafiti zaidi muhimu, ambao unasimamiwa na Kamati ya Ushauri ya Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola. Kikundi hiki hukutana kila mwaka. Sekretarieti ya Ugonjwa wa Ndui ya WHO iliyoko Makao Makuu ya WHO inaratibu shughuli zote zinazohusiana na kutokomeza ndui na baada ya kutokomeza. Hizi ni pamoja na kusimamia utafiti unaoidhinishwa na virusi vya variola hai kwa ajili ya kutengeneza hatua za kupinga kama vile chanjo na dawa za kuzuia virusi. Tangu enzi ya kutokomeza, chanjo salama na matibabu mahususi yametengenezwa kwa ugonjwa wa ndui na magonjwa yanayohusiana kama vile ndui ya nyani. Sekretarieti pia inawajibika kwa maandalizi ya dharura na inasimamia hifadhi ya dharura ya chanjo ya ndui katika tukio lisilowezekana kwamba ugonjwa huo utaibuka tena. Sekretarieti inasimamia ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na usalama wa viumbe hai wa hifadhi zilizoidhinishwa za virusi vya variola kama ilivyoagizwa na Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni, na kuhakikisha ripoti ya kila mwaka kwa Mashirika Yanayoongoza ya WHO. Sekretarieti ya Ndui inaunga mkono Hifadhi za WHO katika kuhifadhi historia ya kimataifa ya ndui na kuweka kumbukumbu za mafunzo tuliyopata kutokana na kutokomeza kabisa ugonjwa wa ndui. Majukumu ya sasa ya Sekretarieti na wajibu wa Ndui kwa WHO ni pamoja na:
translations.reference: In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States decided to have this stock destroyed in June 1999, and only to allow small samples to be kept for research purposes at two designated secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in the Russian Federation. Temporary retention of the existing stocks of variola virus are used for further essential research, which is overseen by the Advisory Committee for Variola Virus Research. This group meets on an annual basis. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat based in WHO Headquarters coordinates all smallpox eradication-related and post-eradication activities. These include overseeing permitted research with live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccine and antivirals. Since the eradication era, safer vaccines and specific treatments have been developed for smallpox and related diseases such as monkeypox. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages a smallpox vaccine emergency stockpile in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat manages the biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the authorized variola virus repositories as mandated by the World Health Assembly, and ensures annual reporting to WHO Governing Bodies. The Smallpox Secretariat supports WHO Archives in preserving the global history of smallpox and documenting the lessons learned from successful eradication of smallpox. The WHO Smallpox Secretariat's current roles and responsibilities include:
translations.hypothesis: In 1996, at the 49th World Health Assembly, Member States agreed to destroy all remaining stocks of variola virus by June 1999, and to allow only small samples to be kept for research purposes in two secure laboratories – one in the United States and one in Russia. The temporary suspension of variola virus stockpiles allows for further important research, which is managed by the Variola Virus Research Advisory Committee. This committee meets annually. The WHO's Smallpox Eradication Programme Secretariat, based at WHO headquarters, coordinates all activities related to the eradication and post-eradication of smallpox. This includes overseeing research on live variola virus for the development of countermeasures such as vaccines and antiviral drugs. Since the eradication of smallpox, safe and effective vaccines and treatments for smallpox and related diseases have been developed. The Secretariat is also responsible for emergency preparedness and manages the emergency stockpile of smallpox vaccine in the unlikely event that the disease re-emerges. The Secretariat oversees the security of the variola virus stocks and ensures that they comply with the World Health Assembly's requirements, and provides an annual report to the WHO's leading organizations. The Smallpox Eradication Programme Secretariat supports the WHO's efforts to preserve the international history of smallpox and to document the lessons learned from its complete eradication. The current responsibilities of the Secretariat and the Smallpox Eradication Programme for WHO include:

translations.source: Utafiti wa virusi vya Variola na maendeleo ya hatua za kupinga kuandaa mikutano ya kila mwaka ya Kamati ya Ushauri ya WHO kuhusu Utafiti wa Virusi vya Variola (ACVVR) kuratibu mapitio ya mapendekezo ya utafiti wa kufanya kazi na virusi vya variola kupitia ACVVR kusimamia mahusiano hazina na virusi viwili vya variola (ambazo pia ni Vituo vya Kushirikiana vya WHO) kurekodi, kuchakata na kufuatilia maombi kutoka kwa watafiti wa DNA ya virusi vya variola kutoka kwa hazina 2 chini ya sheria maalum. kufanya ukaguzi wa usalama wa viumbe na ulinzi wa viumbe hifadhi 2 za kimataifa katika hazina za virusi vya variola kuendeleza mwongozo wa kawaida kuhusu utafiti juu ya DNA ya virusi vya variola na masuala yanayohusiana Maandalizi ya dharura na majibu kuratibu na kuandaa mwongozo wa kawaida juu ya ukuzaji na utumiaji wa hatua za matibabu kwa virusi vya orthopox. kuandaa mikakati ya kujiandaa na kukabiliana, na zana za kutekeleza hatua za kujitayarisha
translations.reference: Variola virus research and development of countermeasures organizing annual meetings of the WHO Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) coordinating review of research proposals for work with variola virus through the ACVVR managing relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres) recording, processing and monitoring requests from researchers for variola virus DNA from the 2 repositories under specific rules conducting biennial biosafety and biosecurity inspections of the 2 global variola virus repositories developing normative guidance regarding research on variola virus DNA and related matters Emergency preparedness and response coordinating and preparing normative guidance on development and use of medical countermeasures for orthopoxviruses developing preparedness and response strategies, and tools to implement preparedness measures
translations.hypothesis: Research on variola viruses and the development of countermeasures to prepare for the annual WHO Expert Advisory Committee on Variola Virus Research (ACVVR) meetings, to coordinate reviews of research proposals for working with variola viruses through the ACVVR, to manage relationships with the two variola virus repositories (which are also WHO Collaborating Centres), to record, process, and monitor applications from variola virus DNA researchers from the two repositories under specific regulations, to conduct security assessments of the two international wildlife reserves in the variola virus repositories, to develop standard guidelines on variola virus DNA research and related issues, to prepare for emergencies and responses, to coordinate and develop standard guidelines on the development and use of countermeasures against orthopox viruses, to develop preparedness and response strategies, and to develop tools for implementing preparedness measures.

translations.source: kudumisha na kusimamia hifadhi ya kimataifa ya chanjo ya ndui kuratibu maendeleo ya mtandao wa maabara kwa utambuzi na uchunguzi wa virusi vya orthopox kuunda mwongozo wa kawaida na mikakati ya kukabiliana na ndui ya nyani kwa binadamu na virusi vingine vya (ndui) orthopox kusaidia kujenga uwezo na kuendeleza nyenzo za mafunzo kuchangia mipango ya usalama wa afya duniani Kuhifadhi historia ya ndui duniani kusimamia kumbukumbu za Mpango wa Kutokomeza Ugonjwa wa Ndui kwa ushirikiano na Shirika la Kumbukumbu la WHO na Maktaba ya WHO kuongoza mijadala ya sera na matukio ya ukumbusho kuhusu kutokomeza ugonjwa wa ndui kuripoti kila mwaka kwa Bodi ya Utendaji ya WHO na Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni Sekretarieti pia hukusanya na kusimamia ufadhili wa shughuli hizi na kwa matukio ya dharura kama inavyohitajika kama vile mashauriano ya kiufundi na mafunzo.
translations.reference: maintaining and managing the smallpox global vaccine stockpile coordinating development of a laboratory network for orthopoxvirus detection and diagnostics developing normative guidance and response strategies for human monkeypox and other orthopoxviruses supporting capacity-building and developing training materials contributing to global health security initiatives Preserving global smallpox history overseeing the Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with WHO Archives and the WHO Library leading on policy discussions and commemorative events regarding smallpox eradication reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly The Secretariat also mobilizes and manages funding for these activities and for ad hoc events as required such as technical consultations and training.
translations.hypothesis: Maintaining and managing the international vaccine bank for smallpox, coordinating the development of a network of laboratories for the diagnosis and research of orthopoxviruses, developing standard guidelines and strategies for combating smallpox in humans and other orthopoxviruses, supporting the development of capacity and training materials, contributing to global health security initiatives, preserving the history of smallpox, managing the WHO Smallpox Eradication Programme archives in collaboration with the WHO Archives and the WHO Library, leading policy and commemorative events related to smallpox eradication, reporting annually to the WHO Executive Board and the World Health Assembly, and the Secretariat also collects and manages funding for these activities and for emergency situations as needed, such as technical consultations and training.

translations.source: Kung'atwa na nyoka Muhtasari: Kuumwa na nyoka ni ugonjwa unaoweza kuhatarisha maisha unaosababishwa na sumu wakati wa kuumwa na nyoka mwenye sumu. Uchafuzi unaweza pia kusababishwa na kunyunyiziwa sumu machoni na aina fulani za nyoka ambao wana uwezo wa kutema sumu kama njia ya kujikinga. Juhudi duni za hapo awali za kudhibiti kuumwa kwa nyoka zimetoa data iliyogawanyika, isiyo sahihi ya epidemiolojia. Waathiriwa wengi hawahudhurii vituo vya afya au hospitali na badala yake wanategemea matibabu ya kienyeji. Hata hivyo, takwimu zinazopatikana zinaonyesha watu milioni 4.5–5.4 huumwa na nyoka kila mwaka. Kati ya hizi, milioni 1.8-2.7 hupata ugonjwa wa kliniki na 81,000 hadi 138,000 hufa kutokana na matatizo. Vikundi vilivyo katika hatari kubwa ni pamoja na wafanyikazi wa kilimo vijijini, wafugaji, wavuvi, wawindaji, watoto wanaofanya kazi, watu wanaoishi katika nyumba ambazo hazijajengwa vizuri na wale ambao wana fursa ndogo ya kupata elimu na huduma za afya. Maradhi na vifo hutokea mara nyingi zaidi miongoni mwa vijana na watoto hukumbwa na vifo vingi zaidi. Zaidi ya hayo, wanawake hupata vikwazo vilivyoongezeka vya kupata huduma za matibabu katika baadhi ya tamaduni na wanawake wajawazito wako katika hatari kubwa.
translations.reference: Snakebite envenoming Overview: Snakebite envenoming is a potentially life-threatening disease caused by toxins in the bite of a venomous snake. Envenoming can also be caused by having venom sprayed into the eyes by certain species of snakes that have the ability to spit venom as a defence measure. Inadequate past efforts to control snakebite envenoming has produced fragmented, inaccurate epidemiological data. Many victims do not attend health centres or hospitals and instead rely on traditional treatments. However, available data show 4.5–5.4 million people get bitten by snakes annually. Of this, 1.8–2.7 million develop clinical illness and 81 000 to 138 000 die from complications. High-risk groups include rural agricultural workers, herders, fishermen, hunters, working children, people living in poorly constructed houses and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Morbidity and mortality occur most frequently among young people and children suffer higher case fatality. Furthermore, women experience increased barriers to accessing medical care in some cultures and pregnant women are extremely vulnerable.
translations.hypothesis: Snake Bites: Snakebites are a life-threatening illness caused by venom injected during a snake bite. Contamination can also occur when venom enters the eyes of a person through contact with snake saliva or venomous snake fangs. Early efforts to control snakebites have yielded fragmented and unreliable epidemiological data. Many affected individuals do not seek medical attention, instead relying on traditional remedies. However, statistics show that between 4.5 and 5.4 million people are bitten by snakes each year. Of these, 1.8 to 2.7 million develop clinical disease, and 81,000 to 138,000 die as a result. Those at greatest risk include rural farmers, livestock herders, fishermen, hunters, children who work, people living in poorly constructed homes, and those with limited access to education and healthcare. Illness and death are more common among young people and children, with a higher mortality rate. Furthermore, women are at increased risk of complications and death in some cultures, and pregnant women are particularly vulnerable.

translations.source: Mgogoro unaoendelea unaozuia upatikanaji wa matibabu salama na madhubuti ya antivenom katika maeneo mengi, na hasa Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara, ni sababu moja inayochangia uwezekano wa kutafuta msaada kupitia dawa za jadi. Dalili: Kuumwa au kunyunyiziwa sumu kutoka kwa nyoka wenye sumu kunaweza kusababisha aina mbalimbali za dharura za kimatibabu. Ugonjwa wa sumu kutoka kwa aina tofauti za nyoka unaweza kusababisha dalili tofauti, zingine mbaya zaidi kuliko zingine. Hii inafanya utayarishaji wa antivenino sahihi kuwa tatizo linaloendelea. Evenoming inaweza kusababisha kupooza hatari ambayo inaweza kuzuia kupumua, na kufanya matibabu ya haraka muhimu. Watu wanaweza pia kupata matatizo ya kutokwa na damu ambayo yanaweza kusababisha kuvuja damu mbaya au kushindwa kwa figo kusikoweza kurekebishwa. Uharibifu mkubwa wa tishu za ndani pia unaweza kutokea, ambayo inaweza kusababisha ulemavu wa kudumu na hata kukatwa kwa kiungo. Watoto wako katika hatari kubwa ya athari kali kutokana na uzito mdogo wa mwili kuliko watu wazima. Vifo vingi na madhara makubwa kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka vinaweza kuzuilika kabisa kwa kufanya antivenom salama na madhubuti zipatikane na kufikiwa zaidi, hasa katika maeneo yenye hatari kubwa. Dawa za kupambana na nyoka za ubora wa juu ndizo matibabu pekee madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za sumu za kuumwa na nyoka.
translations.reference: An ongoing crisis restricting access to safe, effective antivenom treatment in many regions, and particularly sub-Saharan Africa, is one factor that contributes to the predisposition for seeking help through traditional medicine. Symptoms: Bites or sprayed venom from venomous snakes can cause a range of acute and serious medical emergencies. Envenoming from different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some more serious than others. This makes the preparation of correct antivenoms an ongoing problem. Envenoming can cause severe paralysis that may prevent breathing, making immediate medical attention critical. People may also experience bleeding disorders that can lead to fatal haemorrhages or irreversible kidney failure. Severe local tissue destruction may also occur, which can lead to permanent disability and even limb amputation. Children are at higher risk of severe effects due to lower body mass than adults. Most deaths and serious consequences from snake bites are entirely preventable by making safe and effective antivenoms more widely available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High quality snake antivenoms are the only effective treatment to prevent or reverse most of the venomous effects of snake bites.
translations.hypothesis: The ongoing conflict that prevents access to safe and effective antivenom in many areas, particularly in Southern Sahara, is a major factor contributing to the likelihood of seeking help through traditional remedies. Symptoms: Being bitten or injected with venom from venomous snakes can cause a variety of medical emergencies. Illnesses caused by different types of snakes can cause different symptoms, some of which are more severe than others. This makes the production of the correct antivenom a continuing challenge. Snakebites can cause paralysis, which can lead to respiratory failure, and require immediate medical attention. People can also experience severe bleeding, which can lead to life-threatening blood loss or kidney failure. Significant tissue damage can also occur, which can lead to permanent disability or even amputation. Children are at particular risk of severe complications due to their smaller body size. Many deaths and serious injuries from snakebites could be prevented if safe and effective antivenom were readily available and accessible, particularly in high-risk areas. High-quality antivenom is the only effective treatment for many of the complications caused by snakebites.

translations.source: Matibabu: Dawa za kuua nyoka ni matibabu madhubuti ya kuzuia au kubadilisha athari nyingi za kuumwa na nyoka na zimejumuishwa katika orodha ya WHO ya dawa muhimu. Upatikanaji na ufikiwaji wa dawa hizi, pamoja na kuongeza uelewa juu ya mbinu za kimsingi za kuzuia miongoni mwa jamii na wahudumu wa afya, ni njia bora za kupunguza madhara makubwa na vifo kutokana na kuumwa na nyoka. Baada ya kuumwa na nyoka anayeshukiwa kuwa na sumu, fuata hatua hizi: Ondoka mara moja kutoka eneo ambalo kuumwa kulitokea. Ondoa kitu chochote kinachokaza karibu na sehemu ya mwili iliyoumwa ili kuepusha madhara ikiwa uvimbe utatokea. Mhakikishie mhathiriwa, kwani kuumwa na nyoka wengi wenye sumu hakusababishi kifo cha papo hapo. Zuisha mtu huyo kabisa na umpeleke kwenye kituo cha afya haraka iwezekanavyo Kuweka shinikizo kwenye sehemu za kung'atwa na padi ya shinikizo inayoweza kufaa katika baadhi ya matukio. Epuka njia za jadi za misaada ya kwanza au dawa za mitishamba. Paracetamol inaweza kutolewa kwa maumivu ya ndani (ambayo inaweza kuwa kali).
translations.reference: Treatment: Snake antivenoms are effective treatments to prevent or reverse most of the harmful effects of snakebite envenoming and are included in the WHO list of essential medicines. The availability and accessibility of these antivenoms, along with raising awareness on primary prevention methods among communities and health workers, are the best ways to limit serious consequences and deaths from snakebite envenoming. After a bite by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately move away from the area where the bite occurred. Remove anything tight from around the bitten part of the body to avoid harm if swelling occurs. Reassure the victim, as most venomous snake bites do not cause immediate death. Immobilize the person completely and transport the person to a health facility as soon as possible Applying pressure at the bite site with a pressure pad may be suitable in some cases. Avoid traditional first aid methods or herbal medicines. Paracetamol may be given for local pain (which can be severe).
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: Antivenom is the primary treatment for neutralizing or reversing the effects of most snake bites, and it is included in the WHO list of essential medicines. Access to and administration of this medication, along with increased public awareness of basic prevention measures, are the best ways to reduce the serious consequences and deaths associated with snake bites. If someone has been bitten by a snake suspected of being venomous, follow these steps: Immediately remove the person from the area where the bite occurred. Remove any constricting objects near the bite area to prevent further injury if swelling occurs. Keep the affected limb immobilized with a splint, if possible. Do not apply a tourniquet, as this can be harmful. Transport the person to a hospital or medical facility as quickly as possible. Paracetamol can be given for pain relief (which may be severe).

translations.source: Kutapika kunaweza kutokea, hivyo kumweka mtu upande wake wa kushoto katika nafasi ya kurejeshwa. Fuatilia kwa karibu njia ya hewa na kupumua na uwe tayari kufufua ikiwa ni lazima
translations.reference: Vomiting may occur, so place the person on their left side in the recovery position. Closely monitor airway and breathing and be ready to resuscitate if necessary
translations.hypothesis: Spinal injuries can occur, so it is important to position the person on their left side in a recovery position. Closely monitor their breathing and be prepared to provide rescue breaths if necessary.

translations.source: helminthiases ya udongo Muhtasari: Maambukizi ya ugonjwa wa helminth (STH) ni miongoni mwa maambukizi ya kawaida duniani kote na huathiri jamii maskini zaidi na maskini zaidi. Husambazwa na mayai yaliyopo kwenye kinyesi cha binadamu ambayo nayo huchafua udongo katika maeneo ambayo usafi wa mazingira ni duni. Aina kuu zinazoambukiza watu ni minyoo (Ascaris lumbricoides), mjeledi (Trichuris trichiura) na ndoano (Necator americanus na Ancylostoma duodenale). Minyoo ya matumbo hutoa dalili nyingi ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara, maumivu ya tumbo), malaise ya jumla na udhaifu. Hookworms husababisha kupoteza damu kwa muda mrefu kwa utumbo na kusababisha anemia. Helminths ya udongo hupitishwa kwa mayai ambayo hupitia kwenye kinyesi cha watu walioambukizwa. Minyoo ya watu wazima huishi kwenye utumbo ambapo hutoa maelfu ya mayai kila siku. Katika maeneo ambayo hayana usafi wa kutosha, mayai haya huchafua udongo.: mayai ambayo yameshikanishwa na mboga humezwa wakati mboga haijapikwa kwa uangalifu, kuosha au kuchambuliwa;
translations.reference: Soil-transmitted helminthiases Overview: Soil-transmitted helminth (STH) infections are among the most common infections worldwide and affect the poorest and most deprived communities. They are transmitted by eggs present in human faeces which in turn contaminate soil in areas where sanitation is poor. The main species that infect people are the roundworm (Ascaris lumbricoides), the whipworm (Trichuris trichiura) and the hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Intestinal worms produce a wide range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea, abdominal pain), general malaise and weakness. Hookworms cause chronic intestinal blood loss that result in anaemia. Soil-transmitted helminths are transmitted by eggs that are passed in the faeces of infected people. Adult worms live in the intestine where they produce thousands of eggs each day. In areas that lack adequate sanitation, these eggs contaminate the soil. This can happen in several ways: eggs that are attached to vegetables are ingested when the vegetables are not carefully cooked, washed or peeled;
translations.hypothesis: Soil-transmitted helminth infections: Summary: Infections with soil-transmitted helminth (STH) parasites are among the most common infections worldwide, and they disproportionately affect the poorest populations. These parasites are transmitted through the ingestion of eggs present in human feces, which contaminate the soil in areas with poor sanitation. The main parasites that infect humans are roundworms (Ascaris lumbricoides), whipworms (Trichuris trichiura), and hookworms (Necator americanus and Ancylostoma duodenale). Roundworms can cause a variety of symptoms, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, and general malaise. Hookworms can cause chronic blood loss in the intestines, leading to anemia. Soil-transmitted helminth infections are transmitted through eggs that pass through the feces of infected individuals. Adult worms live in the intestines, where they produce thousands of eggs per day. In areas with poor sanitation, these eggs can contaminate the soil: eggs that are ingested when vegetables are not properly washed or cooked.

translations.source: mayai humezwa kutoka kwa vyanzo vya maji vilivyochafuliwa; mayai humezwa na watoto wanaocheza kwenye udongo uliochafuliwa na kisha kuweka mikono midomoni mwao bila kunawa. Dalili: Ugonjwa unahusiana na idadi ya minyoo waliohifadhiwa. Watu walio na maambukizi ya nguvu ya mwanga (minyoo wachache) kwa kawaida hawateseki kutokana na maambukizi. Maambukizi mazito zaidi yanaweza kusababisha dalili mbalimbali ikiwa ni pamoja na udhihirisho wa matumbo (kuhara na maumivu ya tumbo), utapiamlo, malaise ya jumla na udhaifu, na kuharibika kwa ukuaji na ukuaji wa mwili. Maambukizi ya nguvu ya juu sana yanaweza kusababisha kizuizi cha matumbo ambacho kinapaswa kutibiwa kwa upasuaji. S. stercoralis inaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa ngozi na utumbo na pia inajulikana kuhusishwa na utapiamlo sugu kwa watoto. Katika kesi ya kupungua kwa kinga ya mwenyeji, vimelea vinaweza kusababisha ugonjwa wa hyperinfection/usambazaji ambao ni hatari kila wakati ikiwa haujaponywa mara moja na ipasavyo na mara nyingi husababisha kifo licha ya matibabu. Mnamo mwaka wa 2001, wajumbe katika Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni waliidhinisha kwa kauli moja azimio (WHA54.19) likizitaka nchi zilizo na ugonjwa huo kuanza kukabiliana na minyoo kwa umakini, haswa kichocho na helminths ya udongo. Mkakati wa udhibiti wa maambukizo ya helminth yanayopitishwa kwenye udongo ni kudhibiti maradhi kupitia matibabu ya mara kwa mara ya watu walio hatarini wanaoishi katika maeneo yenye ugonjwa.
translations.reference: eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; eggs are ingested by children who play in the contaminated soil and then put their hands in their mouths without washing them. Symptoms: Morbidity is related to the number of worms harboured. People with infections of light intensity (few worms) usually do not suffer from the infection. Heavier infections can cause a range of symptoms including intestinal manifestations (diarrhoea and abdominal pain), malnutrition, general malaise and weakness, and impaired growth and physical development. Infections of very high intensity can cause intestinal obstruction that should be treated surgically. S. stercoralis may cause dermatological and gastro-intestinal morbidity and is also known to be associated with chronic malnutrition in children. In case of reduced host immunity, the parasite can cause the hyperinfection/dissemination syndrome that is invariably fatal if not promptly and properly cured and is often fatal despite the treatment. In 2001, delegates at the World Health Assembly unanimously endorsed a resolution (WHA54.19) urging endemic countries to start seriously tackling worms, specifically schistosomiasis and soil-transmitted helminths. The strategy for control of soil-transmitted helminth infections is to control morbidity through the periodic treatment of at-risk people living in endemic areas.
translations.hypothesis: Eggs are ingested from contaminated water sources; children playing in soil contaminated with the parasite then put their hands in their mouths without washing. Symptoms: The disease is related to the number of parasites present. People with low levels of light infection (few parasites) typically do not show symptoms. More severe infections can cause a variety of symptoms, including diarrhea, abdominal pain, vomiting, general malaise, and stunted growth. Very high levels of infection can lead to intestinal blockage, which may require surgery. *S. stercoralis* can cause skin and intestinal infections and is also known to be associated with chronic diarrhea in children. In cases of weakened immunity, the parasites can cause hyperinfection/dissemination, which is always life-threatening if not treated promptly and effectively, and often results in death despite treatment. In 2001, the World Health Assembly adopted a resolution (WHA54.19) calling on countries with the disease to begin combating the parasite, particularly soil-transmitted helminths. A strategy for controlling soil-transmitted helminth infections involves treating at-risk populations in endemic areas.

translations.source: Watu walio katika hatari ni: watoto wa shule ya mapema/ chekechea watoto wa umri wa shule wanawake wa umri wa uzazi (ikiwa ni pamoja na wanawake wajawazito katika trimester ya pili na ya tatu na wanawake wanaonyonyesha); watu wazima katika kazi fulani hatarishi kama vile wachumaji chai au wachimbaji. Matibabu Dawa zinazopendekezwa na WHO -albendazole (400 mg) na mebendazole (miligramu 500) - ni nzuri, bei nafuu na ni rahisi kusimamiwa na wafanyikazi wasio wa matibabu (k.m. walimu). Zimepitia majaribio ya kina ya usalama na zimetumika kwa mamilioni ya watu wenye athari chache na ndogo. Albendazole na mebendazole zote mbili zimetolewa kwa wizara za afya za kitaifa kupitia WHO katika nchi zote zilizo na ugonjwa huo kwa matibabu ya watoto wote wa umri wa kwenda shule. Ivermectin kwa udhibiti wa S. stercoralis inatarajiwa kupatikana kwa bei nafuu kuanzia 2021.
translations.reference: People at risk are: preschool children school-age children women of reproductive age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters and breastfeeding women) adults in certain high-risk occupations such as tea-pickers or miners. TreatmentThe WHO recommended medicines –albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – are effective, inexpensive and easy to administer by non-medical personnel (e.g. teachers). They have been through extensive safety testing and have been used in millions of people with few and minor side-effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are donated to national ministries of health through WHO in all endemic countries for the treatment of all children of school age. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to be available at affordable price from 2021.
translations.hypothesis:
The at-risk populations include: early school children/preschool children, school-aged children, women of childbearing age (including pregnant women in the second and third trimesters, and breastfeeding women); and adults in certain occupations such as tea pickers or miners.

Recommended treatments: The WHO recommends albendazole (400 mg) and mebendazole (500 mg) – these are effective, affordable, and easily managed by non-medical personnel (e.g., teachers). They have undergone extensive safety trials and have been used by millions of people with few side effects. Both albendazole and mebendazole are being supplied to national health ministries through the WHO in all affected countries for the treatment of school-aged children. Ivermectin for the control of S. stercoralis is expected to become available at affordable prices starting in 2021.

translations.source: watoto wanazaliwa wakiwa wamekufa Muhtasari: Mtoto anayefariki dunia baada ya wiki 28 za ujauzito, lakini kabla au wakati wa kuzaliwa, huainishwa kuwa mfu. Kuna karibu watoto milioni 2 wanaozaliwa kila mwaka - mmoja kila baada ya sekunde 16. Zaidi ya 40% ya watoto wote wanao kufa kabla ya kuzaliwa hutokea wakati wa leba - hasara ambayo inaweza kuepukwa kwa kuboreshwa kwa huduma bora na yenye heshima wakati wa kujifungua ikiwa ni pamoja na ufuatiliaji wa kawaida na upatikanaji wa huduma ya dharura ya uzazi inapohitajika. Kupata mtoto aliyekufa wakati wa ujauzito au kuzaa ni janga ambalo halijashughulikiwa vya kutosha katika ajenda za kimataifa, sera na programu zinazofadhiliwa. Kuna gharama za kisaikolojia, hasa kwa wanawake na familia zao, kama vile unyogovu wa uzazi, athari za kifedha na athari za kiuchumi, pamoja na unyanyapaa na mwiko. Mnamo mwaka wa 2014, Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha Mpango wa Utekelezaji wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (ENAP), ambao unajumuisha lengo la kimataifa la watoto 12 au pungufu wa trimester ya tatu (marehemu) wanaojifungua kwa jumla ya watoto 1,000 wanaozaliwa katika kila nchi ifikapo 2030. Mnamo 2021, nchi 139 hasa zenye kipato cha juu na za juu za kipato cha kati zilifikia lengo hili, lakini nchi 56 hazitafikia lengo la ENAP kufikia 2030 ikiwa juhudi zaidi hazitafanywa. Ikiwa mwelekeo wa sasa utaendelea, watoto milioni 15.9 watazaliwa wamekufa; karibu nusu ya hizi (milioni 7.7, 48%) zitatokea katika Afrika Kusini mwa Jangwa la Sahara. Sababu: Kukiwa na huduma bora za afya wakati wote wa ujauzito na kujifungua, watoto wengi wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa wanaweza kuzuilika. Sababu kuu za uzazi ni pamoja na matatizo ya ujauzito na uzazi, ujauzito wa muda mrefu, maambukizi ya uzazi kama vile malaria, kaswende na VVU, hali ya uzazi hasa shinikizo la damu, kisukari, na kizuizi cha ukuaji wa fetasi (wakati mtoto ambaye hajazaliwa hawezi kufikia uwezo wake wa ukuaji na hivyo kuwa mdogo kuliko inavyopaswa kuwa). Matatizo ya kuzaliwa nayo yanasababisha chini ya 10% ya watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamefariki walioripotiwa kitaifa katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na idadi kamili katika mazingira ya kipato cha chini haijulikani kwa sababu ya mapungufu katika uchunguzi.
translations.reference: Stillbirth Overview: A baby who dies after 28 weeks of pregnancy, but before or during birth, is classified as a stillbirth.There are nearly 2 million stillbirths every year – one every 16 seconds. Over 40% of all stillbirths occur during labour – a loss that could be avoided with improved quality and respectful care during childbirth including routine monitoring and timely access to emergency obstetric care when required. Experiencing a stillbirth during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy insufficiently addressed in global agendas, policies and funded programmes. There are psychological costs, especially to women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial consequences and economic repercussions, as well as stigma and taboo.In 2014, the World Health Assembly endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of 12 or fewer third trimester (late) stillbirths per 1,000 total births in every country by 2030. In 2021, 139 mainly high-income and upper middle-income countries had met this target, but 56 countries will not each the ENAP target by 2030 if further efforts are not made. If current trends continue, 15.9 million babies will be stillborn; nearly half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: With quality health care throughout pregnancy and childbirth, most stillbirths are preventable. The major causes of stillbirth include pregnancy and childbirth-related complications, prolonged pregnancy, maternal infections such as malaria, syphilis and HIV, maternal conditions especially hypertension, and diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when an unborn baby is unable to achieve its growth potential and therefore smaller than it should be). Congenital abnormalities are responsible for less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and the exact proportion in low-income settings is unknown due to limitations in diagnostics.
translations.hypothesis: Stillborn infants: A summary: A baby who dies before or during birth is considered stillborn. Approximately 2 million babies are stillborn each year – one every 16 seconds. More than 40% of all stillbirths occur during the third trimester – a period that can be reduced by providing better, more respectful care during labor and delivery, including routine monitoring and access to emergency obstetric services when needed. The loss of a baby during pregnancy or childbirth is a tragedy that is not adequately addressed in international agendas, policies, and programs. There are significant psychological costs, especially for women and their families, such as maternal depression, financial hardship, and economic loss, as well as abuse and neglect. In 2014, the World Health Organization endorsed the Every Newborn Action Plan (ENAP), which includes a global target of reducing the number of stillbirths to 4 by 2030, meaning that for every 1,000 live births, no more than 4 are stillborn. By 2021, 139 countries, primarily high-income and upper-middle-income countries, had met this target, but 56 countries were not on track to meet the ENAP target by 2030 without additional efforts. If the current trend continues, an estimated 15.9 million babies will be stillborn. Approximately half of these (7.7 million, 48%) will occur in sub-Saharan Africa. Causes: The main causes of stillbirth include complications during pregnancy and childbirth, prolonged pregnancy, sexually transmitted infections such as malaria, syphilis, and HIV, and maternal health conditions such as high blood pressure, diabetes, and fetal growth restriction (when the baby does not grow adequately, it may be born small for gestational age). These complications can lead to stillbirth, with less than 10% of stillbirths reported nationally in high-income countries and no complete data available in low-income countries due to a lack of surveillance.

translations.source: Mambo yanayohusiana na umri wa mama na kufanya mazoezi ya kuvuta sigara yanaweza pia kuongeza hatari ya ugonjwa wa uzazi na vifo vya kabla ya kuzaliwa. Kujifungua kunaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia ya kupanga uzazi ili kuepuka mimba zisizotarajiwa, afya bora na lishe bora kabla na wakati wa ujauzito, huduma bora na yenye heshima katika ujauzito na uzazi ikijumuisha wafanyakazi wa afya wenye ujuzi wa kutosha wakiwemo wakunga. Matibabu ya kaswende kwa wajauzito inaweza kuzuia watoto wanaokadiriwa kufikia 200,000 wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa, wakati ufuatiliaji wa mapigo ya moyo wa fetasi na ufuatiliaji wa leba na uingiliaji wa haraka inapohitajika ni muhimu kwa kuzuia uzazi 832,000 ndani ya uzazi na kupunguza vifo vya watoto wachanga. Watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa huhusishwa sana na viashirio vya kijamii na kiuchumi vya kiafya. Kinga na utunzaji sikivu unahitaji kuunganishwa katika mwendelezo wa huduma ya afya ya uzazi, na zaidi. Hii ni pamoja na utunzaji wa heshima na msaada katika tukio la kifo. Kusikiliza uzoefu na sauti za wanawake na jumuiya zao ni muhimu ili kusaidia kushughulikia masuala ya unyanyapaa unaohusishwa na kifo cha kabla. Jibu la WHO: Mengi zaidi yanahitajika kufanywa ili kujumuisha uzuiaji wa mimba mfu ndani ya ajenda za kimataifa na kitaifa za huduma ya afya ya hali ya juu kwa wanawake, vijana na watoto. Kupitia Mkakati wa Kimataifa wa Afya ya Wanawake, Watoto na Vijana 2016–2030 na kutekeleza mapendekezo ya Mpango Kazi wa Kila Mtoto Wachanga (2014), WHO inajitahidi kukomesha uzazi unaoweza kuzuilika, kama sehemu ya juhudi za kuboresha afya ya uzazi, watoto wachanga, watoto na vijana. Watoto wengi waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa hawapati cheti cha kuzaliwa na hawajasajiliwa.
translations.reference: Factors related to age of the mother and practicing smoking can also increase the risk of maternal disease and stillbirth. Stillbirths can be prevented through family planning to avoid unwanted pregnancies, good health and nutrition prior to and during pregnancy, quality and respectful antenatal and childbirth care including adequate skilled health personnel including midwives. Syphilis treatment in pregnancy could also prevent an estimated 200 000 stillbirths, while fetal heart rate monitoring and labour surveillance and prompt interventions when needed are crucial for preventing 832 000 intrapartum stillbirths and reducing neonatal deaths. Stillbirths are strongly linked to adverse social and economic determinants of health. Prevention and responsive care need to be integrated across the continuum of maternal health care, and beyond. This includes respectful and supportive care in the event of a death. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is essential to help address issues of stigma associated with stillbirth. WHO response: More needs to be done to integrate stillbirth prevention within global and national agendas for high quality health care for women, adolescents, and babies. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Adolescents Health 2016–2030 and implementing the recommendations of the Every Newborn Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to end preventable stillbirths, as part of efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health. Most stillbirths do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.
translations.hypothesis: Factors related to a mother's age and smoking habits can also increase the risk of pregnancy complications and stillbirths. Family planning can help prevent unwanted pregnancies, leading to better health and nutrition for both the mother and the baby, as well as improved prenatal and postnatal care, including access to skilled birth attendants. Treatment for pre-eclampsia in pregnant women can prevent an estimated 200,000 babies from being stillborn, while fetal monitoring and prompt intervention when necessary are crucial in preventing 832,000 pregnancies from resulting in stillbirths. Stillbirths are often associated with social and economic factors related to health. Comprehensive and sensitive care is essential in continuing reproductive health services, and beyond. This includes respectful care and support in the event of a stillbirth. Listening to the experiences and voices of women and their communities is important in addressing the issues surrounding stillbirth. WHO's response: More needs to be done to integrate prevention of stillbirths into national and international health agendas for women, young people, and children. Through the Global Strategy for Women's, Children's and Youth Health 2016–2030 and the implementation of the Every Child Counts Action Plan (2014), WHO is working to eliminate preventable stillbirths as part of its efforts to improve maternal, newborn, child, and adolescent health. Many stillborn babies do not receive a birth certificate and are not registered.

translations.source: Kuboresha mifumo ya kuripoti kuzaliwa na vifo vya watoto wachanga ni suala la haki za binadamu na sharti la kupunguza watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa. Aidha, kuna haja ya kuimarisha mifumo ya kawaida ya taarifa za afya ili kunasa na kuripoti kwa usahihi watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa ajili ya ufuatiliaji na kuboresha ubora wa huduma. WHO hutoa zana kusaidia nchi kuboresha data zao kuhusu watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa na vifo vya watoto wachanga. WHO pia ina mwongozo wa kusaidia nchi kukagua na kuchunguza vifo vya watu binafsi ili ziweze kupendekeza na kutekeleza masuluhisho ya kuzuia vile vile katika siku zijazo na mfumo wa kawaida wa uainishaji wa vifo wakati wa kujifungua (ICD-PM). WHO na UNICEF wanaunga mkono wito wa kuchukua hatua za pamoja kukomesha watoto wanaozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa kwa njia zifuatazo: kuongeza uelewa na kupunguza unyanyapaa kusaidia wanawake na familia zilizofiwa kuimarisha mifumo ya afya ili kutoa huduma bora kutaifisha na kuainisha malengo ya uzazi kuboresha upimaji wa watoto waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa ili kuongeza ushahidi na maarifa.
translations.reference: Improving systems for reporting births and neonatal deaths is a matter of human rights and a prerequisite for reducing stillbirths. In addition, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately capture and report stillbirths for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on both stillbirths and neonatal deaths. WHO also has guidance to help countries review and investigate individual deaths so they can recommend and implement solutions to prevent similar ones in the future and a standard classification system for perinatal deaths (ICD-PM). WHO and UNICEF are supporting a call for collective action to end preventable stillbirths by: increasing awareness and reducing stigma supporting bereaved women and families strengthening health systems to provide high-quality care nationalizing and localizing stillbirth targets improving measurement of stillbirths to enhance evidence and knowledge.
translations.hypothesis: Improving systems for reporting infant and child mortality is a matter of human rights and a crucial step in reducing the number of children who die before their fifth birthday. Furthermore, there is a need to strengthen routine health information systems to accurately collect and report data on infant and child mortality for monitoring and improving the quality of care. WHO provides tools to help countries improve their data on infant and child mortality. WHO also offers guidance to help countries investigate and analyze individual deaths to recommend and implement preventive measures in the future, and to use the International Classification of Diseases, Pregnancy and Childbirth (ICD-PM) for consistent classification of deaths during childbirth. WHO and UNICEF support the call for coordinated action to eliminate preventable infant and child deaths through the following measures: increasing awareness and reducing violence; supporting survivors and their families; strengthening health systems to provide better care; addressing and eliminating gender-based violence; and improving access to reproductive health services.

translations.source: Bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni Muhtasari: Hadi watu bilioni mbili duniani kote hawana uwezo wa kufikia dawa zinazohitajika, chanjo, vifaa vya matibabu ikiwa ni pamoja na uchunguzi wa ndani na bidhaa nyingine za afya, jambo ambalo huzua ombwe ambalo mara nyingi hujazwa na bidhaa duni na ghushi. Tatizo hili linaongezeka huku misururu ya ugavi duniani ikizidi kuwa changamano, kumaanisha kuwa bidhaa zinazotengenezwa katika nchi moja zinaweza kuunganishwa katika nchi ya pili na kusambazwa katika mipaka ili kupelekwa sokoni au kuuzwa kwa watumiaji katika nchi ya tatu. Ukuaji wa biashara ya mtandaoni pia huchangia mwelekeo huu kwa kurahisisha ununuzi wa dawa mtandaoni, mara nyingi kutoka kwa vyanzo visivyoidhinishwa. WHO imebainisha suala hili kuwa moja ya changamoto za dharura za kiafya kwa muongo ujao ikizingatiwa kuwa zaidi ya dawa moja kati ya kumi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati inakadiriwa kuwa duni au kughushi. Hakuna nchi ambayo haijaguswa kutokana na suala hili, na WHO imepokea ripoti za dawa za kimatibabu zisizo na viwango au duni, chanjo na uchunguzi wa kimatibabu kutoka maeneo yote ya dunia. Dawa zote mbili za kawaida na za wabunifu zinaweza kughushiwa, kuanzia bidhaa za bei ghali sana za saratani hadi bidhaa za bei nafuu kwa matibabu ya maumivu. Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zimekubaliana kuhusu mkakati wa kina wa kimataifa unaolenga kuzuia, kugundua na kukabiliana na hali hiyo ili kufikia kiwango cha juu cha upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora za matibabu, salama, makini na bora. Athari: Bidhaa za kimatibabu zisizo na kiwango na zisizo za kweli huleta matatizo mengi kwa mtu binafsi anayetumia dawa na kwa familia, jamii kubwa, mfumo wa huduma za afya na mnyororo wa usambazaji. Wakati dawa hazifanyi kazi inavyopaswa, zinaweza kushindwa kutibu au kuzuia magonjwa, kuchangia upinzani wa antimicrobial, kupoteza rasilimali za thamani na kusababisha kupoteza imani katika madawa na kutoamini watoa huduma za afya.
translations.reference: Substandard and falsified medical products Overview: Up to two billion people around the world lack access to necessary medicines, vaccines, medical devices including in vitro diagnostics, and other health products, which creates a vacuum that is too often filled by substandard and falsified products. This problem is growing as global supply chains become more complex, meaning products manufactured in one country may be packaged in a second country and distributed across borders to be marketed or sold to consumers in a third. The growth of e-commerce also contributes to this trend by making it easier to purchase medicines online, often from unauthorized sources. WHO has identified this issue one of the urgent health challenges for the next decade given that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries are estimated to be substandard or falsified. No country remains untouched from this issue, and WHO has received reports of substandard or falsified medical medicines, vaccines and in vitro diagnostics from all regions of the world. Both generic and innovator medicines can be falsified, ranging from very expensive products for cancer to very inexpensive products for treatment of pain. WHO Member States have agreed on a comprehensive global strategy focused on prevention, detection and response to move towards achieving increased access to quality, safe, effective and quality medical products. Impact: Substandard and falsified medical products create many problems for both the individual taking the medicine and for families, the larger society, health care system and supply chain. When medicines do not work the way they should, they can fail to treat or prevent disease, contribute to antimicrobial resistance, waste precious resources and lead to a loss of confidence in medicines and distrust of healthcare providers.
translations.hypothesis: Counterfeit and substandard medicines: An estimated two billion people worldwide lack access to essential medicines, vaccines, and medical devices, including diagnostic tests and other health products. This creates a market for counterfeit and substandard products, which are often sold online or in unregulated pharmacies. The problem is exacerbated by the increasing complexity of global supply chains, which means that products manufactured in one country can be diverted to another and sold on the black market. The growth of e-commerce has also contributed to the problem, as it has made it easier to purchase counterfeit medicines online, often from unverified sources. The World Health Organization (WHO) has identified this issue as one of the major health emergencies of the 21st century, noting that more than one in ten medicines in low- and middle-income countries is counterfeit or substandard. No country has been immune to this problem, and the WHO has received reports of counterfeit and substandard medicines from all over the world. Both common and innovative medicines can be counterfeited, from expensive cancer drugs to affordable pain relievers. WHO member states have agreed on a comprehensive international strategy to prevent, detect, and respond to this problem, in order to ensure that people have access to safe, effective, and affordable medicines.

translations.source: Hii inaweza kuwa na athari mbaya ya kijamii na kiuchumi, kuchangia hasara inayohusiana na tija na kuongeza gharama kwa mfumo wa afya wa mtu binafsi na wa kitaifa. Wana uwezekano mkubwa wa kufikia wagonjwa katika hali ambapo kuna vikwazo vya upatikanaji wa bidhaa bora na salama za matibabu, utawala duni na uwezo dhaifu wa kiufundi. Ingawa suala hili linaathiri nchi zote, nchi ambako kuna mifumo dhaifu ya udhibiti au haipo hubeba mzigo mkubwa zaidi wa bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na duni. Katika ulimwengu unaozidi kuwa wa utandawazi, hakuna nchi moja yenye rasilimali na uwezo wa kutosha wa kushughulikia suala hili kwa kujitenga. Kupitia mifumo na michakato ya kimataifa kama vile Mfumo wa Ufuatiliaji na Ufuatiliaji wa Kimataifa wa WHO na Utaratibu wa Nchi Wanachama wa WHO, Nchi Wanachama zinafanya kazi pamoja ili kuzuia, kugundua na kujibu bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na potofu.
translations.reference: This can have a negative socioeconomic impact, contributing to related loss in productivity and adding expenses to the individual and national health system. They are most likely to reach patients in situations where there is constrained access to quality and safe medical products, poor governance and weak technical capacity. Although this issue impacts all countries, countries where there are weak or non-existent regulatory systems bear the greatest burden of substandard and falsified medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no one country has sufficient resources and capacity to deal with this issue in isolation. Through global systems and processes such as the WHO Global Surveillance and Monitoring System and WHO Member State Mechanism, Member States are working together to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis: This can have significant social and economic consequences, leading to losses in productivity and increasing the burden on both individual and national healthcare systems. There is a high likelihood that substandard and unsafe medical products will reach patients in situations where access to quality healthcare is already limited, due to poor regulation and weak technical capacity. While this issue affects all countries, those with weak regulatory systems or no systems at all bear the greatest burden of substandard and unsafe medical products. In an increasingly globalized world, no single country has the resources and capacity to address this issue on its own. Through international frameworks and mechanisms such as the WHO's International Monitoring Programme and the WHO's Framework Convention on International Trade in Goods, countries are working together to prevent, detect, and respond to substandard and unsafe medical products.

translations.source: Kuzuia kujiua Muhtasari: Zaidi ya watu 700,000 hufa kwa kujiua kila mwaka. Kwa kila kujiua, kunakadiriwa kuwa zaidi ya majaribio 20 ya kujiua. Kujiua kunaweza kutokea katika hatua yoyote ya maisha na katika maeneo yote ya dunia. Mnamo mwaka wa 2019, kujiua ilikuwa sababu ya nne ya vifo kati ya vijana wenye umri wa miaka 15-29 ulimwenguni, na 77% ya watu wote waliojiua walitokea katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Takriban 20% ya watu wanaojiua duniani kote walitokana na kujitia sumu. Ingawa uhusiano kati ya kujiua na hali ya afya ya akili (haswa, huzuni na matatizo ya matumizi ya pombe) na jaribio la awali la kujiua limethibitishwa vyema katika nchi za mapato ya juu, kujiua kwa watu wengi hutokea bila kutarajia wakati wa shida. Sababu zaidi za hatari ya kujiua ni pamoja na uzoefu wa kupoteza, upweke, ubaguzi, kuvunjika kwa uhusiano, matatizo ya kifedha, maumivu ya kudumu na ugonjwa, vurugu, unyanyasaji na migogoro au dharura nyingine za kibinadamu. Unyanyapaa kwenye afya ya akili na kujiua inamaanisha watu wengi wanaofikiria juu ya kujiua hawatafuti msaada. Kujiua na majaribio ya kujiua kuna athari mbaya ambayo huathiri familia, marafiki, wafanyakazi wenza, jamii na jamii.
translations.reference: Suicide prevention Overview: More than 700 000 people die by suicide every year. For each suicide, there are an estimated more than 20 suicide attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in all regions of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among 15–29-year-olds globally, and 77% of all suicides happened in low- and middle-income countries. Almost 20% of global suicides were due to pesticide self-poisoning. While the link between suicide and mental health conditions (in particular, depression and alcohol use disorders) and a previous suicide attempt is well established in high-income countries, many suicides happen impulsively in moments of crisis. Further risk factors for suicide include experience of loss, loneliness, discrimination, a relationship break-up, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and conflict or other humanitarian emergencies. Stigma around mental health conditions and suicide means many people thinking about suicide do not seek help. Suicides and suicide attempts have a ripple effect that impacts on families, friends, colleagues, communities and societies.
translations.hypothesis: Suicide: More than 700,000 people die by suicide each year. For every suicide, it is estimated that there are several attempts. Suicide can occur at any stage of life and in any part of the world. In 2019, suicide was the fourth leading cause of death among young people aged 15-29 globally, and 77% of all suicides occurred in low- and middle-income countries. Approximately 20% of people who die by suicide worldwide use firearms. While the link between suicide and mental health (particularly depression and substance abuse) and previous suicide attempts has been well established in high-income countries, many suicides occur unexpectedly during times of crisis. The most common risk factors for suicide include loss, loneliness, discrimination, relationship breakdown, financial problems, chronic pain and illness, violence, abuse, and other human tragedies. Mental health and suicide are often linked, with many people who are considering suicide not seeking help. Suicide and suicide attempts have a devastating impact on families, friends, colleagues, communities, and society as a whole.

translations.source: Kujiua: ukweli na takwimu duniani kote Kujiua ulimwenguni kote katika ripoti ya 2019 LIVE LIFE: mwongozo wa utekelezaji wa kuzuia kujiua katika nchi Kinga: Mengi yanaweza kufanywa katika ngazi ya mtu binafsi, jamii na kitaifa ili kuzuia majaribio ya kujiua na kujiua. Mtazamo wa LIVE LIFE wa WHO unapendekeza hatua nne muhimu, zenye msingi wa ushahidi ambazo zinapaswa kujumuishwa katika kila mwitikio wa kitaifa wa kujiua: kupunguza upatikanaji wa njia za kujiua kuingiliana na vyombo vya habari kwa kuripoti habari za kujiua kukuza stadi za maisha ya kijamii na kimhemko kwa vijana kutambua mapema, kutathmini, kusimamia na kufuatilia mtu yeyote ambaye ameathiriwa na tabia za kujiua. Nguzo za msingi za afua hizo ni uchambuzi wa hali, ushirikiano wa sekta mbalimbali, kuongeza uelewa, kujenga uwezo, ufadhili, ufuatiliaji, ufuatiliaji na tathmini.
translations.reference: Suicide: facts and figures globally Suicide worldwide in 2019 report LIVE LIFE: an implementation guide for suicide prevention in countries Prevention: Much can be done at individual, community and national levels to prevent suicide and suicide attempts. WHO’s LIVE LIFE approach recommends four effective, evidence-based key interventions which should be included in every national response to suicide: limit access to the means of suicide interact with the media for responsible reporting of suicide foster socio-emotional life skills in adolescents early identify, assess, manage and follow up anyone who is affected by suicidal behaviours. The foundational pillars of the interventions are situation analysis, multisectoral collaboration, awareness raising, capacity building, financing, surveillance, monitoring and evaluation.
translations.hypothesis:
Suicide: The Facts and Figures Worldwide

Suicide Worldwide: According to the 2019 WHO report, suicide is a leading cause of death globally. There are many things that can be done at the individual, community, and national levels to prevent suicide attempts and deaths. The WHO's LIVE LIFE approach recommends four key steps, based on evidence, that should be included in every national suicide prevention strategy: reducing access to methods of suicide, engaging the media in reporting on suicide, promoting social and emotional skills among young people, and identifying, assessing, managing, and monitoring individuals at risk of suicide. The key pillars of this approach are: situational analysis, multi-sectoral collaboration, increased awareness, capacity building, funding, monitoring, and evaluation.

translations.source: Juhudi za kuzuia kujiua zinahitaji uratibu na ushirikiano kati ya sekta nyingi, ikiwa ni pamoja na afya, elimu, kazi, kilimo, biashara, haki, sheria, ulinzi, siasa na vyombo vya habari. Uzuiaji wa kujiua mara nyingi haushughulikiwi ipasavyo kwa sababu ya unyanyapaa na ukosefu wa ufahamu kwamba kujiua ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma. Jamii pia ina jukumu muhimu katika kuzuia kujiua. Zana ya ushiriki wa jumuiya ya WHO huongoza jamii katika kutambua vipaumbele na kutekeleza shughuli kulingana na mazingira ya ndani. Jibu la WHO: Uharaka wa kuchukua hatua ili kuzuia kujiua umetambuliwa na kupewa kipaumbele katika viwango vya juu zaidi. Kupungua kwa viwango vya kujiua katika nchi ni kiashirio katika Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu ya Umoja wa Mataifa (kiashiria pekee cha afya ya akili), Mpango wa Kazi Mkuu wa WHO na Mpango Kamili wa Utekelezaji wa Afya ya Akili wa 2013–2030. Ripoti ya kwanza ya WHO ya kujiua duniani, Kuzuia kujiua: umuhimu wa kimataifa, iliyochapishwa katika 2014, ililenga kuongeza ufahamu wa umuhimu wa afya ya umma wa majaribio ya kujiua na kujiua na kufanya kuzuia kujiua kuwa kipaumbele cha afya ya umma. Serikali zinahimizwa kutekeleza kifurushi cha WHO cha LIVE LIFE cha afua muhimu zinazofaa na zenye msingi wa ushahidi kwa ajili ya kuzuia kujitoa mhanga, na kuendeleza au kuimarisha mikakati ya kitaifa ya kuzuia kujiua katika mkabala wa afya ya umma wa sekta mbalimbali. Uongozi wa kitaifa na uratibu juu ya kuzuia kujiua unasaidiwa zaidi na uchapishaji wa mikakati ya Kitaifa ya kuzuia kujiua: maendeleo, mifano na viashiria. Kwa kutambua kwamba data na takwimu ni muhimu kwa utetezi na kufanya maamuzi, WHO ilichapisha kujiua duniani kote mwaka wa 2019: Makadirio ya Afya Ulimwenguni.
translations.reference: Suicide prevention efforts require coordination and collaboration among multiple sectors, including health, education, labour, agriculture, business, justice, law, defence, politics and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed because of stigma and lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health problem. Communities also play a crucial role in preventing suicide. The WHO community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying priorities and implementing activities according to local contexts. WHO response: The urgency to act to prevent suicides has been recognized and prioritized at the highest levels. The reduction of suicide rates in countries is an indicator in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (the only indicator for mental health), WHO’s General Programme of Work and WHO’s Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO world suicide report, Preventing suicide: a global imperative, published in 2014, aimed to increase awareness of the public health significance of suicide and suicide attempts and make suicide prevention a public health priority. Governments are encouraged to implement WHO’s LIVE LIFE package of effective and evidence-based key interventions for suicide prevention, and to develop or strengthen comprehensive national suicide prevention strategies in a multisectoral public health approach. National leadership and coordination on suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of National suicide prevention strategies: progress, examples and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are necessary for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates.
translations.hypothesis: Efforts to prevent suicide require coordination and collaboration across multiple sectors, including health, education, employment, agriculture, business, human rights, law, security, politics, and the media. Suicide prevention is often not adequately addressed due to stigma and a lack of awareness that suicide is a major public health issue. Communities also play a crucial role in suicide prevention. The WHO's community engagement toolkit guides communities in identifying risk factors and implementing activities based on local contexts. The WHO's response to suicide has been prioritized, with a focus on taking swift action to prevent it. A decline in suicide rates in countries is an indicator of progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals (a key indicator of mental health), the WHO's Global Strategy and the Comprehensive Mental Health Action Plan 2013–2030. The first WHO report on suicide worldwide, Suicide Prevention: A Global Imperative, published in 2014, aimed to raise awareness of the importance of public health efforts to prevent suicide and to make suicide prevention a priority in public health. Governments are encouraged to implement the WHO's LIVE LIFE package of evidence-based and cost-effective interventions to prevent suicide and to develop or strengthen national suicide prevention strategies within a multi-sectoral framework for public health. National leadership and coordination in suicide prevention are further supported by the publication of national suicide prevention strategies: guidelines, examples, and indicators. Recognizing that data and statistics are essential for advocacy and decision-making, WHO published Suicide Statistics Worldwide in 2019: Global Health Estimates.

translations.source: Maono ya WHO ni ulimwengu ambapo kifo kwa kujiua si sababu kuu ya vifo tena na ambapo watu wanaojaribu au wanaofikiria kujiua hujisikia vizuri kutafuta msaada ambao hutolewa kwa uwezo na heshima kwa wote.
translations.reference: WHO’s vision is a world where death by suicide is no longer a leading cause of death and where people who are attempting or thinking about suicide feel comfortable seeking help which is provided capably and respectfully to all.
translations.hypothesis: The WHO's vision is a world where suicide is no longer a leading cause of death, and where individuals who are struggling with suicidal thoughts or attempts feel safe and supported in seeking help, with respect and dignity for all.

translations.source: Maendeleo endelevu Muhtasari: Maendeleo endelevu ni neno pana la kuelezea sera, miradi na uwekezaji ambao hutoa manufaa leo bila kutoa dhabihu afya ya mazingira, kijamii na kibinafsi katika siku zijazo. Sera hizi mara nyingi hufafanuliwa kuwa za kijani kwa sababu zinalenga kupunguza athari za maendeleo kwa mazingira. Hata hivyo, manufaa ya maendeleo endelevu yanaonekana pia katika sehemu mbalimbali za afya na ustawi wa binadamu, ikiwa ni pamoja na kupunguzwa kwa uchafuzi- na magonjwa yanayohusiana na mazingira, matokeo bora ya afya na kupungua kwa dhiki. Kadiri matishio ya mabadiliko ya tabianchi yanavyozidi kuwa muhimu, mikakati ya kushinda, kuboresha afya na kuokoa gharama inatoa faida mbalimbali kwa wadau mbalimbali. Kwa mfano, shughuli za afya za kijani zinaweza kuzalisha manufaa ya afya ya mgonjwa na mfanyakazi huku pia kuokoa nishati, kupunguza hatari za hali ya hewa na kuokoa gharama za muda mrefu. Shirika la Afya Ulimwenguni limejitolea kutafuta maendeleo endelevu katika kazi zake zote kusaidia kuwalinda watu wa kesho dhidi ya hatari za kiafya zinazoongezeka leo. Athari: Mikakati mingi ya maendeleo endelevu inaweza kutoa mafanikio makubwa kwa afya, hali ya hewa na mazingira, na manufaa yanaweza kuonekana mara moja. Kwa mfano, malengo mengi ya afya na maendeleo yanaweza kufikiwa kwa wakati mmoja kwa kukabiliana na uchafuzi wa hewa, ambao unawajibika kwa vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka. Uzalishaji chafuzi unajumuisha vichafuzi vikali vya hali ya hewa vya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—hasa kaboni nyeusi, ozoni na methane—ambazo ni hatari sana kwa afya lakini zina maisha mafupi tu ya angahewa.
translations.reference: Sustainable development Overview: Sustainable development is a broad term to describe policies, projects and investments that provide benefits today without sacrificing environmental, social and personal health in the future. These policies are often described as green because they focus on limiting the impact of development on the environment. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also felt across a wide cross section of human health and well-being, including reductions in pollution- and environment-related disease, improved health outcomes and decreased stress. As the threats of climate change become increasingly important, win-win strategies for mitigation, health improvement and cost savings offer a range of advantages for various stakeholders. For example, greener health care operations can generate patient and worker health benefits while also saving energy, mitigating climate risks and creating long-term cost savings. The World Health Organization is committed to pursuing sustainable development in all its work to help protect the people of tomorrow from the health growing health risks of today. Impact: Many sustainable development strategies can offer significant wins for health, climate and the environment, and the benefits can be seen almost immediately. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by tackling air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths annually. Polluting emissions include powerful short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone and methane—which are very harmful to health but have only brief atmospheric lifetimes.
translations.hypothesis: Sustainable Development Summary: Sustainable development is a broad term that encompasses policies, projects, and investments that generate benefits today without compromising the health of the environment, society, and individuals in the future. These policies are often referred to as "green" because they aim to reduce the environmental impact of development. However, the benefits of sustainable development are also evident in various aspects of human health and well-being, including reduced exposure to environmental pollutants and related diseases, improved health outcomes, and reduced poverty. As the threats of climate change become increasingly apparent, strategies for mitigating, adapting to, and preventing health risks offer a range of benefits to various stakeholders. For example, green health initiatives can improve the health of patients and workers while also saving energy, reducing air pollution, and lowering long-term costs. The World Health Organization is committed to promoting sustainable development in all its activities to help protect people from the growing health risks of today. Benefits: Many sustainable development strategies can have significant benefits for health, the environment, and the economy, and these benefits can be realized quickly. For example, many health and development goals can be achieved simultaneously by addressing air pollution, which is responsible for an estimated 7 million premature deaths each year. Air pollution includes short-lived air pollutants (SLAPs)—particularly black carbon, ozone, and methane—which are highly detrimental to health but have relatively short atmospheric lifetimes.

translations.source: Sera za usafiri na uwekezaji unaopendelea usafiri safi wa umma, pamoja na kutembea na kuendesha baiskeli, zinaweza kuwa na athari kubwa katika ubora wa hewa. Hii inaweza kupunguza hatari nyingi za kiafya kwa wakati mmoja, pamoja na kiharusi, mshtuko wa moyo, ugonjwa wa mapafu na saratani kadhaa. Wanaweza pia kupunguza makadirio ya vifo milioni 1.25 kila mwaka kutokana na majeraha ya barabarani, huku pia wakiboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za afya, hasa katika maeneo yenye watu wengi. Sera za maendeleo endelevu za makazi zinaweza kupunguza zaidi uzalishaji wa hewa chafu kupitia mambo ya kuzingatia kama vile eneo la majengo na matumizi ya ardhi, uchaguzi wa vifaa vya ujenzi, vipengele vya muundo na uingizaji hewa na nishati. Jibu la WHO: Kazi ya WHO inaongozwa na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu, hasa Lengo la 3: "Hakikisha maisha yenye afya na kukuza ustawi kwa wote katika umri wote." Malengo yanaarifu miradi na sera kote katika Shirika, ikijumuisha miongozo ya makazi, maendeleo ya mijini, mifumo ya usafirishaji na utoaji wa huduma za afya. Kwa mfano, miongozo ya Makazi na afya ya WHO, iliyochapishwa kwa mara ya kwanza mwaka wa 2018, inaangazia ongezeko la athari za hali ya makazi kwa afya ya binadamu kwa kuzingatia ukuaji wa miji, hali ya hewa na mabadiliko ya idadi ya watu. Mwongozo huo unazipa Nchi Wanachama, washirika na umma mapendekezo yenye msingi wa ushahidi yanayohusiana na uhaba wa nafasi ya kuishi (msongamano), halijoto ya chini na ya juu ndani ya nyumba, hatari za majeraha nyumbani, na upatikanaji wa nyumba kwa watu walio na matatizo ya utendaji. Mpango wa Afya Mijini (UHI) pia unaangazia njia za miji kuwezesha afya njema kwa kuchochea hatua madhubuti dhidi ya uchafuzi wa hewa mijini na vichafuzi vya hali ya hewa ya muda mfupi (SLCPs)—kuokoa maisha kwa kuunganisha afya, mazingira na maendeleo endelevu. WHO pia inashirikiana na Muungano wa Hali ya Hewa na Safi (CCAC) kwenye kampeni ya Breathe Life, ambayo inalenga kupunguza uchafuzi wa hewa na kupunguza vifo vya mapema milioni 7 kila mwaka ambavyo husababisha.
translations.reference: Transport policies and investments that favour clean public transport, along with walking and cycling, can have a dramatic impact on air quality. This can reduce multiple health risks at the same time, including stroke, heart attack, lung disease and some cancers. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million deaths annually from traffic injury, while also improving access to health services, particularly in densely populated areas. Sustainable development housing policies can further reduce emissions through considerations such as building siting and land use, choices of construction materials, design features and ventilation and energy. WHO response: WHO’s work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: “Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages.” The Goals inform projects and policies across the Organization, including guidelines on housing, urban development, transportation systems and health care delivery. For example, the WHO Housing and health guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the increasing impact of housing conditions on human health in light of urban growth, climate and demographic changes. The guidelines provide Member States, partners and the public with evidence-based recommendations relevant to inadequate living space (crowding), low and high indoor temperatures, injury hazards in the home, and accessibility of housing for people with functional impairments. The Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on ways for cities to enable good health by catalysing effective action on urban air pollution and short-lived climate pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by linking health, environment and sustainable development. WHO also collaborates with the Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to cut air pollution and reduce the 7 million premature deaths annually that it causes.
translations.hypothesis: Policies that promote clean public transportation, as well as walking and cycling, can have a significant impact on air quality. This can reduce the risk of many health problems, including respiratory illnesses, heart disease, and certain types of cancer. They can also reduce the estimated 1.25 million annual deaths caused by traffic accidents, while also improving access to healthcare, especially in densely populated areas. Sustainable urban development policies can further reduce air pollution through measures such as building density and land use, building material selection, design features, and ventilation and energy efficiency. WHO's Response: The WHO's work is guided by the Sustainable Development Goals, particularly Goal 3: "Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages." These goals inform projects and policies throughout the organization, including urban planning, urban development, transportation systems, and health service delivery. For example, the WHO's Housing, Health, and Environment (HHE) guidelines, first published in 2018, highlight the growing impact of housing conditions on human health, taking into account urbanization, climate change, and population growth. The guidelines provide Member States, partners, and the public with evidence-based recommendations related to housing density, indoor temperature and humidity, home safety, and access to adequate housing. The WHO's Urban Health Initiative (UHI) also focuses on how cities can promote healthy living by taking concrete steps to reduce air pollution and short-lived air pollutants (SLCPs)—saving lives by integrating health, environment, and sustainable development. The WHO is also working with the Clean Air Coalition (CAC) on the Breathe Life campaign, which aims to reduce air pollution and prevent 7 million premature deaths each year.

translations.source: Kaswende Muhtasari: Kaswende ni ugonjwa wa zinaa unaosababishwa na bakteria (STI) unaosababishwa na Treponema pallidum ambayo husababisha magonjwa na vifo vingi, na inatibika. Kaswende huambukizwa kwa njia ya kujamiiana na vidonda vya kuambukiza, kwa kuongezewa damu au kutoka kwa mwanamke mjamzito hadi kwa kijusi chake. Uambukizaji wa kaswende kutoka kwa mama kwenda kwa mtoto huwa mbaya kwa kijusi katika hali ambazo hazikugunduliwa au kutibiwa vya kutosha mapema katika ujauzito. Kaswende ya kina mama, isipotibiwa, kutibiwa kuchelewa au kutotibiwa na penicillin, husababisha matokeo mabaya ya uzazi (ABOs) yanayokadiriwa katika 50-80% ya kesi, kutegemeana na hatua ya kaswende. ABO hizi mara nyingi huwa kali na hujumuisha kuzaliwa mfu (mara nyingi), kifo cha watoto wachanga, kuzaliwa kabla ya wakati, uzito wa chini, na watoto wachanga waliozaliwa kuzaliwa. Kaswende inaweza kuzuiwa kwa njia salama za ngono ikijumuisha matumizi sahihi na ya mara kwa mara ya kondomu. Watu wengi wenye kaswende hawana dalili zozote au wana dalili ndogo tu ambazo hazitambuliki. Kutambua maambukizi kupitia vipimo vya maabara na matibabu ya kesi chanya kutazuia maambukizi zaidi na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito ikiwa ni pamoja na kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Uchunguzi unaweza kufanywa kwa kutumia vipimo vya haraka ambavyo vinaweza kutoa matokeo kwa chini ya dakika 20, kuruhusu matibabu ya haraka.
translations.reference: Syphilis Overview: Syphilis is a bacterial sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by Treponema pallidum which results in substantial morbidity and mortality, and it is curable. Syphilis is transmitted through sexual contact with the infectious lesions, via blood transfusion or from a pregnant woman to her fetus. Mother-to-child transmission of syphilis is usually devastating to the fetus in cases that were not detected or treated sufficiently early in pregnancy. Maternal syphilis, when untreated, treated late or not treated with penicillin, results in adverse birth outcomes (ABOs) estimated in 50−80% of cases, depending on the stage of syphilis. These ABOs are often severe and include stillbirth (most commonly), neonatal death, prematurity, low birth weight, and congenitally infected infants. Syphilis can be prevented through safer sex practices including the correct and consistent use of condoms. Many people with syphilis do not have any symptoms or have only minor symptoms that go unnoticed. Identifying infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes including congenital syphilis. Screening can be done using rapid tests that can provide results in less than 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment.
translations.hypothesis: Kaswende Summary: Kaswende is a sexually transmitted infection (STI) caused by the bacterium Treponema pallidum, which can cause serious illness and death. It is treatable. Kaswende is transmitted through sexual contact and can also be spread through open wounds or by sharing needles. Transmission from mother to child can be particularly dangerous, as the infection can cause serious complications for the baby if it is not detected and treated early in pregnancy.
