Untreated or inadequately treated kaswende can lead to serious reproductive outcomes (ABOs) in 50-80% of cases, depending on the severity of the infection. These ABOs often include stillbirth, neonatal death, premature birth, low birth weight, and congenital syphilis. Kaswende can be prevented through safe sexual practices, including the consistent and correct use of condoms. Many people with kaswende have no symptoms or only mild symptoms that are easily mistaken for other conditions. Early detection through laboratory testing and appropriate treatment can prevent further transmission and serious reproductive outcomes, including congenital syphilis. Rapid testing methods are available that can provide results in under 20 minutes, allowing for prompt treatment.

translations.source: Isipotibiwa, kaswende hudumu miaka mingi na hupitia hatua tofauti. Matibabu ya kaswende hufanywa na penicillin ya sindano ya benzathine, na dozi 1 hadi 3 kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. Penicillin pekee inaweza kuzuia kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Matibabu na penicillin yametatizwa na upungufu wa mara kwa mara na wa muda mrefu wa penicillin ya benzathine katika nchi nyingi, zikiwemo nchi zenye kipato cha juu, cha chini na cha kati. Dalili: Watu wengi wenye kaswende hawaonyeshi dalili au hawazioni. Kaswende ambayo haijatibiwa, hudumu miaka mingi na ina sifa katika hatua tofauti. Maambukizi ya ngono kwa kawaida hutokea katika hatua za mwanzo, yaani, hadi miaka 2 baada ya kuambukizwa. Wakati wa awamu ya awali (kaswende ya msingi), ambayo hudumu kwa wastani wa siku 21, kidonda cha upweke, kisicho na uchungu, kawaida kigumu na cha pande zote (chancre) huonekana kwenye sehemu ya chanjo (mawasiliano), mara nyingi kwenye uke, uume au mkundu (lakini pia inaweza kuwa nje ya sehemu ya siri) na inaweza kwenda bila kutambuliwa. Chancre huponya kwa au bila matibabu ndani ya wiki 3 hadi 10. Ikiwa haijatibiwa, ugonjwa unaendelea hadi hatua ya sekondari.
translations.reference: When untreated, syphilis lasts many years and goes through different stages. Treatment for syphilis is done with injectable penicillin benzathine, with 1 to 3 doses depending on the stage of the disease. Only penicillin can prevent congenital syphilis. Treatment with penicillin has been hampered by periodic and prolonged shortages of benzathine penicillin in numerous countries, including high-, low- and middle-income countries. Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not present symptoms or do not perceive them. Untreated, syphilis lasts many years and is characterized into different stages. Sexual transmission typically occurs during early stages, i.e., up to 2 years after infection. During the initial phase (primary syphilis), which lasts on average 21 days, a solitary, painless, usually hard and round sore (chancre) appears at the site of inoculation (contact), often in the vagina, penis or anus (but it may also be extra-genital) and may go unnoticed. The chancre heals with or without treatment within 3 to 10 weeks. If untreated, the disease progresses to the secondary stage.
translations.hypothesis: If left untreated, syphilis can persist for many years and progress through different stages. Treatment for syphilis involves penicillin injections, with a dosage of 1 to 3 depending on the stage of the disease. Penicillin alone can cure primary syphilis. However, treatment with penicillin is often disrupted by infrequent or long-term interruptions in the use of benzathine penicillin in many countries, including those with high, low, and middle-income economies. Symptoms: Many people with syphilis do not show symptoms or are unaware that they have the infection. Syphilis that is left untreated can persist for many years and progress through different stages. Sexual transmission typically occurs in the early stages, i.e., up to 2 years after infection. During the primary stage (lasting an average of 21 days), a painless, ulcer-like sore (chancre) is typically seen on the site of infection (mucous membranes), most commonly on the genitals, but it can also occur on the rectum (or even outside the genital area). The chancre heals within 3 to 10 weeks, either on its own or with treatment. If left untreated, the infection progresses to secondary syphilis.

translations.source: Pili kaswende ina sifa ya upele ambao unaweza kutofautiana sana na kuiga hali zingine za kuambukiza au zisizo za kuambukiza, lakini kitabia huathiri viganja na nyayo. Upele hauwashi na unaweza kuwa mdogo vya kutosha kupuuzwa. Katika sehemu zenye joto na unyevunyevu za mwili, kama vile njia ya haja kubwa na midomo, vidonda vikubwa vyeupe au kijivu vilivyoinuliwa vinaweza kutokea katika eneo la chancre ya msingi (condyloma lata). Dalili na ishara za kaswende ya pili hutatuliwa yenyewe, hata bila matibabu. Bila matibabu, hatua ya latent huanza. Kaswende iliyojificha haitoi dalili zozote za kimatibabu au ishara na matibabu ni tofauti na awamu ya awali. Baada ya miaka au miongo bila matibabu, magonjwa ya neva na ya moyo na mishipa kati ya wengine yanaweza kutokea. Matibabu: Utambuzi wa kaswende unatokana na historia ya mtu, uchunguzi wa kimwili, upimaji wa kimaabara na wakati mwingine radiolojia, kwani dalili si za kawaida au hazionekani. Kutambua maambukizi ya dalili kupitia vipimo vya maabara na matibabu ya kesi chanya kutazuia maambukizi zaidi na matokeo mabaya ya ujauzito, ikiwa ni pamoja na kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Vipimo vya kimaabara vya kaswende ni pamoja na kugundua moja kwa moja T. pallidum au mbinu zisizo za moja kwa moja kama vile seroloji.
translations.reference: Secondary syphilis is characterized by a rash that can vary widely and mimic other infectious or non-infectious conditions, but characteristically affects the palms and soles. The rash is non-itchy and can be minimal enough to be overlooked. In warm and moist areas of the body, such as the anus and labia, large white or grey raised lesions may develop at the location of the primary chancre (condyloma lata). The symptoms and signs of secondary syphilis spontaneously resolve, even without treatment. Without treatment, the latent stage starts. Latent syphilis presents no clinical symptoms or signs and the treatment is different from the early phase. After years or decades without treatment, neurological and cardiovascular diseases among others may occur. Treatment: Syphilis diagnosis is based on the person’s history, physical examination, laboratory testing and sometimes radiology, as symptoms are not common or noticeable. Identifying asymptomatic infection through laboratory tests and treatment of positive cases will prevent further transmission and adverse pregnancy outcomes, including congenital syphilis. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct detection of T. pallidum or indirect methods such as serology.
translations.hypothesis: The second stage is characterized by a proliferation of cells that can vary greatly in appearance, mimicking benign or malignant conditions, but primarily affecting the cervix and surrounding tissues. The proliferation is not cancerous and can be subtle enough to be missed. In warm, moist areas of the body, such as the cervix and mouth, large, pale or grayish, raised lesions may develop in the area of the primary chancre (condyloma lata). The symptoms and signs of the second stage are often not apparent, even without treatment. Without treatment, the latent stage begins. The latent stage does not cause any noticeable symptoms or signs, and the treatment is different from the initial stage. After several years or decades without treatment, neurological and cardiovascular problems can develop. Treatment: Diagnosis of the second stage is based on a patient's history, physical examination, laboratory tests, and sometimes radiology, as the symptoms are often atypical or absent. Identifying infections through laboratory tests and treating positive cases can prevent further spread and complications, including congenital syphilis. Laboratory tests for syphilis include direct detection of T. pallidum or indirect methods such as serology.

translations.source: Uchunguzi wa haraka na vipimo vya uchunguzi pia vinapatikana na vinaweza kutoa matokeo kwa chini ya dakika 20, kuwezesha kuanza kwa matibabu mara moja. WHO inapendekeza kaswende kutibiwa kwa penicillin ya sindano (benzathine penicillin G) kwa dozi 1 au 3, kulingana na hatua ya ugonjwa. WHO ina miongozo ya kina ya matibabu ya kaswende, na moja mahsusi kwa uchunguzi na matibabu ya kaswende katika ujauzito. Kaswende ya kuzaliwa Kwa sasa hakuna vipimo vya uchunguzi wa kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Watoto wote walio hai au waliozaliwa wakiwa wamekufa katika wanawake walio na kaswende wanapaswa kuchunguzwa kwa ushahidi wa kaswende ya kuzaliwa. Watoto waliozaliwa hai wanapaswa kuchunguzwa na kupimwa wakati wa kuzaliwa ili kufafanua mahitaji ya matibabu. Inapoonyeshwa, pia kwa vipindi vya kila mwezi kwa miezi 3 hadi serolojia ya mtoto mchanga iwe, na kubaki, hasi, kwani kingamwili za mama zinaweza kupitishwa kwa mtoto wakati wa ujauzito.
translations.reference: Rapid screening and diagnostic tests are also available and can provide results in less than 20 minutes, facilitating immediate treatment initiation. WHO recommends syphilis to be treated with injectable penicillin (benzathine penicillin G) with 1 or 3 doses, depending on the stage of the disease. WHO has detailed treatment guidelines for syphilis, and one specifically for the screening and treatment of syphilis in pregnancy. Congenital syphilis There are currently no diagnostic tests for congenital syphilis. All live or stillborn infants of women with syphilis should be examined for evidence of congenital syphilis. Live-born infants should be examined and tested at birth to define treatment requirement. When indicated, also at monthly intervals for 3 months until the infant’s serology are, and remain, negative, as maternal antibodies can be passively transmitted to the baby during pregnancy.
translations.hypothesis: Rapid tests and diagnostic tests are also available and can provide results in under 20 minutes, allowing for immediate treatment to begin. The WHO recommends that pregnant women with toxoplasmosis be treated with penicillin injections (benzathine penicillin G) at a dose of 1 or 3, depending on the severity of the infection. The WHO has detailed guidelines for the treatment of toxoplasmosis, including specific guidelines for diagnosis and treatment of toxoplasmosis during pregnancy. Congenital toxoplasmosis: Currently, there are no specific diagnostic tests for congenital toxoplasmosis. All live or deceased infants born to mothers with toxoplasmosis should be tested for congenital toxoplasmosis. Live-born infants should be tested and screened at birth to determine their treatment needs. In addition, regular screening should be performed every month for 3 to 6 months, or until the infant's serology is negative, as the mother's infection can be transmitted to the infant during pregnancy.

translations.source: Taeniasis na cysticercosis Muhtasari: Kuambukizwa na minyoo Taenia solium kunaweza kusababisha hali mbili tofauti: taeniasis na cysticercosis. Taeniasis ni maambukizi ya matumbo na minyoo na hutokea wakati mtu anakula nyama ya nguruwe mbichi au ambayo haijaiva vizuri. Taeniasis haina athari kubwa kwa afya ya binadamu. Hata hivyo, mayai ya minyoo hupita kwenye kinyesi cha mtoaji wa minyoo na kukosekana kwa usafi wa mazingira kunaweza kuchafua mazingira. Ingawa mayai ya minyoo yanaambukiza nguruwe, mayai ya T. solium pia yanaweza kumwambukiza binadamu ikiwa yamemezwa na mtu (kupitia njia ya kinyesi-kwa mdomo, au kwa kumeza chakula au maji yaliyochafuliwa). Umezaji wa mayai ya minyoo husababisha kuambukizwa na vimelea vya buu kwenye tishu kama vile misuli, ngozi, macho na mfumo mkuu wa neva (human cysticercosis), pamoja na athari mbaya kwa afya. Wakati uvimbe hutokea kwenye ubongo, hali hiyo inaitwa neurocysticercosis. Dalili: Taeniasis kawaida huwa na dalili za upole na zisizo maalum zinazohusiana na maambukizi ya matumbo (maumivu ya tumbo, kichefuchefu, kuhara, nk). Dalili hizi zinaweza kuendelea hadi mdudu afe baada ya matibabu, vinginevyo anaweza kuishi kwa miaka.
translations.reference: Taeniasis and cysticercosis Overview: Infection with the tapeworm Taenia solium can result in two distinct conditions: taeniasis and cysticercosis. Taeniasis is the intestinal infection with the tapeworm and it occurs when a person eats raw or undercooked, infected pork. Taeniasis has no major impact on human health. However, tapeworm eggs pass in the faeces of the tapeworm carrier and in the absence of a proper sanitation may contaminate the environment. While tapeworm eggs are infective for pigs, T. solium eggs may also infect humans if they are ingested by a person (via the fecal-oral route, or by ingesting contaminated food or water). Ingestion of tapeworm eggs causes infection with the larval parasite in the tissues such as muscles, skin, eyes and the central nervous system (human cysticercosis), with possible devastating effects on health. When cysts develop in the brain, the condition is referred to as neurocysticercosis. Symptoms: Taeniasis is usually characterized by mild and non-specific symptoms associated to an intestinal infection (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhoea, etc.). These symptoms may continue until the tapeworm dies following treatment, otherwise it may live for years.
translations.hypothesis: Taeniasis and Cysticercosis: Summary: Infection with the *Taenia solium* tapeworm can lead to two distinct conditions: taeniasis and cysticercosis. Taeniasis is the infection of the intestines by the tapeworm, and it occurs when a person eats raw or undercooked pork containing the larvae. Taeniasis does not typically cause serious health problems in humans. However, the eggs of the tapeworm can pass through the stool of the infected person and contaminate the environment. While the eggs can infect pigs, they can also infect humans if they are ingested (through the fecal-oral route, or by consuming contaminated food or water). Once the eggs hatch in the human body, they develop into cysts in various tissues, such as muscles, skin, eyes, and the central nervous system (human cysticercosis), which can cause significant health problems. When the cysts develop in the brain, the condition is called neurocysticercosis. Symptoms: Taeniasis typically presents with mild and non-specific symptoms related to intestinal infection (abdominal pain, nausea, diarrhea, etc.). These symptoms may persist until the person dies after treatment, or they may resolve and the person can live for many years.

translations.source: Kwa cysticercosis, kipindi cha incubation ni tofauti, na watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kubaki bila dalili kwa miaka. Katika baadhi ya maeneo yenye ugonjwa huo, watu walioambukizwa wanaweza kuendeleza vinundu vinavyoonekana au vinavyoweza kubalika (kitunguu kidogo kigumu au nodi ambayo ni dhabiti ambayo inaweza kutambuliwa kwa kuguswa) chini ya ngozi. Neurocysticercosis inahusishwa na aina mbalimbali za ishara na dalili kulingana na idadi, ukubwa, hatua na eneo la mabadiliko ya pathological pamoja na majibu ya kinga ya mwenyeji na genotype ya vimelea, lakini pia inaweza kuwa kliniki bila dalili. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha maumivu ya kichwa ya muda mrefu, upofu, kifafa (kifafa ikiwa kinajirudia), hydrocephalus, meningitis, shida ya akili na dalili zinazosababishwa na vidonda vinavyochukua nafasi za mfumo mkuu wa neva. Matibabu: Matibabu ya taeniasis na Taenia solium ni muhimu ili kuzuia neurocysticercosis na kama chombo cha kusaidia katika kudhibiti au kusimamisha mzunguko wa maambukizi ya vimelea. Matibabu yanaweza kufanywa kwa misingi ya mtu binafsi, au kama Utawala wa Misa ya Dawa kulingana na hali ya eneo na mbinu za udhibiti zinazotekelezwa. Taeniasis inaweza kutibiwa kwa kutumia anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). Katika neurocysticercosis, kwa kuwa uharibifu wa cysts unaweza kusababisha majibu ya uchochezi, matibabu maalum ya ugonjwa hai inahitajika na inaweza kujumuisha kozi ndefu na kipimo cha juu cha anthelmintic, pamoja na tiba ya kusaidia na corticosteroids na/au dawa za kuzuia kifafa, na ikiwezekana upasuaji. Muda wa matibabu na kipimo inaweza kutofautiana sana na inategemea hasa idadi, ukubwa, eneo na hatua ya maendeleo ya cysts, edema yao ya uchochezi inayozunguka, ukali na ukali wa dalili za kliniki au ishara.
translations.reference: For cysticercosis, the incubation period is variable, and infected people may remain asymptomatic for years. In some endemic regions, infected people may develop visible or palpable nodules (a small solid bump or node which is solid that can be detected by touch) beneath the skin. Neurocysticercosis is associated with a variety of signs and symptoms depending on the number, size, stage and location of the pathological changes as well as the host’s immune response and the parasite’s genotype, but it can also be clinically asymptomatic. Symptoms may include chronic headaches, blindness, seizures (epilepsy if they are recurrent), hydrocephalus, meningitis, dementia and symptoms caused by lesions occupying spaces of the central nervous system. Treatment: The treatment of taeniasis by Taenia solium is important to prevent neurocysticercosis and as a tool to assist in controlling or stopping the parasite transmission cycle. The treatment can be done on an individual bases, or as Mass Drug Administration depending on the local circumstances and the control approaches being implemented. Taeniasis can be treated using anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). In neurocysticercosis, since the destruction of cysts may lead to an inflammatory response, specialised treatment of active disease is required and may include long courses with high doses of anthelmintic, as well as supporting therapy with corticosteroids and/or anti-epileptic drugs, and possibly surgery. The dosage and the duration of treatment can vary greatly and depend mainly on the number, size, location and developmental stage of the cysts, their surrounding inflammatory edema, acuteness and severity of clinical symptoms or signs.
translations.hypothesis: For cysticercosis, the incubation period varies, and individuals infected may remain asymptomatic for years. In some areas, infected individuals may develop visible or palpable (a small, firm lump that can be felt) cysts under the skin. Neurocysticercosis is associated with a variety of signs and symptoms depending on the number, size, stage, and location of the pathological changes, as well as the host's immune response and the parasite genotype, but it can also be asymptomatic. Symptoms may include chronic headaches, seizures, paralysis, hydrocephalus, meningitis, cognitive impairment, and other symptoms caused by cysts encroaching on the central nervous system. Treatment: Treatment for taeniasis and Taenia solium is important to prevent neurocysticercosis and as a means of controlling or interrupting the parasite's life cycle. Treatment can be individualized or as a mass drug administration, depending on the prevalence and control measures in place. Taeniasis can be treated with anthelmintics (Praziquantel, Niclosamide, Albendazole). In neurocysticercosis, because cyst destruction can cause inflammatory reactions, specific treatment for the disease is necessary and may include a prolonged course of anthelmintics, as well as supportive care and corticosteroids and/or anti-inflammatory medications, and possibly surgery. The duration and dosage of treatment can vary widely and depends primarily on the number, size, location, and stage of development of the cysts, the extent of the surrounding inflammatory edema, the severity and nature of the clinical signs or symptoms.

translations.source: Pepopunda Muhtasari: Pepopunda ni ugonjwa hatari unaoambukizwa kwa kuambukizwa na vijidudu vya bakteria, Clostridium tetani, wanaoishi kwenye udongo, mate, vumbi na samadi. Bakteria wanaweza kuingia ndani ya mwili kupitia majeraha ya kina, majeraha au kuchomwa na kuathiri mfumo wa neva. Maambukizi husababisha mikazo ya misuli yenye uchungu, haswa ya misuli ya taya na shingo, na inajulikana kama "lockjaw". Watu wa rika zote wanaweza kupata pepopunda lakini ugonjwa huu ni wa kawaida na mbaya zaidi kwa watoto wachanga na mama zao wakati mama hana kinga dhidi ya pepopunda kwa chanjo, pepopunda toxoid. Pepopunda inayotokea wakati wa ujauzito au ndani ya wiki 6 za mwisho wa ujauzito huitwa pepopunda ya mama, wakati pepopunda ikitokea ndani ya siku 28 za kwanza za maisha inaitwa pepopunda changa. Ugonjwa huu unasalia kuwa tatizo muhimu la afya ya umma katika sehemu nyingi za dunia, lakini hasa katika nchi za kipato cha chini au wilaya, ambapo chanjo ni ya chini na njia chafu za uzazi ni za kawaida. WHO inakadiria kuwa katika 2018 (mwaka wa hivi karibuni ambao makadirio yanapatikana), watoto wachanga 25,000 walikufa kutokana na pepopunda ya watoto wachanga, 88% walipungua kutoka hali hiyo mnamo 2000. Dalili: Takriban visa vyote vya pepopunda hutokea kwa watu ambao hawajawahi kupata chanjo au kwa watu wazima ambao hawajasasisha picha zao za nyongeza. Ishara na dalili za pepopunda huonekana wakati wowote kutoka siku 3 hadi 21 baada ya bakteria ya pepopunda kuingia mwilini kupitia kidonda.
translations.reference: Tetanus Overview: Tetanus is a serious illness contracted through exposure to the spores of the bacterium, Clostridium tetani, which live in soil, saliva, dust and manure. The bacteria can enter the body through a deep cuts, wounds or burns affecting the nervous system. The infection leads to painful muscle contractions, particularly of the jaw and neck muscle, and is commonly known as “lockjaw”. People of all ages can get tetanus but the disease is particularly common and serious in newborn babies and their mothers when the mother is unprotected from tetanus by the vaccine, tetanus toxoid. Tetanus occurring during pregnancy or within 6 weeks of the end of pregnancy is called maternal tetanus, while tetanus occurring within the first 28 days of life is called neonatal tetanus. The disease remains an important public health problem in many parts of the world, but especially in low-income countries or districts, where immunization coverage is low and unclean birth practices are common. WHO estimates that in 2018 (the latest year for which estimates are available), 25 000 newborns died from neonatal tetanus, 88% reduction from the situation in 2000. Symptoms: Nearly all cases of tetanus occur in people who have never been vaccinated or in adults who have not kept up to date on their booster shots. Signs and symptoms of tetanus appear anytime from 3 to 21 days after tetanus bacteria enter the body through a wound.
translations.hypothesis: Pepopunda Summary: Pepopunda is a serious infection caused by bacteria, Clostridium tetani, which live in soil, dust, manure, and decaying organic matter. The bacteria can enter the body through wounds or cuts and affect the nervous system. The infection causes muscle spasms, especially in the jaw and neck, and is known as "lockjaw". People of all ages can get pepopunda, but the disease is more common and severe in infants and young children, especially when the mother has not been vaccinated against it. Pepopunda that occurs during pregnancy or within the first 6 weeks of life is called maternal tetanus, while tetanus that occurs within the first 28 days of life is called neonatal tetanus. This disease remains a major public health problem in many parts of the world, particularly in low-income countries and rural areas, where vaccination rates are low and unsafe delivery practices are common. The WHO estimates that in 2018 (the most recent year for which data is available), 25,000 newborns died from neonatal tetanus, with 88% of these deaths occurring before 2000. Symptoms: Most cases of tetanus occur in people who have never been vaccinated or in adults whose vaccination is not up-to-date. Symptoms of tetanus appear anytime from 3 to 21 days after the Clostridium tetani bacteria enter the body through a wound.

translations.source: Kesi nyingi hutokea ndani ya siku 14. Dalili zinaweza kujumuisha: kuuma kwa taya au kutoweza kufungua mdomo misuli ya spasms mara nyingi nyuma, ya tumbo na mwisho mshtuko wa ghafla wa misuli yenye uchungu mara nyingi huchochewa na kelele za ghafla shida kumeza mishtuko wa moyo maumivu ya kichwa homa na jasho mabadiliko katika shinikizo la damu au kasi ya moyo.
translations.reference: Most cases occur within 14 days. Symptoms can include: jaw cramping or the inability to open the mouth muscle spasms often in the back, abdomen and extremities sudden painful muscle spasms often triggered by sudden noises trouble swallowing seizures headache fever and sweating changes in blood pressure or fast heart rate.
translations.hypothesis: Many cases resolve within 14 days. Symptoms may include: pain in the abdomen or inability to open the mouth, muscle spasms, often in the back, abdomen, and legs, sudden muscle weakness with tenderness, often triggered by sudden noises, difficulty swallowing, heart palpitations, headaches, fever, and changes in blood pressure or heart rate.

translations.source: Katika tetanasi ya watoto wachanga, dalili ni pamoja na mshtuko wa misuli, ambayo mara nyingi hutanguliwa na kutoweza kwa mtoto mchanga kunyonya au kunyonyesha, na kulia sana. Tetanasi hugunduliwa kwa misingi ya vipengele vya kliniki na hauhitaji uthibitisho wa maabara. Pepopunda inahitaji matibabu katika kituo cha matibabu, mara nyingi katika hospitali ya rufaa. Watu wanaopona pepopunda hawana kinga ya asili na wanaweza kuambukizwa tena na hivyo wanahitaji kuchanjwa. Kinga: Pepopunda inaweza kuzuiwa kwa chanjo zenye pepopunda-toxoid (TTCV), ambazo zimejumuishwa katika programu za kawaida za chanjo ulimwenguni kote na kusimamiwa wakati wa mawasiliano ya utunzaji wa ujauzito. Ili kulindwa katika maisha yote, WHO inapendekeza kwamba mtu binafsi apokee dozi 6 (3 za msingi pamoja na dozi 3 za nyongeza) za TTCV. Mfululizo wa msingi wa dozi 3 unapaswa kuanza mapema kama wiki 6 za umri, na dozi zinazofuata zipewe na muda wa chini wa wiki 4 kati ya dozi. Dozi 3 za nyongeza zinafaa kutolewa katika mwaka wa pili wa maisha (miezi 12-23), katika umri wa miaka 4-7, na katika umri wa miaka 9-15. Kimsingi, kunapaswa kuwa na angalau miaka 4 kati ya dozi za nyongeza. Kuna aina nyingi za chanjo zinazotumiwa kulinda dhidi ya pepopunda: chanjo ya diphtheria na pepopunda (DT) chanjo ya diphtheria, pepopunda na kifaduro (DTaP) chanjo ya chanjo ya dondakoo na diphtheria (Td) chanjo ya diphtheria, diphtheria na pertussis (Tdap).
translations.reference: In neonatal tetanus, symptoms include muscle spasms, which are often preceded by the newborn’s inability to suck or breastfeed, and excessive crying. Tetanus is diagnosed on the basis of clinical features and does not require laboratory confirmation. Tetanus requires treatment in a medical facility, often in a referral hospital. People who recover from tetanus do not have natural immunity and can be infected again and therefore need to be immunized. Prevention: Tetanus can be prevented through immunization with tetanus-toxoid-containing vaccines (TTCV), which are included in routine immunization programmes globally and administered during antenatal care contacts. To be protected throughout life, WHO recommends that an individual receives 6 doses (3 primary plus 3 booster doses) of TTCV. The 3-dose primary series should begin as early as 6 weeks of age, with subsequent doses given with a minimum interval of 4 weeks between doses. The 3 booster doses should preferably be given during the second year of life (12–23 months), at 4–7 years of age, and at 9–15 years of age. Ideally, there should be at least 4 years between booster doses. There are many kinds of vaccines used to protect against tetanus: diphtheria and tetanus (DT) vaccinesdiphtheria, tetanus, and pertussis (whooping cough) (DTaP) vaccinestetanus and diphtheria (Td) vaccinestetanus, diphtheria, and pertussis (Tdap) vaccines.
translations.hypothesis: In tetanus, symptoms include muscle spasms, which are often accompanied by the infant's inability to suck or feed, and severe pain. Tetanus is diagnosed based on clinical signs and does not require laboratory testing. Treatment for tetanus requires hospitalization, often in a specialized hospital. Individuals recovering from tetanus are not immune and can be re-infected, so they require ongoing monitoring. Prevention: Tetanus can be prevented through vaccination with tetanus-toxoid (TT) vaccines, which are included in routine vaccination programs worldwide and administered during pregnancy. To be protected throughout life, the WHO recommends that individuals receive 6 doses (3 primary doses plus 3 booster doses) of TT vaccine. The primary series of 3 doses should begin as early as 6 weeks of age, with subsequent doses spaced at least 4 weeks apart. Booster doses should be administered at 12-23 months of age, at 4-7 years of age, and at 9-15 years of age. There should be at least 4 years between booster doses. There are several types of vaccines used to protect against tetanus: diphtheria and tetanus (DT) vaccine, diphtheria, tetanus, and acellular pertussis (DTaP) vaccine, tetanus and diphtheria (Td) vaccine, and diphtheria, tetanus, and acellular pertussis (Tdap) vaccine.

translations.source: Pepopunda ya watoto wachanga inaweza kuzuiwa kwa kuwapa chanjo ya TTCV wanawake walio katika umri wa kuzaa, wakati wa ujauzito au nje ya ujauzito. Zaidi ya hayo, mbinu thabiti za matibabu zinaweza pia kuzuia ugonjwa wa pepopunda ikiwa ni pamoja na kujifungua safi na utunzaji wa kamba wakati wa kuzaa, na utunzaji sahihi wa jeraha kwa upasuaji na meno.
translations.reference: Neonatal tetanus can be prevented by immunizing women of reproductive age with TTCV, either during pregnancy or outside of pregnancy. Additionally, robust medical practices can also prevent tetanus disease including clean delivery and cord care during childbirth, and proper wound care for surgical and dental procedures.
translations.hypothesis: Preventing postpartum infections in newborns can be achieved by administering the TT vaccine to women of childbearing age, either during pregnancy or after childbirth. Furthermore, consistent medical practices can also prevent these infections, including safe delivery and postpartum care, as well as proper wound care after surgery and dental procedures.

translations.source: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na Jibu Muhtasari: Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na Jibu (TBE) ni sababu muhimu ya maambukizo ya virusi ya mfumo mkuu wa neva katika nchi za mashariki, kati, kaskazini na inazidi kuongezeka magharibi mwa Ulaya, na kaskazini mwa Uchina, Mongolia, na Shirikisho la Urusi. Virusi vya encephalitis vinavyoenezwa na Jibu ni mwanachama wa familia ya Flaviviridae. Takriban visa 10 000-12 000 vya ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoenezwa na kupe huripotiwa kila mwaka, lakini takwimu hii inaaminika kuwa chini sana kuliko idadi halisi ya kesi za kimatibabu. Idadi kubwa ya maambukizo na virusi hutokana na kupe walioambukizwa, ambao mara nyingi hubakia kushikamana na ngozi kwa siku. Katika matukio machache, maambukizi yanaweza kutokana na unywaji wa maziwa ambayo hayajasafishwa kutoka kwa mbuzi, kondoo au ng'ombe. Watu hugusana na kupe wakati wa shughuli za nje katika maeneo ya misitu hadi mwinuko wa mita 2000. Hakuna maambukizi ya moja kwa moja kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu. Dalili: Maambukizi mengi hubaki bila dalili. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa wa kliniki, kipindi cha incubation cha encephalitis inayoenezwa na kupe huchukua siku 2-28 (mara nyingi siku 7-14) na hufuatiwa na siku 1-8 za dalili za jumla za baridi, kama vile uchovu, maumivu ya kichwa na malaise ya jumla kawaida hujumuishwa na homa ya ≥38 °C.
translations.reference: Tick-borne encephalitis Overview: Tick-borne encephalitis (TBE) is an important cause of viral infections of the central nervous system in eastern, central, northern and increasingly western European countries, and in northern China, Mongolia, and the Russian Federation. Tick-borne encephalitis virus is a member of the family Flaviviridae. Approximately 10 000–12 000 clinical cases of tick-borne encephalitis are reported each year, but this figure is believed to be significantly lower than the actual total number of clinical cases. The vast majority of infections with the virus result from infected ticks, which often remain firmly attached to the skin for days. On rare occasions, infection can result from consumption of unpasteurized milk from infect goats, sheep or cows. People come in contact with the ticks during outdoor activities in forested areas up to an altitude of about 2000 meters. There is no direct person-to-person transmission. Symptoms: Most infections remain asymptomatic. In case of clinical illness, the incubation period for tick-borne encephalitis lasts 2–28 days (most commonly 7–14 days) and is followed by 1–8 days of general cold symptoms, such as fatigue, headache and general malaise, usually combined with fever of ≥38 °C.
translations.hypothesis: Encephalitis Transmitted by Ticks: Encephalitis transmitted by ticks (TBE) is a significant cause of viral infections of the central nervous system in Eastern, Central, Northern Europe, and is increasing in Western Europe, as well as in Northern China, Mongolia, and the Russian Federation. The viruses that cause TBE belong to the Flaviviridae family. Approximately 10,000-12,000 cases of TBE are reported each year, but it is believed that this number is significantly lower than the actual number of clinical cases. The majority of infections result from tick bites, during which the ticks often remain attached to the skin for several days. In rare cases, infections can occur through the consumption of unpasteurized milk from animals that have been infected with the virus. People are most at risk of being bitten by ticks during outdoor activities in wooded areas up to an altitude of 2000 meters. There is no direct transmission of the virus from person to person. Symptoms: Many infections are asymptomatic. In clinical cases, the incubation period for TBE ranges from 2-28 days (most commonly 7-14 days), followed by a period of 1-8 days of general flu-like symptoms, such as fatigue, headache, and malaise, often accompanied by a fever of ≥38 °C.

translations.source: Baada ya muda usio na dalili wa siku 1-20 hadi 15% ya wagonjwa hupata awamu ya pili ya ugonjwa huo na homa inayozidi 40 ° C mara kwa mara na ishara za kuhusika kwa mfumo mkuu wa neva, kama vile meninjitisi (k.m. homa, maumivu ya kichwa na kukakamaa), encephalitis (kwa mfano, kusinzia, kuchanganyikiwa, na usumbufu wa hisia), myelitis au radiculitis. Ugonjwa wa encephalitis unaoendelea katika awamu hii ya pili unaweza kusababisha kupooza, matokeo ya kudumu au kifo. Karibu 1% ya kesi zilizo na patholojia za neva zinaweza kufa; viwango vya juu vya vifo vimeripotiwa kutoka kwa shirikisho la Urusi, ambalo linaweza kuhusishwa na aina tofauti ya virusi. Ukali wa ugonjwa huongezeka na umri wa mgonjwa, lakini vifo vimeripotiwa kutoka kwa vikundi vyote vya umri. Hakuna matibabu maalum kwa ugojwa tick-borne encephalitis. Kinga: Watu wanaweza kujilinda dhidi ya kupe kwa kuvaa mavazi yanayofaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na suruali ndefu na viatu vilivyofungwa, wanapopanda au kupiga kambi katika nchi au maeneo yaliyo hatarini. Mwili mzima unapaswa kuchunguzwa kila siku na kupe zilizounganishwa kuondolewa haraka iwezekanavyo. Matumizi ya bidhaa za maziwa ambazo hazijasafishwa pia zinapaswa kuepukwa katika maeneo hayo. Kinga hutoa ulinzi bora zaidi. Hivi sasa, kuna chanjo 4 zinazotumiwa sana za ubora uliohakikishwa:
translations.reference: After an asymptomatic interval of 1–20 days up to 15% of patients experience a second phase of the disease characterized by fever frequently exceeding 40 °C and signs of central nervous system involvement, such as meningitis (e.g. fever, headache, and a stiff neck), encephalitis (e.g., drowsiness, confusion, and sensory disturbances), myelitis or radiculitis. Encephalitis developing during this second phase may result in paralysis, permanent sequelae or death. About 1% of cases with neurological pathologies may die; higher fatality rates have been reported from the Russian federation, which may be related to a different virus subtype. Severity of illness increases with age of the patient, but fatalities have been reported from all age groups. There is no specific treatment for tick-borne encephalitis. Prevention: People can protect themselves from ticks by wearing appropriate clothing, including long trousers and closed footwear, when hiking or camping in countries or areas at risk. The whole body should be inspected daily and attached ticks removed as soon as possible. The consumption of unpasteurized dairy products should also be avoided in those areas. Immunization offers the most effective protection. Currently, there are 4 widely used vaccines of assured quality:
translations.hypothesis: After an asymptomatic period of 1-20 days, 15% of patients enter the second phase of the disease, characterized by a fever exceeding 40°C and neurological symptoms, such as seizures (e.g., convulsions, headaches, and confusion), encephalitis (e.g., drowsiness, disorientation, and sensory disturbances), or myelitis or radiculitis. Prolonged encephalitis in this second phase can lead to coma, permanent disability, or death. Approximately 1% of cases with neurological pathology can result in death; high mortality rates have been reported from Russia, which may be associated with different strains of the virus. The severity of the disease increases with the patient's age, but deaths have been reported in all age groups. There is no specific treatment for tick-borne encephalitis. Prevention: People can protect themselves from tick bites by wearing appropriate clothing, including long sleeves and closed-toe shoes, when hiking or camping in areas where ticks are present. The entire body should be checked daily for ticks, and any ticks found should be removed as quickly as possible. Avoid using unpasteurized milk products in these areas. Prevention provides the best protection. Currently, there are four widely used, quality-assured vaccines:

translations.source: FSME-Immun na Encepur, iliyotengenezwa nchini Austria na Ujerumani ipasavyo, na kulingana na aina za virusi za Ulaya ; na TBE-Moscow na EnceVir, iliyotengenezwa katika Shirikisho la Urusi na kwa kulingana na matatizo ya Mashariki ya Mbali. Chanjo 4 zinachukuliwa kuwa salama na zenye ufanisi. Katika maeneo ambayo ugonjwa huo umeenea sana, WHO inapendekeza kwamba chanjo itolewe kwa vikundi vyote vya umri, pamoja na watoto. Kupe pia huambukiza Borreliosis (ugonjwa wa Lyme), ambao ni maambukizi ya bakteria. Chanjo ya TBE haifai dhidi ya ugonjwa huu, ambao hata hivyo unatibika kwa dawa za kuua viini.
translations.reference: FSME-Immun and Encepur, manufactured in Austria and Germany respectively, and based on European strains of the virus; and TBE-Moscow and EnceVir, manufactured in the Russian Federation and based on Far-Eastern strains. The 4 vaccines are considered to be safe and effective. In areas where the disease is highly endemic, WHO recommends that vaccination be offered to all age groups, including children. Ticks also transmit Borreliosis (Lyme disease), which is a bacterial infection. TBE vaccination is not effective against this disease, which however is treatable with antimicrobials.
translations.hypothesis: FSME-Immun and Encepur, manufactured in Austria and Germany respectively, and based on European virus strains; and TBE-Moscow and EnceVir, manufactured in the Russian Federation and based on Eastern European strains. These four vaccines are considered safe and effective. In areas where the disease is prevalent, the WHO recommends that the vaccine be administered to all age groups, including children. Köpe also transmits Borreliosis (Lyme disease), which is a bacterial infection. The TBE vaccine is not effective against this disease, which can be treated with antibiotics.

translations.source: Tumbaku Muhtasari: Nikotini iliyo katika tumbaku inalevya sana na utumiaji wa tumbaku ni sababu kuu ya hatari kwa magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa na ya kupumua, zaidi ya aina 20 tofauti za saratani, na hali zingine nyingi za kiafya zinazodhoofisha. Kila mwaka, zaidi ya watu milioni 8 hufa kutokana na matumizi ya tumbaku. Vifo vingi vinavyohusiana na tumbaku hutokea katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati, ambazo mara nyingi hulengwa na kuingiliwa kwa sekta ya tumbaku na masoko. Tumbaku pia inaweza kuwa mbaya kwa wasiovuta sigara. Uvutaji wa moshi wa sigara pia umehusishwa katika matokeo mabaya ya kiafya, na kusababisha vifo milioni 1.2 kila mwaka. Takriban nusu ya watoto wote huvuta hewa iliyochafuliwa na moshi wa tumbaku na watoto 65,000 hufa kila mwaka kutokana na magonjwa yanayohusiana na moshi wa sigara. Kuvuta sigara wakati wa ujauzito kunaweza kusababisha hali kadhaa za kiafya kwa watoto. Bidhaa za tumbaku zinazopashwa joto (HTPs) zina tumbaku na huwaweka watumiaji kwenye hewa chafu zenye sumu, nyingi ambazo husababisha saratani na ni hatari kwa afya. Mifumo ya kielektroniki ya utoaji wa nikotini (ENDS) na mifumo ya kielektroniki ya uwasilishaji isiyo ya nikotini (ENNDS), inayojulikana kama sigara za kielektroniki, haina tumbaku na inaweza au isiwe na nikotini, lakini inadhuru kwa afya na bila shaka si salama.
translations.reference: Tobacco Overview: Nicotine contained in tobacco is highly addictive and tobacco use is a major risk factor for cardiovascular and respiratory diseases, over 20 different types or subtypes of cancer, and many other debilitating health conditions. Every year, more than 8 million people die from tobacco use. Most tobacco-related deaths occur in low- and middle-income countries, which are often targets of intensive tobacco industry interference and marketing. Tobacco can also be deadly for non-smokers. Second-hand smoke exposure has also been implicated in adverse health outcomes, causing 1.2 million deaths annually. Nearly half of all children breathe air polluted by tobacco smoke and 65 000 children die each year due to illnesses related to second-hand smoke. Smoking while pregnant can lead to several life-long health conditions for babies. Heated tobacco products (HTPs) contain tobacco and expose users to toxic emissions, many of which cause cancer and are harmful to health. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS) and electronic non-nicotine delivery systems (ENNDS), commonly known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may or may not contain nicotine, but are harmful to health and undoubtedly unsafe.
translations.hypothesis: Tobacco Summary: Nicotine in tobacco is highly addictive, and the use of tobacco is a major risk factor for heart and lung diseases, as well as over 20 different types of cancer, and many other health problems. Each year, more than 8 million people die from tobacco use. Many of the deaths related to tobacco occur in low- and middle-income countries, which are often targeted by the tobacco industry and its marketing efforts. Tobacco can also be harmful to non-smokers. Exposure to secondhand smoke is also linked to negative health outcomes, resulting in an estimated 1.2 million deaths each year. Approximately half of all children breathe air contaminated with tobacco smoke, and 65,000 children die each year from diseases related to secondhand smoke. Smoking during pregnancy can lead to a number of health problems for the child. Heat-not-burn tobacco products (HHTPs) deliver nicotine in a way that exposes users to harmful chemicals, many of which are known carcinogens. Electronic nicotine delivery systems (ENDS), also known as e-cigarettes, do not contain tobacco and may or may not contain nicotine, but they are harmful to health and are not safe.

translations.source: Hata hivyo, ni mapema mno kutoa jibu wazi kuhusu athari za muda mrefu za HTPs na/au matumizi ya sigara za kielektroniki. Athari: Inakadiriwa kuwa watu bilioni 1.3 duniani kote wanatumia bidhaa za tumbaku, 80% kati yao wakiwa katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Utumiaji wa tumbaku huchangia umaskini kwa kubadilisha matumizi ya kaya kutoka kwa mahitaji ya kimsingi, kama vile chakula na malazi, hadi tumbaku. Tabia hii ya matumizi ni ngumu kudhibiti kwa sababu tumbaku ina uraibu sana. Pia husababisha vifo vya mapema na ulemavu wa watu wazima wenye umri wa kuzalisha katika kaya hivyo kusababisha kupungua kwa mapato ya kaya na kuongezeka kwa gharama za huduma za afya. Pamoja na athari mbaya za tumbaku kwa afya, jumla ya gharama za kiuchumi za uvutaji wa sigara (kutokana na matumizi ya afya na upotevu wa tija kwa pamoja) inakadiriwa kuwa karibu dola za Marekani trilioni 1.4 kwa mwaka, sawa na asilimia 1.8 ya pato la taifa kwa mwaka (GDP). Takriban 40% ya gharama hii ilitokea katika nchi zinazoendelea, ikionyesha mzigo mkubwa ambao nchi hizi zinakabiliwa. Ushuru wa tumbaku hutumiwa kwa kiasi ili kukabiliana na athari hii na inaonekana kama njia ya gharama nafuu zaidi ya kuzuia matumizi ya tumbaku, hasa miongoni mwa vijana na watu wa kipato cha chini. Ongezeko la ushuru ambalo linaongeza bei ya tumbaku kwa 10% hupunguza matumizi ya tumbaku kwa takriban 4% katika nchi zenye mapato ya juu na karibu 5% katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati. Jibu la WHO: Kiwango cha janga hili la kibinadamu na kiuchumi ni la kushangaza, lakini pia linaweza kuzuilika.
translations.reference: However, it is too early to provide a clear answer on the long-term impact of HTPs and/or e-cigarette use. Impact: An estimated 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, 80% of whom are in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by diverting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This spending behaviour is difficult to curb because tobacco is so addictive. It also causes premature death and disability of productive age adults in households thus leading to reduced household income and increased healthcare costs. In addition to the detrimental impact of tobacco on health, the total economic cost of smoking (from health expenditures and productivity losses together) are estimated to be around US$ 1.4 trillion per year, equivalent in magnitude to 1.8% of the world's annual gross domestic product (GDP). Almost 40% of this cost occurred in developing countries, highlighting the substantial burden these countries suffer. Tobacco taxes are applied in part to combat this effect and are seen as the most cost-effective way of curbing tobacco use, particularly among youth and low-income populations. A tax increase that increases tobacco prices by 10% decreases tobacco consumption by about 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries. WHO response: The scale of this human and economic tragedy is shocking, but it’s also preventable.
translations.hypothesis: However, it is premature to provide a definitive answer regarding the long-term effects of HTPs and/or e-cigarettes. Effects: It is estimated that over 1.3 billion people worldwide use tobacco products, with 80% of them in low- and middle-income countries. Tobacco use contributes to poverty by shifting household spending from basic needs, such as food and shelter, to tobacco. This pattern of consumption is difficult to control because tobacco is highly addictive. It also leads to premature death and disability among working-age adults in these households, resulting in reduced household income and increased healthcare costs. In addition to the health effects of tobacco, the overall economic costs of smoking are estimated to be around $1.4 trillion per year, or 1.8% of global GDP. Approximately 40% of these costs are incurred in developing countries, highlighting the significant burden these countries face. Tobacco taxes are used to some extent to address this issue, and they appear to be a more cost-effective way to discourage tobacco use, especially among young people and low-income populations. An increase in tobacco taxes, such as a 10% increase, has been shown to reduce tobacco use by approximately 4% in high-income countries and about 5% in low- and middle-income countries.

translations.source: Tumbaku Kubwa - pamoja na watengenezaji wote wa bidhaa za tumbaku - inapigania kuhakikisha hatari za bidhaa zao zinafichwa, lakini tunapambana: Mnamo 2003, Nchi Wanachama wa WHO zilipitisha kwa kauli moja Mkataba wa Mfumo wa WHO wa Kudhibiti Tumbaku (WHO FCTC), mkataba pekee wa afya ya umma chini ya udhamini wa WHO. Imetumika tangu 2005, kwa sasa ina Vyama 181. Ili kusaidia nchi kutekeleza WHO FCTC, WHO ilianzisha MPOWER, kifurushi cha hatua za kiufundi na rasilimali, ambayo kila moja inalingana na angalau kifungu kimoja cha WHO FCTC. MPOWER inajenga uwezo wa nchi kutekeleza hatua 6 za kupunguza mahitaji ya bidhaa za tumbaku: kufuatilia matumizi ya tumbaku na sera za kuzuia, kulinda watu dhidi ya matumizi ya tumbaku, kutoa msaada wa kuacha matumizi ya tumbaku, kuonya juu ya hatari ya tumbaku, kutekeleza marufuku ya utangazaji wa tumbaku kukuza na kufadhili na kuongeza kodi kwenye tumbaku.
translations.reference: Big Tobacco — along with all manufacturers of tobacco products — is fighting to ensure the dangers of their products are concealed, but we are fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States unanimously adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only public health treaty under the auspices of WHO. In force since 2005, it has currently 181 Parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO introduced MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one provision of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER builds the capacity of countries to implement 6 measures to reduce the demand for tobacco products: monitor tobacco use and prevention policies, protect people from tobacco use, offer help to quit tobacco use, warn about the dangers of tobacco, enforce bans on tobacco advertising, promotion and sponsorship and raise taxes on tobacco.
translations.hypothesis: The Tobacco Industry – along with all tobacco product manufacturers – has historically fought to conceal the dangers of their products, but we are now fighting back: In 2003, WHO Member States adopted the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (WHO FCTC), the only global health treaty endorsed by the WHO. It has been in force since 2005, and now has 181 parties. To help countries implement the WHO FCTC, WHO has launched MPOWER, a package of technical measures and resources, each of which corresponds to at least one article of the WHO FCTC. MPOWER helps countries implement six measures to reduce tobacco demand: monitoring tobacco use and prevention policies, protecting people from tobacco smoke, providing tobacco control support, warning about the dangers of tobacco, implementing tobacco advertising bans, and increasing tobacco taxes.

translations.source: Trakoma Muhtasari: Trakoma ni ugonjwa wa macho na unaongoza kwa kusababisha upofu duniani kote. Inasababishwa na bakteria ya lazima ndani ya seli inayoitwa Chlamydia trachomatis. Maambukizi hupitishwa kutoka kwa mtu hadi kwa mtu kwa uhamisho wa moja kwa moja au usio wa moja kwa moja wa kutokwa kwa macho na pua ya watu walioambukizwa; uhamisho usio wa moja kwa moja unajumuisha kubeba kwenye mwili wa aina za nzi. Watoto wa umri wa shule ya mapema huhifadhi hifadhi kuu ya maambukizi. Mifano zinaeleza kuwa mtu anahitaji zaidi ya maambukizi 150 kwa maisha yote ili kuendeleza matatizo ya upofu ya trakoma. Trakoma imeenea katika baadhi ya watu maskini zaidi duniani, ambao wanaishi vijijini na maeneo ya mbali na hawana upatikanaji wa kutosha wa maji, usafi wa mazingira na huduma za afya. Afrika ndilo bara lililoathiriwa zaidi, lakini pia lina juhudi kubwa zaidi za kudhibiti. Ugonjwa huo pia hupatikana Amerika ya Kati na Kusini, Asia, Australia, na Mashariki ya Kati. Kufikia 2 Januari 2020, nchi 13 zilikuwa zimeripoti kufikia malengo ya kuondoa.
translations.reference: Trachoma Overview: Trachoma is a disease of the eye and the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. It is caused by an obligate intracellular bacterium called Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person by direct or indirect transfer of ocular and nasal discharges of infected people; indirect transfer includes carriage on the body of species of flies. Preschool-age children harbour the principal reservoir of infection. Models suggest that an individual requires more than 150 lifetime infections to develop the blinding complications of trachoma. Trachoma is endemic in some of the world’s poorest populations, who live in rural and remote areas and have highly inadequate access to water, sanitation and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but it also has the most widespread control efforts. The disease is also found in Central and South America, Asia, Australia, and the Middle East. As of 2 January 2020, 13 countries had reported achieving elimination goals.
translations.hypothesis: Trakoma Summary: Trakoma is a leading cause of preventable blindness worldwide. It is caused by bacteria called Chlamydia trachomatis, which infects the eye. The infection is spread from person to person through direct or indirect contact with the infected eyes or throat; indirect contact includes contact with contaminated surfaces or insects. Children in early primary school are particularly vulnerable. Studies have shown that a person needs to be infected with more than 150 trachoma bacteria to develop trachoma-related blindness. Trakoma is prevalent in some of the poorest communities in the world, where people live in poverty and have limited access to clean water, sanitation, and healthcare. Africa is the most affected continent, but also has the most successful control programs. The disease is also found in the Middle East, Central and South America, Asia, and Australia. As of January 2, 2020, 13 countries had reached their elimination goals.

translations.source: Katika mwaka wa 2019 pekee, watu 92 622 walipata matibabu ya upasuaji kwa trakoma ya hali ya juu na milioni 95.2 walitibiwa kwa viua vijasumu. Licha ya mafanikio hayo, ugonjwa huu bado ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya jamii, huku kukiwa na wastani wa hasara ya dola za Marekani bilioni 8 kwa mwaka katika uzalishaji kutokana na upofu na ulemavu wa macho. Dalili: Maambukizi yanahusishwa na mabadiliko ya uchochezi katika kiwambo cha sikio inayojulikana kama "trakoma hai". Kinga ya mtu binafsi inaweza kufuta sehemu moja ya maambukizi, lakini katika jumuiya za endemic, kupatikana tena kwa viumbe hutokea mara kwa mara. Baada ya miaka ya matukio ya mara kwa mara ya trakoma hai, ndani ya kope inaweza kuwa na kovu kali sana (kovu la trachomatous conjunctival scarring) kwamba hugeuka ndani na kusababisha kope kusugua kwenye mboni ya jicho (trachomatous trichiasis), na kusababisha maumivu ya mara kwa mara na kutovumilia mwanga; mabadiliko haya na mengine ya jicho yanaweza kusababisha kovu ya konea. Ikiachwa bila kutibiwa, hali hii husababisha kuundwa kwa opacities isiyoweza kutenduliwa, na kusababisha uharibifu wa kuona au upofu. Umri ambao hii hutokea inategemea mambo kadhaa ikiwa ni pamoja na ukubwa wa maambukizi ya ndani. Katika jamii zenye magonjwa mengi, inaweza kutokea utotoni, ingawa mwanzo wa ulemavu wa kuona kati ya umri wa miaka 30 na 40 ni kawaida zaidi. Wanawake hupofushwa hadi mara nne zaidi ya wanaume, labda kutokana na kuwasiliana mara kwa mara na watoto walioambukizwa. Watu wanaoishi katika jamii maskini zaidi na za mbali zaidi za Afrika, Asia, Australia, Mashariki ya Kati, Amerika ya Kati na Kusini na Visiwa vya Pasifiki wako hatarini.
translations.reference: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea. Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.hypothesis: In 2019 alone, 92,622 people received surgery for trachoma, and 95.2 million were treated for the condition. Despite these successes, this disease remains a major public health problem, resulting in an average annual loss of $8 billion in productivity due to blindness and disability. Symptoms: The infection is associated with inflammation in the conjunctiva, known as "trachoma." While an individual may be able to clear the infection, re-infection is common in endemic areas. After repeated episodes of trachoma, the conjunctiva can become severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring), leading to inward turning of the eyelashes (trachomatous trichiasis), which causes chronic pain and light sensitivity; other eye changes can also lead to corneal damage. If left untreated, this condition can lead to irreversible opacities, resulting in vision loss or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors, including the severity of the infection. In endemic areas, it can occur in childhood, although onset of blindness between the ages of 30 and 40 is more common. Women are affected four times more often than men, possibly due to increased contact with infected children. People living in the poorest and most remote communities in Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America, and the Pacific Islands are at particular risk.

translations.source: Matibabu: Trakoma inaweza kuzuiwa kwa kupunguza mambo hatarishi ya kimazingira. Haya ni pamoja na ukosefu wa usafi wa mazingira, kaya zenye msongamano wa watu, ukosefu wa maji na uhaba wa upatikanaji na matumizi ya vyoo. Maambukizi kwa kawaida huanza katika umri mdogo na kukua chini ya mara kwa mara kwa muda mfupi na kuongezeka kwa umri. Maambukizi mara nyingi hupatikana kwa njia ya familia na kuwasiliana na wengine wenye ugonjwa hai. Maambukizi ya kujirudia husababisha dalili mbaya zaidi za ugonjwa huo. Trakoma inaweza kuondolewa kama tatizo la afya ya umma kwa kutumia mkakati SALAMA, unaojumuisha: Upasuaji wa kutibu trichiasis ya trachomatous, hatua ya upofu ya ugonjwa huo; Dawa za antibiotiki za kuondoa maambukizi, hasa usimamizi wa dawa nyingi za antiniotiki azithromycin, ambayo hutolewa na mtengenezaji kwa programu za kitaifa kupitia Mpango wa Kimataifa wa Trakoma; usafi wa uso; na Uboreshaji wa mazingira, hasa upatikanaji bora wa maji na usafi wa mazingira.
translations.reference: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease. Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.hypothesis: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by reducing environmental risk factors. These include poor sanitation, crowded living conditions, lack of access to clean water, and inadequate hygiene practices. Infections typically begin in childhood and progress over time, with severity increasing with age. Infections are often spread through family contact and close association with other infected individuals. Recurrent infections lead to more severe symptoms. Trachoma can be managed as a public health problem through the use of a strategy called SALABA, which includes: surgical treatment of trichiasis (inversion of the eyelashes); antibiotic treatment to eliminate the infection, particularly the use of azithromycin, which is provided by manufacturers through national programs under the International Trachoma Programme; facial hygiene; and environmental improvement, particularly access to clean water and improved sanitation.

translations.source: WHO ilipitisha mkakati wa SALAMA mnamo 1993 na inaendelea katika jukumu lake la kutoa uongozi na uratibu wa juhudi za kimataifa za kuondoa trakoma kama shida ya afya ya umma.
translations.reference: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.hypothesis: In 1993, WHO adopted the SALAMA strategy and continues to play a leading role in coordinating international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.

translations.source: Dawa ya asili, Nyongeza na Shirikishi Muhtasari: Dawa ya jadi Dawa ya jadi ina historia ndefu. Ni jumla ya maarifa, ustadi, na mazoea kulingana na nadharia, imani, na uzoefu wa asili wa tamaduni tofauti, iwe ya kuelezeka au la, inayotumika katika kudumisha afya na vile vile katika kuzuia, utambuzi, uboreshaji au matibabu ya magonjwa ya kimwili na kiakili. Dawa ya ziada Maneno "dawa ya ziada" au "dawa mbadala" yanarejelea seti pana ya mazoea ya utunzaji wa afya ambayo si sehemu ya mila ya nchi hiyo au tiba ya kawaida na haijajumuishwa kikamilifu katika mfumo mkuu wa huduma ya afya. Zinatumika kwa kubadilishana na dawa za jadi katika nchi zingine. Dawa za mitishamba Dawa za mitishamba ni pamoja na mimea, mimea, maandalizi ya mitishamba na bidhaa za mitishamba zilizomalizika, ambazo zina viungo hai vya sehemu za mimea, au vitu vingine vya mimea, au mchanganyiko. Athari: Mkakati wa Tiba Asilia wa WHO 2014–2023 ulitayarishwa na kuzinduliwa kwa kuitikia azimio la Bunge la Afya Ulimwenguni kuhusu tiba asilia (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.hypothesis: Traditional Medicine, Complementary Medicine, and Herbal Medicine: Traditional medicine has a long history. It encompasses a wide range of knowledge, skills, and practices used in various cultures to maintain health and to prevent, manage, or treat illnesses. Complementary medicine refers to a broad range of health care practices that are not part of mainstream medicine but are used alongside conventional treatments. Herbal medicine includes plants, herbs, herbal preparations, and herbal products that contain biologically active compounds from plants or other plant sources.

translations.source: Mkakati huo unalenga kusaidia Nchi Wanachama katika kuunda sera tendaji na kutekeleza mipango ya utekelezaji ambayo itaimarisha jukumu la tiba asilia katika kuweka idadi ya watu kuwa na afya bora. Kushughulikia changamoto, kukabiliana na mahitaji yaliyoainishwa na Nchi Wanachama na kuendeleza kazi iliyofanywa chini ya mkakati wa tiba asilia wa WHO: 2002-2005, mkakati uliosasishwa wa kipindi cha 2014-2023 unatoa kipaumbele zaidi kuliko mtangulizi wake katika kuweka kipaumbele huduma za afya na mifumo, ikiwa ni pamoja na bidhaa za tiba asilia na nyongeza, mazoea na watendaji. Malengo ya kimkakati ni: Kujenga msingi wa maarifa kwa usimamizi hai wa T&CM kupitia sera zinazofaa za kitaifa; Kuimarisha uhakikisho wa ubora, usalama, matumizi sahihi na ufanisi wa T&CM kwa kudhibiti bidhaa, mazoea na watendaji; Kukuza huduma ya afya kwa wote kwa kuunganisha huduma za T&CM katika utoaji wa huduma za afya na kujihudumia. Jibu la WHO: Ripoti ya kimataifa ya WHO kuhusu tiba asilia na nyongeza ya 2019 ilitengenezwa ili kushughulikia pengo la data za kuaminika, zinazoaminika na rasmi kutoka kwa Nchi Wanachama katika eneo la T&CM. Ripoti hii hukagua maendeleo ya kimataifa katika T&CM katika kipindi cha miongo miwili iliyopita na inategemea michango kutoka Nchi 179 Wanachama wa WHO. Inatoa taarifa muhimu kwa watunga sera, wataalamu wa afya na umma kwa kutumia mchango unaowezekana wa T&CM kwa afya na ustawi.
translations.reference: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners; To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.hypothesis: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing and implementing policies and programs that strengthen the role of traditional medicine in promoting better health for the population. Addressing challenges, meeting the needs of Member States, and building on the work done under the WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2002-2005, the updated strategy for the 2014-2023 period places greater emphasis on health services and systems, including traditional medicine products, practices, and practitioners. Strategic goals include: Establishing a foundation for evidence-based management of traditional medicine through appropriate national policies; Strengthening the quality, safety, appropriate use, and efficiency of traditional medicine by regulating products, practices, and practitioners; Promoting health for all by integrating traditional medicine into health services and self-care. The WHO response: The WHO Global Report on Traditional Medicine and Complementary Medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible, and official data from Member States in the field of traditional medicine. The report examines international developments in traditional medicine over the past two decades and draws on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policymakers, healthcare professionals, and the public on the potential contribution of traditional medicine to health and well-being.

translations.source: Kupandikiza Muhtasari: Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo Kupandikiza seli za binadamu, tishu au viungo huokoa maisha ya watu wengi na kurejesha kazi muhimu ambapo hakuna njia mbadala za ufanisi kulinganishwa zilizopo. Katika miaka 50, upandikizaji umekuwa jambo la mafanikio duniani kote. Hata hivyo, kuna tofauti kubwa kati ya nchi katika upatikanaji wa upandikizaji unaofaa na katika kiwango cha usalama, ubora, ufanisi wa mchango na upandikizaji wa seli za binadamu, tishu na viungo. Vipengele vya maadili vya upandikizaji viko mbele. Hasa, mahitaji ya wagonjwa ambayo hayajafikiwa na uhaba wa vipandikizi husababisha jaribu la usafirishaji wa viungo vya mwili wa binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji. Kupandikiza binadamu Seli za binadamu na tishu za kupandikiza zinaweza kuokoa maisha au kurejesha kazi muhimu. Kwa mfano pandikizi la corneal linaweza kurejesha kuona kwa upofu wa corneal;
translations.reference: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs. The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
translations.hypothesis: Organ Transplantation: The transplantation of human cells, tissues, and organs is a life-saving procedure that restores function where there are no effective alternative treatments. Over the past 50 years, organ transplantation has become a major success story worldwide. However, there are significant differences between countries in terms of access to suitable organs and the standards of safety, quality, and efficiency of organ transplantation. Ethical considerations are also at the forefront. In particular, the shortage of organs leads to the practice of using human cadaver organs for transplantation. Human cell and tissue transplantation can save lives or restore function. For example, a corneal transplant can restore vision in corneal blindness;

translations.source: upandikizaji wa seli za shina za hematopoietic unaweza kuponya magonjwa ya kuzaliwa au kupatikana ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya leukemia. Tazama habari zaidi juu ya Seli za Shina za Hematopoeitic upandikizaji wa vali ya moyo wa binadamu mara nyingi hujumuisha hali bora ya uingizwaji na wagonjwa wanaopokea hawahitaji tiba ya muda mrefu ya kuzuia mgando. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, mnyama kwa binadamu, inayofafanuliwa kama chembe hai, tishu au viungo vya asili ya wanyama na maji maji ya mwili wa binadamu, seli, tishu au viungo ambavyo vina ex vivo na nyenzo hizi hai, xenogeneic, ina uwezo wa kuunda nyenzo mbadala ya asili ya mwanadamu na kuziba upungufu katika nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya upandikizaji. Uchangiaji: Michango kutoka kwa watu walioaga dunia inapaswa kuendelezwa kwa uwezo wao wa juu zaidi wa kimatibabu ili kuepuka, inapowezekana, hatari za asili kwa wafadhili hai, kama ilivyoelezwa katika Kanuni ya 3 ya Mwongozo wa WHO. Kuna hitaji linalotambulika kwa jamii, na wataalamu wa afya, kuelimishwa vyema kuhusu uchangiaji na upandikizaji na huo ndio ufunguo wa mafanikio ya mipango ya uchangiaji wa marehemu. Hata hivyo, licha ya matumizi ya mara kwa mara ya vifaa vinavyotolewa na wafadhili waliofariki, michango kutoka kwa wafadhili wanaoishi ni muhimu kwa baadhi ya aina za upandikizaji au kufidia ugavi mdogo wa nyenzo zinazopatikana kutoka kwa wafadhili waliofariki ili kukidhi mahitaji ya mgonjwa. Utoaji hai unatekelezwa licha ya ukweli kwamba unahusisha hatari kwa wafadhili ambazo haziwezi kupuuzwa. Kwa vile ununuzi wa nyenzo za binadamu kwa ajili ya kupandikiza kutoka kwa wafadhili waliokufa au wanaoishi na upandikizaji wa alojeneki unaofuata unaweza kuhusisha hatari za kimaadili na kiusalama kwa mpokeaji na mfadhili, udhibiti mkali na uangalizi unaofaa unafaa kutekelezwa na mamlaka za afya ili kuwalinda.
translations.reference: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation. Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.

translations.source: Kanuni Elekezi huamuru utunzaji bora kwa wafadhili na wapokeaji. Uangalizi wa uwazi wa mamlaka za afya juu ya shughuli za mchango na upandikizaji pia ni muhimu ili kuongeza imani ya umma katika mfumo. Kwa kuongezea, uamuzi wa kuwa mfadhili mara nyingi hutegemea ufahamu kwamba mchango katika upatikanaji wa rasilimali za upandikizaji unaweza kufaidisha mahitaji ya afya ya familia ya mfadhili siku moja. Ikiwa tumejiandaa kupokea upandikizaji tukihitaji, basi tunapaswa kuwa tayari kutoa Maadili: Kanuni Mwongozo wa WHO kuhusu Seli ya Binadamu, Tishu na Upandikizaji wa Kiungo Mnamo 1987 Mkutano wa Arobaini wa Afya Ulimwenguni, unaohusika na biashara ya faida katika viungo vya binadamu, ulianzisha utayarishaji wa Kanuni za Kwanza za Mwongozo wa WHO juu ya Uhamishaji, iliyoidhinishwa na Bunge mnamo 1991 katika azimio la WHA44.25. Kanuni hizi Elekezi zimeathiri pakubwa kanuni na taratibu za kitaaluma pamoja na sheria kote ulimwenguni takriban miongo miwili. Baada ya mchakato wa mashauriano uliochukua miaka kadhaa, Bunge la Sitini na tatu la Afya Duniani lilipitisha azimio WHA63.22 tarehe 21 Mei 2010, likiidhinisha Kanuni za Mwongozo wa WHO zilizosasishwa na kubainisha maeneo ya maendeleo ili kuboresha uchangiaji na upandikizaji. Azimio la Istanbul Wataalamu wa mchango na upandikizaji kutoka mikoa yote, kupitia mashirika na taasisi zao nyingi, wanaidhinisha Azimio la Istanbul kuhusu usafirishaji haramu wa viungo na utalii wa kupandikiza ulioendelezwa chini ya uongozi wa Jumuiya ya Upandikizaji na Jumuiya ya Kimataifa ya Nephrology mnamo Mei 2008.
translations.reference: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.hypothesis: The Elekezi Principles mandate the highest standards of care for both donors and recipients. Transparency in the activities of health authorities regarding donation and transplantation is also crucial to build public trust in the system. Furthermore, the decision to donate often relies on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources can benefit the donor's family's health needs in the future.
