If we are prepared to receive a transplant when needed, we should also be prepared to adhere to the following principles: The Elekezi Principles are based on the WHO Guidelines on Human Cells, Tissues and Transplantation, adopted in 1987 at the Forty-fourth World Health Assembly. This assembly, which dealt with the commercial aspects of human organs, established the first WHO Guidelines on Transplantation, which were endorsed by the United Nations General Assembly in resolution WHA44.25. These Elekezi Principles have had a significant impact on professional and legal standards worldwide over the past two decades. After a lengthy consultation process, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the revised WHO Guidelines and identifying areas for improvement in donation and transplantation. The Istanbul Declaration, adopted by professionals in donation and transplantation from all regions, through their various organizations and institutions, at the Istanbul Declaration on the illicit trafficking of human organs and the unethical practice of organ tourism, organized by the International Transplantation and Nephrology Associations in May 2008.

translations.source: Usafiri na afya Muhtasari: Usafiri wa kimataifa unaweza kusababisha hatari mbalimbali kwa afya, kulingana na sifa za msafiri na wasafiri. Wasafiri wanaweza kukutana na mabadiliko ya ghafla na makubwa katika urefu, unyevu, vijidudu, na joto, ambayo inaweza kusababisha afya mbaya. Aidha, hatari kubwa za kiafya zinaweza kutokea katika maeneo ambayo malazi hayana ubora, usafi na usafi wa mazingira hautoshi, huduma za matibabu hazijaendelezwa vizuri na maji safi hayapatikani. Usafiri wa kimataifa unaongezeka, kwani mamilioni ya watu husafiri kwa madhumuni ya kitaaluma, kijamii, burudani na kibinadamu kila mwaka. Wasafiri wote lazima wajitayarishe kwa aina mbalimbali za hatari za kiafya wanazoweza kukabiliwa nazo katika mazingira yasiyofahamika kabla, wakati na baada ya kusafiri. Watu wanaopanga kusafiri wanapaswa kutafuta ushauri kuhusu hatari zinazoweza kutokea katika maeneo waliyochagua na kuelewa jinsi bora ya kulinda afya zao na kupunguza hatari ya kupata magonjwa. Kupanga mbele, hatua zinazofaa za kuzuia na tahadhari makini zinaweza kulinda afya ya wasafiri, na kupunguza hatari za ajali na kupata magonjwa. Ingawa taaluma ya matibabu na sekta ya usafiri inaweza kutoa usaidizi wa kina na ushauri mzuri, inasalia kuwa jukumu la msafiri kutafuta maelezo, kuelewa hatari zinazohusika na kuchukua tahadhari zinazohitajika ili kulinda afya zao wakati wa kusafiri. Hatari: Wakati wa kubainisha hatari za kiafya za usafiri wa kimataifa, wasafiri na wataalamu wa afya wanapaswa kuzingatia mambo muhimu yafuatayo:
translations.reference: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year. All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.hypothesis: Travel and Health Summary: International travel can pose various health risks, depending on the traveler's characteristics and the destination. Travelers may experience sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, insects, and temperature, which can lead to health problems. Furthermore, significant health risks can occur in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, sanitation and environmental hygiene are inadequate, healthcare services are underdeveloped, and clean water is not readily available. International travel is increasing, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational, and humanitarian purposes each year. All travelers should be prepared for the various health risks they may encounter in unfamiliar environments, both before and after travel. People planning to travel should seek advice about the potential risks in the chosen destinations and understand how to best protect their health and minimize the risk of illness. Proactive planning, appropriate preventive measures, and careful attention can protect travelers' health and reduce the risk of accidents and illness. While the medical profession and the travel industry can provide valuable information and advice, it is ultimately the traveler's responsibility to seek out information, understand the potential risks, and take appropriate precautions to protect their health during travel. Risks: When identifying the health risks of international travel, travelers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:

translations.source: mfumo wa usafiri Mwisho(hatima) muda na msimu wa safari madhumuni ya kusafiri viwango vya malazi, usafi wa chakula na usafi wa mazingira tabia ya msafiri afya ya msingi ya msafiri Wasafiri wanaweza kukumbana na hatari zifuatazo za kiafya kulingana na hatima yao: kuumwa na wanyama na wadudu wakiwemo mbu, kupe, viroboto na baadhi ya nzi ambao wanaweza kueneza magonjwa kama malaria. yatokanayo na damu au maji maji mengine ya mwili
translations.reference: mode of transport destination(s) duration and season of travel purpose of travel standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation behaviour of the traveller underlying health of the traveller Travellers may encounter the following health risks depending on their destination: animal and insect bites, including mosquitoes, ticks, fleas, and some flies, which can spread diseases such as malaria exposure to blood or other bodily fluids
translations.hypothesis: Transportation System: Final destination, travel time and season, purpose of travel, accommodation standards, food hygiene, and environmental cleanliness, traveler behavior, basic traveler health, and potential health risks based on the destination: insect bites (mosquitoes, flies, ticks, and other insects that can transmit diseases like malaria), waterborne illnesses, and other bodily fluids.

translations.source: mabadiliko ya mazingira, kama vile urefu, unyevu, joto magonjwa yatokanayo na chakula na maji huduma duni za matibabu majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na ajali za barabarani au shughuli za burudani afya ya kisaikolojia, pamoja na mafadhaiko na mshtuko wa kitamaduni Wasafiri wanaweza pia kukabiliwa na magonjwa kadhaa ya kuambukiza, na mengi ya haya yanaweza kuzuiwa kwa chanjo. Hata hivyo, kuna baadhi ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, ikiwa ni pamoja na baadhi ya hatari zaidi, ambayo hakuna chanjo zilizopo. Maeneo ambayo malazi, usafi na usafi wa mazingira, huduma ya matibabu na ubora wa maji ni wa kiwango cha juu husababisha hatari chache kwa afya ya wasafiri. Pale ambapo huduma hizi hazitolewi, wasafiri wanapaswa kuchukua tahadhari kali ili kuepuka maradhi. Maonyo ya usafiri kutoka kwa vyanzo vya serikali yanapaswa kuchukuliwa kwa uzito.
translations.reference: environmental changes, such as altitude, humidity, temperature foodborne and waterborne diseases inadequate medical services injuries, including from road traffic accidents or recreational activities psychological health, including stress and culture shock Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.hypothesis: Environmental changes, such as altitude, humidity, and temperature, can pose health risks. These include illnesses related to food and water, as well as inadequate medical care, injuries from accidents on roads or during recreational activities, and psychological health issues, such as stress and culture shock. Travelers may also be exposed to various infectious diseases, many of which can be prevented through vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most serious, for which there are no available vaccines. Areas where accommodation, sanitation, and environmental hygiene, medical services, and water quality are of high standard pose a lower risk to travelers' health. When these services are not available, travelers should take extra precautions to avoid illness. Travel advisories from government sources should be taken seriously.

translations.source: Kinga: Kabla ya kusafiri: wasiliana na kituo cha dawa za usafiri au daktari, ikiwezekana wiki 4-8 kabla ya kuondoka ili kupokea chanjo zinazohitajika; soma kuhusu magonjwa ya eneo lako yanayohusiana na unakoenda; omba taarifa kuhusu hatari ya malaria na uzuiaji wa kuumwa na mbu. Hii inaweza kujumuisha kununua dawa zinazofaa za kuzuia na kufunga chandarua na dawa ya kufukuza wadudu; pata uchunguzi wa matibabu na meno; kupata maagizo ya dawa kulingana na urefu wa kukaa na kukusanya kit cha matibabu kinachofaa; nunua bima ya matibabu na bima inayofaa nje ya nchi Wakati wa kusafiri: kula tu chakula kilichopikwa vizuri na kunywa tu vinywaji baridi vya chupa au vifungashio. Chemsha maji ya kunywa ikiwa usalama ni wa shaka.
translations.reference: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up; obtain prescriptions for medication according to length of stay and assemble a suitable medical kit; purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad During travel: eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks. Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.
translations.hypothesis: Preparation: Before traveling: contact a travel clinic or doctor, ideally 4-8 weeks before departure, to receive necessary vaccinations; research the diseases prevalent in the area you are visiting; obtain information about the risk of malaria and how to prevent mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate mosquito repellent and insecticide; get a medical and dental check-up; obtain prescriptions for any necessary medications based on the length of your stay and gather an appropriate medical kit; purchase travel medical and international health insurance. During travel: eat only well-cooked food and drink only bottled or sealed beverages. Boil drinking water if there is any doubt about its safety.

translations.source: kuwa na ufahamu wa ajali au matatizo yanayohusiana na trafiki, wanyama, mizio, jua na michezo; kubeba kadi inayoonyesha kundi lako la damu; kuvaa bangili ya tahadhari ya matibabu, ikiwa una mzio au ugonjwa sugu. Wasafiri wanapaswa kufanyiwa uchunguzi wa kimatibabu wanaporudi ikiwa: kurudi na homa kutoka nchi ambako malaria iko au inaweza kuwepo kuteseka na ugonjwa sugu hupata ugonjwa kwa wiki zinazofuata baada ya kurudi nyumbani alipata matibabu ya malaria akiwa safarini inaweza kuwa wazi kwa ugonjwa mbaya wa kuambukiza alitumia zaidi ya miezi 3 katika nchi inayoendelea
translations.reference: be aware of accidents or problems related to traffic, animals, allergies, sun and sport; carry a card showing your blood group; wear a medical alert bracelet, if you have allergies or a chronic disease Travellers should obtain a medical examination on their return if they: return with a fever from a country where malaria is or may be present suffer from a chronic disease experience illness in the weeks following their return home received treatment for malaria while travelling may have been exposed to a serious infectious disease spent more than 3 months in a developing country
translations.hypothesis: Be aware of potential accidents or health issues related to traffic, animals, weather, and sports; carry a card indicating your blood type; wear a medical alert bracelet if you have allergies or a chronic condition. Travelers should undergo a medical examination upon return if they: have returned from a country where malaria is present or may be present; have a chronic condition that is likely to be exacerbated in the weeks following their return; have received malaria treatment while traveling; or have spent more than 3 months in a developing country.

translations.source: Kifua kikuu Muhtasari: Kifua kikuu husababishwa na bakteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) na mara nyingi huathiri mapafu. Kifua kikuu huenezwa kwa njia ya hewa wakati watu wenye TB ya mapafu wanapokohoa, kupiga chafya au kutema mate. Mtu anahitaji kuvuta vijidudu vichache tu ili kuambukizwa. Kila mwaka, watu milioni 10 wanaugua kifua kikuu (TB). Licha ya kuwa ni ugonjwa unaozuilika na unaotibika, watu milioni 1.5 hufa kutokana na TB kila mwaka - na kuifanya kuwa muuaji mkuu wa kuambukiza duniani. Kifua kikuu ndicho kisababishi kikuu cha vifo vya watu walio na VVU na pia mchangiaji mkubwa katika ukinzani wa viua viini Watu wengi wanaougua TB wanaishi katika nchi zenye kipato cha chini na cha kati, lakini TB ipo duniani kote. Takriban nusu ya watu wote walio na TB wanaweza kupatikana katika nchi 8: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Ufilipino na Afrika Kusini. Takriban robo ya idadi ya watu duniani inakadiriwa kuwa wameambukizwa bakteria wa TB, lakini watu wengi hawataendelea kupata ugonjwa wa TB na wengine wataondoa maambukizi.
translations.reference: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB). Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.hypothesis: Executive Summary: Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with pulmonary TB cough, sneeze, or spit. Only one in ten people need to be exposed to the bacteria to become infected. Every year, 10 million people contract TB. Despite being a curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the leading cause of infectious disease deaths worldwide. TB is a major killer of people with HIV and also a significant contributor to mortality among people living with HIV. Many people with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is a global problem. Approximately half of all people with TB are in eight countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, the Philippines, and South Africa. An estimated one-third of the world's population has been infected with TB bacteria, but many do not develop the disease, and some are never aware that they have been infected.

translations.source: Wale ambao wameambukizwa lakini sio (bado) wagonjwa na ugonjwa huo hawawezi kuusambaza. Watu walioambukizwa na bakteria wa TB wana hatari ya maisha ya 5-10% ya kuugua TB. Wale walio na kinga dhaifu, kama vile watu wanaoishi na VVU, utapiamlo au kisukari, au watu wanaotumia tumbaku, wana hatari kubwa ya kuugua. Dalili: Dalili za kawaida za ugonjwa wa TB ni pamoja na: Kikohozi cha muda mrefu, maumivu ya kifua, udhaifu au uchovu, kupungua uzito, homa, jasho la usiku. Mara nyingi, dalili hizi zitakuwa nyepesi kwa miezi mingi, hivyo kusababisha kuchelewa kutafuta huduma na kuongeza hatari ya kueneza maambukizi kwa wengine. Mtoa huduma wa afya akishuku mgonjwa kuwa na ugonjwa wa TB, atampeleka mgonjwa kupima. Katika kesi ya ugonjwa unaoshukiwa wa TB ya mapafu, wagonjwa wataombwa kutoa sampuli ya makohozi kwa ajili ya kupima bakteria wa TB. Kwa ugonjwa wa TB usio wa mapafu, sampuli za maji na tishu zilizoathirika za mwili zinaweza kupimwa. WHO inapendekeza vipimo vya haraka vya uchunguzi wa molekuli kama vipimo vya awali kwa watu wanaoonyesha dalili na dalili za TB. Zana nyingine za uchunguzi zinaweza kujumuisha hadubini ya smear ya sputum na X-rays ya kifua.
translations.reference: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others. If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.hypothesis: Individuals who have been infected but are not yet ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5-10% chance of developing the disease. Individuals with weakened immune systems, such as those with HIV, diabetes, or those who smoke, are at a higher risk of developing the disease. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB include: persistent cough, chest pain, fatigue or weakness, weight loss, fever, and night sweats. Often, these symptoms are mild for months, leading to delayed diagnosis and an increased risk of spreading the infection to others. If a healthcare provider suspects a patient has TB, they will refer the patient for testing. In the case of suspected pulmonary TB, patients will be asked to provide a sputum sample for testing. For extra-pulmonary TB, samples of fluid and tissue from the affected part of the body may be tested. The WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as a first-line test for individuals with TB symptoms. Other diagnostic tests may include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.

translations.source: Kwa maambukizi ya TB, mtu huambukizwa na bakteria ya TB ambayo haifanyi kazi katika mwili. Maambukizi haya yanaweza kukua na kuwa ugonjwa wa TB ikiwa mfumo wao wa kinga utadhoofika. Watu wenye maambukizi ya TB hawaonyeshi dalili zozote za TB. Ili kutambua maambukizi ya TB, watoa huduma za afya watachunguza wagonjwa walio katika hatari ili kudhibiti TB hai, na wanaweza kutumia kipimo cha ngozi au damu ili kuangalia maambukizi ya TB. Matibabu: Ugonjwa wa TB unatibika. Inatibiwa kwa kozi ya kawaida ya miezi 6 ya antibiotiki 4. Dawa za kawaida ni pamoja na rifampicin na isoniazid. Katika baadhi ya matukio, bakteria ya TB haijibu dawa za kawaida. Katika kesi hii, mgonjwa ana TB sugu ya dawa. kwa TB sugu ya dawa ni ya mda mrefu na ngumu zaidi.
translations.reference: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable. It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.hypothesis: For individuals infected with TB, they are exposed to TB bacteria that do not function properly in the body. These infections can grow and develop into active TB disease if the body's immune system is weakened. People infected with TB do not show any symptoms of the disease. To detect TB infections, healthcare providers will screen high-risk individuals for latent TB, and may use a skin or blood test to check for active infections. Treatment: TB is curable. It is treated with a standard course of 6 months of four antibiotics. Common medications include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases, TB bacteria do not respond to standard medications. In these cases, the patient has drug-resistant TB. Drug-resistant TB is a more serious and difficult-to-treat form of the disease.

translations.source: Muda wa dawa za TB hutolewa kwa mgonjwa kwa taarifa, usimamizi na usaidizi na mfanyakazi wa afya au mtu wa kujitolea aliyefunzwa. Bila msaada kama huo, ufuasi wa matibabu unaweza kuwa mgumu. Ikiwa matibabu haijakamilika vizuri, ugonjwa unaweza kuwa sugu wa dawa na unaweza kuenea. Katika kesi ya maambukizi ya TB (ambapo mgonjwa ameambukizwa na bakteria ya TB lakini sio mgonjwa), matibabu ya kuzuia TB yanaweza kutolewa ili kukomesha kuanza kwa ugonjwa huo. Tiba hii hutumia dawa sawa kwa muda mfupi. Chaguo za matibabu za hivi majuzi zimefupisha muda wa matibabu hadi mwezi 1 au 3 tu, ikilinganishwa na miezi 6 iliyopita.
translations.reference: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time. Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.hypothesis: The duration of TB treatment is determined by a healthcare professional or trained volunteer, who provides the patient with information, guidance, and support. Without this assistance, adherence to the treatment regimen can be challenging. If treatment is not completed properly, the disease can become drug-resistant and spread. In the case of latent TB infection (where the patient has been infected with TB bacteria but is not yet sick), preventive TB treatment can be administered to prevent the development of the disease. This treatment involves taking the same medication for a short period of time. Recent advances in treatment have reduced the duration of treatment to as little as one or three months, compared to the previous six months.

translations.source: Mionzi ya ultraviolet Muhtasari: Mionzi ya Ultraviolet (UV) hufunika masafa ya urefu wa 100-400 nm, ambayo ni masafa ya juu na urefu wa chini wa mawimbi kuliko mwanga unaoonekana. Mionzi ya UV hutoka kwa jua, lakini pia inaweza kuundwa na vyanzo vya bandia vinavyotumiwa katika sekta, biashara na burudani. Kanda ya UV inashughulikia safu ya urefu wa 100-400 nm na imegawanywa katika bendi tatu: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). Mwangaza wa jua unapopita kwenye angahewa, UVC zote na takriban 90% ya mionzi ya UVB humezwa na ozoni, mvuke wa maji, oksijeni na dioksidi kaboni. Mionzi ya UVA huathiriwi kidogo na angahewa. Kwa hiyo, mionzi ya UV inayofikia uso wa Dunia kwa kiasi kikubwa inajumuisha UVA na sehemu ndogo ya UVB. Kiasi cha mionzi ya UV kutoka kwenye jua ambayo hupiga uso wa Dunia inategemea mambo kadhaa, ikiwa ni pamoja na urefu wa jua angani, latitudo, kifuniko cha mawingu, urefu, unene wa safu ya ozoni na kutafakari kwa ardhi. Kupungua kwa tabaka la ozoni kwa sababu ya uchafuzi unaotengenezwa na binadamu huongeza kiwango cha UVA na UVB kinachofika kwenye uso. Hii inaweza kuathiri afya ya binadamu, wanyama, viumbe vya baharini na maisha ya mimea.
translations.reference: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide. UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.hypothesis: Ultraviolet Radiation Summary: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers a wavelength range of 100-400 nm, which is a higher and shorter wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes from the sun, but it can also be produced by artificial sources used in industry, business, and entertainment. The UV spectrum is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm), UVB (280-315 nm), and UVC (100-280 nm). When sunlight passes through the atmosphere, most of the UVC and approximately 90% of the UVB are absorbed by ozone, water vapor, oxygen, and carbon dioxide. UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation that reaches the Earth's surface is mainly UVA, with a small amount of UVB. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that reaches the Earth's surface depends on several factors, including the intensity of the sun, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer, and the reflectivity of the Earth's surface. The depletion of the ozone layer due to human-caused pollution has increased the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can have an impact on human health, animal health, marine life, and plant life.

translations.source: Kwa wanadamu, kuongezeka kwa mfiduo wa UV kunaweza kusababisha saratani ya ngozi, mtoto wa jicho na uharibifu wa mfumo wa kinga. Kinga: Kuongezeka kwa idadi ya visa vya saratani ya ngozi katika miongo kadhaa iliyopita kunahusiana sana na kuongezeka kwa mwanga wa jua wakati wa shughuli za nje na vyanzo bandia vya mionzi ya UV kama vile taa za jua na vitanda vya ngozi. Mfiduo kupita kiasi pia ndio sababu kuu ya athari mbaya kwa macho na mfumo wa kinga. Kuchukua tahadhari chache rahisi kunaweza kupunguza sana hatari ya hali hizi za afya: Weka kikomo wakati wa jua la mchana kutoka 10 asubuhi hadi 4 p.m., hasa siku ambazo index ya UV iko juu. Wakati miale ya UV ni mikali zaidi na lazima uwe nje, jaribu kutafuta kivuli na kuvaa mavazi ya kujikinga, ikiwa ni pamoja na kofia yenye ukingo ambayo hutoa ulinzi wa jua kwa kichwa chako, uso na shingo. Tumia kinga ya jua yenye wigo mpana wa angalau SPF 15 - bora zaidi - na upake tena kila saa 2. Epuka taa za jua na vyumba vya ngozi, kwani vinajulikana kuharibu ngozi na macho. Mionzi ya UV ya watoto inapaswa kupunguzwa kwa uangalifu kwa sababu wako katika hali ya ukuaji na kwa hivyo huathirika zaidi na matishio ya mazingira kuliko watu wazima. Kazi nyingi muhimu kama vile mfumo wa kinga haujatengenezwa kikamilifu wakati wa kuzaliwa, na mazingira yasiyo salama yanaweza kuingilia ukuaji wao wa kawaida. Majibu ya WHO: WHO inafanya kazi na Nchi Wanachama na washirika ili kuongeza uelewa wa umma juu ya athari za mionzi ya urujuanimno kupita kiasi.
translations.reference: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck. Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.
translations.hypothesis: For humans, increased exposure to UV radiation can lead to skin cancer, cataracts, and damage to the immune system. Prevention: The increasing number of skin cancer cases over the past few decades has been strongly linked to increased sun exposure during outdoor activities and from artificial sources of UV radiation, such as tanning beds and sunlamps. Excessive exposure is also a major cause of eye and immune system problems. By taking a few simple precautions, the risk of these health problems can be significantly reduced: Limit sun exposure during the middle of the day, from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., especially on days when the UV index is high. When you must be outdoors, seek shade and wear protective clothing, including a wide-brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face, and neck. Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen with an SPF of at least 15 – ideally 30 or higher – and reapply every two hours. Avoid tanning beds and sunlamps, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children are particularly vulnerable to the harmful effects of UV radiation because their bodies are still developing and they are more susceptible to environmental hazards than adults. Many important bodily functions are not fully developed at birth, and exposure to harmful UV radiation can interfere with their normal development.

translations.source: Mpango wa INTERSUN ni ushirikiano kati ya WHO, Mpango wa Mazingira wa Umoja wa Mataifa, Shirika la Hali ya Hewa Duniani, Shirika la Kimataifa la Utafiti wa Saratani na Tume ya Kimataifa ya Ulinzi wa Mionzi isiyo ya Ion. Inakuza na kutathmini utafiti juu ya athari za kiafya za mionzi ya UV, na kukuza jibu linalofaa kupitia miongozo, mapendekezo na usambazaji wa habari. Malengo ya mpango huo ikiwa ni pamoja na kutoa ushauri wa kiutendaji na mzuri kuhusu athari za kiafya na kimazingira zitokanazo na mionzi ya jua, kuhimiza nchi kuchukua hatua ili kupunguza hatari za kiafya zitokanazo na UV na kutoa mwongozo kwa mamlaka za kitaifa kuhusu mipango ya uhamasishaji wa jua. Ili kufikia mwisho huu, mpango huo unashirikiana na wataalam na mashirika maalum kutekeleza shughuli muhimu za utafiti, kutambua na kuhesabu hatari za afya kutokana na mionzi ya UV, kuendeleza utabiri wa kuaminika wa matokeo ya afya na mazingira ya mabadiliko ya mfiduo wa UV na kupungua kwa ozoni ya stratospheric, na kuendeleza njia za vitendo za ufuatiliaji mabadiliko katika athari za kiafya zinazotokana na UV kwa wakati. Mpango huo hutoa zana na miongozo inayohusiana na fahirisi ya UV, ulinzi wa jua kwa watoto, vitanda vya jua bandia, utalii na afya ya kazini.
translations.reference: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.
translations.hypothesis: The INTERSUN program is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Health Organization, the International Agency for Research on Cancer, and the International Commission on Radiological Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and aims to provide evidence-based guidance through guidelines, recommendations, and information dissemination. The program's objectives include providing expert advice on the health and environmental effects of UV radiation, encouraging countries to take action to reduce the health risks associated with UV exposure, and providing guidance to national authorities on sun safety programs. To achieve these goals, the program works with experts and specialized organizations to conduct important research activities, identify and quantify health risks associated with UV radiation, develop reliable predictions of the health and environmental impacts of changing UV exposure and ozone depletion, and develop practical methods for monitoring changes in UV-related health effects over time. The program provides tools and guidance on topics such as UV index, sun protection for children, sunscreen, sunbeds, and occupational health.

translations.source: Chanjo ya afya kwa wote Muhtasari: Huduma ya afya kwa wote (UHC) ina maana kwamba watu wote wanapata huduma kamili za ubora wa afya wanazohitaji, wakati na mahali wanapozihitaji, bila matatizo ya kifedha. Inashughulikia mwendelezo kamili wa huduma muhimu za afya, kutoka kwa uhamasishaji wa afya hadi kinga, matibabu, urekebishaji na utunzaji wa uponyaji. Ili kutimiza ahadi hii, nchi zinahitaji kuwa na mifumo thabiti ya afya, yenye ufanisi na yenye usawa ambayo imejikita katika jumuiya zinazohudumia. Huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC) ndiyo njia bora na ya gharama nafuu ya kufika hapo. Kila nchi ina njia tofauti ya kufikia UHC na kuamua nini cha kushughulikia kulingana na mahitaji ya idadi ya watu na rasilimali zilizopo. Kuwekeza katika PHC huhakikisha kwamba mahitaji hayo yote yanatambuliwa, yanapewa kipaumbele na kushughulikiwa kwa njia jumuishi; kwamba kuna nguvu kazi ya afya na utunzaji imara na yenye vifaa; na kwamba sekta zote za jamii zinachangia katika kukabiliana na mambo ya kimazingira na kijamii na kiuchumi yanayoathiri afya na ustawi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kujiandaa kwa ajili ya, kukabiliana na hali ya dharura. Jibu la WHO: Kama msingi wa UHC, WHO inapendekeza kuelekeza upya mifumo ya afya kwa kutumia mbinu ya huduma ya afya ya msingi (PHC). Kufikia UHC ni kipaumbele cha kimkakati cha WHO, kukiwa na lengo la watu bilioni 1 zaidi kunufaika na huduma ya afya kwa wote ifikapo 2025. tunazingatia usaidizi wa kiufundi ili kujenga taasisi za kitaifa na utoaji wa huduma ili kujaza nafasi muhimu wakati wa dharura.
translations.reference: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there. Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.hypothesis: Universal Health Coverage: A Summary: Universal Health Coverage (UHC) means that everyone has access to the quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without facing financial hardship. It encompasses the full range of essential health services, from health promotion and disease prevention to treatment, rehabilitation, and palliative care. To achieve this goal, countries need to have strong, efficient, and equitable health systems that are focused on communities. Primary Health Care (PHC) is the most cost-effective way to achieve UHC. Each country has its own approach to achieving UHC and determining what to prioritize based on the needs of its population and available resources. Investing in PHC ensures that all needs are identified, prioritized, and addressed in a coordinated manner; that there is a strong and well-equipped health workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to addressing the social, economic, and environmental factors that affect health and well-being, including preparedness for and response to emergencies. WHO's Response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends refocusing health systems on primary health care (PHC). Achieving UHC is a strategic priority for WHO, with the goal of reaching an additional 1 billion people with health services by 2025. We are also providing technical support to build national health systems and service delivery capacity to address critical gaps during emergencies.

translations.source: Katika mipangilio thabiti zaidi ya mfumo wa afya, tunasukuma athari za afya ya umma kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote kupitia mazungumzo ya sera ya mifumo ya siku zijazo na usaidizi wa kimkakati ili kuboresha utendaji kazi. Kazi hii inaungwa mkono na mwongozo na makubaliano ya kawaida; na uongozi katika nyanja za diplomasia, utetezi, usawa wa kijinsia, usawa wa afya na haki za binadamu, hatua za sekta mbalimbali, na fedha. Ajenda ya 2030 ya SDGs Kazi ya WHO inawiana na Malengo ya Maendeleo Endelevu (SDG) lengo la 3.8, ambalo linalenga kufikia bima ya afya kwa wote, ikiwa ni pamoja na ulinzi wa hatari za kifedha, upatikanaji wa huduma bora za afya na gharama nafuu za dawa muhimu na chanjo za bei nafuu kwa wote. Ulimwenguni, maendeleo kuelekea huduma ya afya kwa wote ni changamoto. Maendeleo katika utoaji wa huduma yamekwama huku idadi ya watu wanaokabiliwa na matumizi mabaya ya afya nje ya mfukoni ikiongezeka kila mara. WHO hufuatilia maendeleo na kuchapisha ripoti za kimataifa ili kufichua ushahidi wa hivi punde kila baada ya miaka miwili. Tazama ripoti hapa na utembelee karatasi ya ukweli ya UHC kwa habari zaidi.
translations.reference: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously. WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
translations.hypothesis: In the most robust framework for health systems, we aim to translate public health outcomes into universal access to healthcare through strategic dialogue on future health system policies and support, with the goal of improving operational efficiency. This work is guided by common principles and agreements; and leadership in areas such as diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity, and human rights, as well as various sector-specific initiatives and funding. The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development aligns with WHO's work, specifically SDG 3.8, which aims to achieve universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality affordable essential medicines and health technologies, and access to healthcare services. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage remains a challenge. Progress in service delivery has stalled, while the number of people facing poor health outcomes due to financial barriers continues to grow. WHO monitors progress and publishes international reports every two years to highlight the latest evidence. See the report here and explore the UHC roadmap for more information.

translations.source: Afya ya mjini Muhtasari: Ukuaji wa miji ni mojawapo ya mitindo inayoongoza duniani ya karne ya 21 ambayo ina athari kubwa kwa afya. Zaidi ya 55% ya watu duniani wanaishi mijini - idadi ambayo inatarajiwa kuongezeka hadi 68% ifikapo 2050. Kwa vile ukuaji mkubwa wa miji katika siku zijazo utafanyika katika nchi zinazoendelea, dunia leo ina fursa ya kipekee ya kuongoza ukuaji wa miji na mwelekeo mwingine mkuu wa maendeleo ya miji kwa njia ambayo inalinda na kukuza afya. Hii ni muhimu, sio kwa sababu afya na ustawi wa raia labda ndio mali muhimu zaidi ya jiji. Hata hivyo, wengi kati ya watu bilioni 4.2 wanaoishi mijini wanakabiliana na ukosefu wa makazi na usafiri, usafi wa mazingira na usimamizi duni wa taka, na ubora wa hewa ambao unakiuka miongozo ya WHO. Aina zingine za uchafuzi wa mazingira, kama vile kelele, uchafuzi wa maji na udongo, kinachojulikana kama visiwa vya joto mijini, na ukosefu wa nafasi ya kutembea, kuendesha baiskeli na kuishi hai huchanganyika zaidi na kufanya miji kuwa kitovu cha janga la magonjwa yasiyoambukiza na vichochezi vya mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa. Hazina ya WHO kwa Afya ya Mjini Athari: Takriban 40% ya ukuaji wa miji uko katika makazi duni ambayo hayana maji salama na vyoo, na 91% ya watu katika maeneo ya mijini wanapumua hewa chafu. Linapokuja suala la lishe bora, ukuaji wa miji huongeza umbali kutoka shamba hadi uma, na kusababisha mahitaji ya vyakula visivyo na afya, vilivyochakatwa.
translations.reference: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset. However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.hypothesis: Urban Health Summary: Urban growth is one of the leading trends in the 21st century, with significant impacts on health. More than 55% of the world's population lives in cities – a number expected to reach 68% by 2050. As rapid urbanization continues in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to lead urban growth in a way that protects and promotes health. This is not only important because the health and well-being of citizens are arguably the most valuable assets of a city. However, many of the 4.2 billion people living in cities face challenges such as inadequate housing, transportation, sanitation, and waste management, as well as poor air quality that violates WHO guidelines. Other environmental hazards, such as noise, water, and soil pollution, as well as the "urban heat island" effect and lack of green spaces, further contribute to making cities centers of non-communicable diseases and climate change. WHO Report on Urban Health: Approximately 40% of urban growth occurs in informal settlements that lack basic services such as safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urban growth increases the distance between farms and urban areas, leading to a greater demand for unhealthy, processed foods.

translations.source: Wakazi wa mijini pia wako katika hatari kubwa ya kuathiriwa na mabadiliko ya hali ya hewa kwa sababu ya utegemezi wao wa nishati ya mafuta kwa usafiri, kupikia na kupasha joto. Miji huchukua zaidi ya theluthi mbili ya nishati ya ulimwengu na hutoa 60% ya gesi chafuzi, na zile za ndani zinaweza kukumbwa na halijoto ya 3–5º C juu kuliko maeneo ya vijijini yanayozunguka kwa sababu ya upana wake mkubwa wa saruji na nafasi finyu za kijani kibichi. Haya yote yanahatarisha afya ya wakazi wa mijini. Sababu 10 kuu za vifo zinahusiana kwa karibu na ukuaji wa miji wa haraka na usio na mpango, na muundo mbaya wa mijini na mipango. Miji inakabiliwa na mzigo wa kiafya mara tatu wa magonjwa ya kuambukiza kama vile VVU/UKIMWI, kifua kikuu, nimonia, dengi na kuhara; magonjwa yasiyo ya kuambukiza kama vile ugonjwa wa moyo, kiharusi, pumu, saratani, kisukari na unyogovu; na vurugu na majeraha, ikiwa ni pamoja na majeraha ya barabarani. Jibu la WHO: Licha ya changamoto zao, miji inaweza kuunda fursa kwa afya bora, mazingira safi na hatua za hali ya hewa. Sera madhubuti za mijini lazima zitangulize afya, kwani ni muhimu kwa ajili ya kuendeleza maisha bora ya mijini, kujenga nguvu kazi yenye tija, kuunda jamii zenye uthabiti na uchangamfu, kuwezesha uhamaji, kukuza mwingiliano wa kijamii na kulinda idadi ya watu walio hatarini. Mtazamo wa WHO kuhusu afya ya mijini unazingatia ubora bora wa hewa, maji na usafi wa mazingira, na viashirio vingine vya mazingira; mipango ya miji yenye afya; mazingira bora na yasiyo na moshi; uhamaji salama na kiafya; kuzuia vurugu na majeraha; kukuza mifumo ya chakula na lishe bora; usimamizi wa mazingira ya magonjwa yanayoenezwa na vekta.na kujiandaa kwa miji kwa dharura.
translations.reference: Urban dwellers are also highly vulnerable to the effects of climate change because of their dependence on fossil fuels for transport, cooking and heating. Cities account for over two thirds of the world’s energy and emit 60% of greenhouse gases, and those inland may experience temperatures 3–5º C higher than surrounding rural areas because of their large expanses of concrete and limited open green spaces. All of this puts urban dwellers’ health at risk. Most the top 10 causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urbanization, and poor urban design and planning. Cities face the triple health burden of infectious diseases like HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, dengue and diarrhoea; noncommunicable diseases like heart disease, stroke, asthma, cancer, diabetes and depression; and violence and injuries, including road traffic injuries. WHO response: Despite their challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments and climate action. Strong urban policies must prioritize health, as it is essential for fostering good urban livelihoods, building a productive workforce, creating resilient and vibrant communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO’s approach to urban health focuses on better air quality, water and sanitation, and other environmental determinants; healthy urban planning; healthier and smoke-free environments; safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy food systems and diets; environmental management of vector-borne diseases; and urban preparedness for emergencies.
translations.hypothesis: Urban residents are also at high risk of being affected by climate change due to their reliance on fossil fuels for transportation, cooking, and heating. Cities consume more than two-thirds of the world's energy and generate 60% of greenhouse gas emissions, and their indoor air quality can be 3–5°C warmer than that of surrounding rural areas due to their large surface area and limited green space. All of these factors pose a threat to the health of urban residents. The ten leading causes of death are closely related to rapid and unplanned urban growth, and poor urban design and planning. Cities are also facing a triple burden of disease, including infectious diseases such as HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, pneumonia, influenza, and diarrhea; non-communicable diseases such as heart disease, stroke, cancer, diabetes, and mental disorders; and injuries and violence, including traffic accidents. WHO's response: Despite these challenges, cities can create opportunities for better health, cleaner environments, and climate action. Strong urban policies should prioritize health, as it is essential for creating healthy and sustainable cities, building productive workforces, fostering resilient communities, enabling mobility, promoting social interaction, and protecting vulnerable populations. WHO's perspective on urban health focuses on improving air, water, and environmental quality, as well as other environmental indicators; creating healthy and sustainable cities; promoting safe and healthy mobility; preventing violence and injuries; promoting healthy diets and food systems; managing the environmental risks of vector-borne diseases; and preparing cities for emergencies.

translations.source: Kufanya kazi katika sekta zote na kuhakikisha uwiano wa sera katika maeneo mbalimbali ni muhimu katika kuunda mazingira ya kusaidia na kuwezesha afya, ambayo yanahakikisha kwamba masuala ya afya na usawa yanaunganishwa katika mchakato wa kupanga, uwekezaji na maamuzi ya sera katika ngazi ya ndani. Ili kusaidia Nchi Wanachama kushughulikia vipaumbele vilivyo hapo juu, WHO inafanya kazi ili kuimarisha msingi wa ushahidi ili kusaidia watunga sera kufanya maamuzi sahihi wanaposhughulikia hatari za kiafya. Inatoa zana na mwongozo juu ya kile kinachofanya kazi, na inasaidia ufuatiliaji wa viashiria muhimu vinavyohusiana na afya. WHO inaongoza na kushiriki katika kuhimiza mabadilishano ya jiji hadi jiji na kusaidia kuandaa mifumo ya kitaasisi na sera ya utawala bora wa miji kwa afya na ustawi katika miji.
translations.reference: Working across sectors and ensuring the coherence of policies across different areas is key to creating supportive and enabling environments for health, which ensures that health and equity considerations are integrated throughout the planning process, investments and policy decisions at the local level. To help Member States address the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policy-makers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports monitoring of key health-related indicators. WHO leads and engages in fostering city-to-city exchanges and helps develop institutional and policy frameworks for good urban governance for health and well-being in cities.
translations.hypothesis: Working across all sectors and ensuring policy coherence in various areas is crucial in creating an enabling environment for health, which ensures that health and social issues are integrated into the planning, investment, and policy-making processes at the national level. To support Member States in addressing the above priorities, WHO works to strengthen the evidence base to help policymakers make informed decisions when addressing health risks. It provides tools and guidance on what works, and supports the monitoring of key indicators related to health. WHO leads and participates in promoting urban health, working to establish city-to-city networks and support the development of effective institutional and policy frameworks for urban health and well-being.

translations.source: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unajumuisha aina zote za ukatili dhidi ya watu walio chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Kwa watoto wachanga na watoto wadogo, unyanyasaji unahusisha hasa unyanyasaji wa watoto (yaani unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kingono na kihisia na kutelekezwa) mikononi mwa wazazi na watu wengine wenye mamlaka. Wavulana na wasichana wako katika hatari sawa ya unyanyasaji wa kimwili na kihisia na kutelekezwa, na wasichana wako katika hatari kubwa ya unyanyasaji wa kijinsia. Watoto wanapobalehe, unyanyasaji wa rika na ukatili wa marafiki wa karibu, pamoja na unyanyasaji wa watoto, huenea sana. Ukatili dhidi ya watoto unaweza kuzuiwa. Kuzuia na kukabiliana na unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kunahitaji jitihada za kushughulikia hatari na ulinzi katika viwango vyote vinne vya hatari (mtu binafsi, uhusiano, jumuiya, jamii). Azimio la Mei 2016 la Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni liliidhinisha mpango wa kwanza wa utekelezaji wa WHO wa Kimataifa wa kuimarisha jukumu la mfumo wa afya ndani ya mwitikio wa kitaifa wa sekta mbalimbali kushughulikia unyanyasaji wa watu binafsi, hasa dhidi ya wanawake na wasichana, na dhidi ya watoto. A Kulingana na mpango huu, WHO kwa kushirikiana na Nchi Wanachama na washirika wengine, imejitolea: Kufuatilia ukubwa wa kimataifa na sifa za ukatili dhidi ya watoto na kuunga mkono juhudi za nchi kuweka kumbukumbu na kupima ukatili huo. Kudumisha mfumo wa taarifa za kielektroniki unaotoa muhtasari wa data ya kisayansi kuhusu mzigo, sababu za hatari na matokeo ya unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto, na ushahidi wa kuzuilika kwake.
translations.reference: Violence against children Overview: Violence against children includes all forms of violence against people under 18 years old. For infants and younger children, violence mainly involves child maltreatment (i.e. physical, sexual and emotional abuse and neglect) at the hands of parents and other authority figures. Boys and girls are at equal risk of physical and emotional abuse and neglect, and girls are at greater risk of sexual abuse. As children reach adolescence, peer violence and intimate partner violence, in addition to child maltreatment, become highly prevalent. Violence against children can be prevented. Preventing and responding to violence against children requires that efforts systematically address risk and protective factors at all four interrelated levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, society). May 2016 World Health Assembly resolution endorsed the first ever WHO Global plan of action on strengthening the role of the health system within a national multisectoral response to address interpersonal violence, in particular against women and girls, and against children. A According to this plan, WHO in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global magnitude and characteristics of violence against children and supporting country efforts to document and measure such violence. Maintaining an electronic information system that summarizes the scientific data on the burden, risk factors and consequences of violence against children, and the evidence for its preventability.
translations.hypothesis: Child Abuse: Child abuse encompasses all forms of abuse against individuals under the age of 18. For infants and young children, abuse primarily involves physical, emotional, and sexual abuse, as well as neglect, perpetrated by parents and other caregivers. Boys and girls are equally at risk of physical and emotional abuse and neglect, while girls are at a higher risk of sexual abuse. As children grow, bullying and peer violence, along with child abuse, become increasingly prevalent. Child abuse can be prevented. Preventing and addressing child abuse requires efforts to address risk and protective factors at the four levels of risk (individual, relationship, community, and societal). The May 2016 resolution of the World Health Assembly endorsed the WHO's first global plan of action to strengthen the role of the health system within national multi-sectoral responses to address violence against individuals, particularly against women and girls, and against children. According to this plan, WHO, in collaboration with Member States and other partners, is committed to: Monitoring the global prevalence and characteristics of child abuse and supporting national efforts to document and measure this form of abuse. Establishing an electronic information system that provides a summary of scientific data on the burden, risk factors, and outcomes of child abuse, as well as evidence of prevention.

translations.source: Kutengeneza na kusambaza hati za mwongozo wa kiufundi, kanuni na viwango vya kuzuia na kukabiliana na ukatili dhidi ya watoto kulingana na ushahidi. Kuchapisha mara kwa mara ripoti za hali ya kimataifa kuhusu juhudi za nchi kushughulikia unyanyasaji dhidi ya watoto kupitia sera za kitaifa na mipango ya utekelezaji, sheria, programu za kuzuia na huduma za kukabiliana. Nchi zinazounga mkono na washirika katika kutekeleza mikakati ya uzuiaji na majibu yenye msingi wa ushahidi, kama ile iliyojumuishwa katika INSPIRE: Mikakati saba ya kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto. Kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kupunguza na kukomesha ukatili dhidi ya watoto duniani kote, kupitia mipango kama vile Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto, Ushirikiano wa Kimataifa wa Kutokomeza Ukatili dhidi ya Watoto, kwa pamoja kwa Wasichana na Muungano wa Kuzuia Ghasia. Unyanyasaji wa watoto Unyanyasaji wa watoto ni unyanyasaji na utelekezwaji unaotokea kwa watoto chini ya umri wa miaka 18. Inajumuisha aina zote za unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kihisia, unyanyasaji wa kijinsia, kutelekezwa, uzembe na unyonyaji wa kibiashara au mwingine, unaosababisha madhara halisi au yanayoweza kutokea kwa afya, maisha, ukuaji au utu wa mtoto katika muktadha wa uhusiano wa mtoto wajibu, uaminifu au madaraka. Mfiduo wa unyanyasaji wa rafiki wa karibu wakati mwingine hujumuishwa kama unyanyasaji wa watoto.
translations.reference: Developing and disseminating evidence-based technical guidance documents, norms and standards for preventing and responding to violence against children. Regularly publishing global status reports on country efforts to address violence against children through national policies and action plans, laws, prevention programmes and response services. Supporting countries and partners in implementing evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those included in INSPIRE: Seven strategies for ending violence against children. Collaborating with international agencies and organizations to reduce and eliminate violence against children globally, through initiatives such as the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, the Global Partnership to End Violence against Children, Together for Girls and the Violence Prevention Alliance. Child maltreatment Child maltreatment is the abuse and neglect that occurs to children under 18 years of age. It includes all types of physical and/or emotional ill-treatment, sexual abuse, neglect, negligence and commercial or other exploitation, which results in actual or potential harm to the child’s health, survival, development or dignity in the context of a relationship of responsibility, trust or power. Exposure to intimate partner violence is also sometimes included as a form of child maltreatment.
translations.hypothesis: Developing and disseminating technical guidelines, principles, and standards for preventing and responding to child abuse, based on evidence. Regularly publishing international reports on countries' efforts to address child abuse through national policies and implementation plans, laws, prevention programs, and support services. Supporting and collaborating with national and international organizations to implement evidence-based prevention and response strategies, such as those outlined in INSPIRE: Seven Strategies to End Violence Against Children. Partnering with international organizations to reduce and eliminate child abuse worldwide, through initiatives such as the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child, the Global Partnership to End Violence Against Children, and the Girls' Fund for Peace and Security. Child abuse is any act of violence or abuse that occurs against a child under the age of 18. It includes all forms of physical and/or emotional abuse, sexual abuse, neglect, and commercial or other exploitation that causes actual or potential harm to a child's health, well-being, development, or person in the context of a relationship of power, authority, or trust. Exposure to peer pressure can also be considered a form of child abuse.

translations.source: Unyanyasaji wa watoto una madhara makubwa sana ya papo hapo na ya muda mrefu. Zaidi ya kifo, jeraha la kimwili na ulemavu, vurugu inaweza kusababisha dhiki ambayo huharibu ukuaji wa ubongo na kuharibu mifumo ya neva na kinga. Hii nayo inahusishwa na kucheleweshwa kwa maendeleo ya utambuzi, utendaji duni wa shule na kuacha shule, matatizo ya afya ya akili, majaribio ya kujiua, kuongezeka kwa tabia zinazohatarisha afya, urekebishaji na maandalizi ya unyanyasaji. Habari njema ni kwamba unyanyasaji wa watoto unaweza kuzuiwa kupitia hatua zinazosaidia wazazi na walezi, kukuza kanuni na maadili yasiyo ya ukatili, kutoa elimu na mafunzo ya stadi za maisha, kuimarisha mapato ya familia na usalama wa kiuchumi, kutoa mwitikio wa hali ya juu na huduma za usaidizi, kuunda na kudumisha mazingira salama kwa watoto, na kutekeleza na kutekeleza sheria dhidi ya unyanyasaji wa watoto. WHO, kwa kushirikiana na washirika kadhaa: hutoa mwongozo wa kiufundi na wa kawaida kwa kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi; inatetea ongezeko la usaidizi wa kimataifa kwa uwekezaji katika kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi; hutoa usaidizi wa kiufundi kwa programu za kuzuia unyanyasaji wa watoto kulingana na ushahidi katika nchi kadhaa za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Vurugu za vijana Vurugu za vijana ni tatizo la afya ya umma duniani.
translations.reference: Child maltreatment has enormous immediate and long-term repercussions. Beyond death, physical injury and disability, violence can lead to stress that impairs brain development and damages the nervous and immune systems. This in turn is associated with delayed cognitive development, poor school performance and dropout, mental health problems, suicide attempts, increased health-risk behaviours, revictimization and the perpetration of violence. The good news is that child maltreatment can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent norms and values, provide education and life skills training, strengthen families’ income and economic security, offer high quality response and support services, create and sustain safe environments for children, and implement and enforce laws against child maltreatment. WHO, in collaboration with a number of partners: provides technical and normative guidance for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; advocates for increased international support for and investment in evidence-based child maltreatment prevention; provides technical support for evidence-based child maltreatment prevention programmes in several low- and middle-income countries. Youth violence Youth violence is a global public health problem.
translations.hypothesis: Child abuse has severe, both immediate and long-term, consequences. In addition to death, physical injury, and disability, abuse can cause emotional distress that impairs brain development and damages the nervous and immune systems. This can lead to delayed cognitive development, poor school performance, dropping out of school, mental health problems, suicidal ideation, increased risk of unhealthy behaviors, and substance abuse. Fortunately, child abuse can be prevented through interventions that support parents and caregivers, promote non-violent norms and values, provide life skills education, strengthen family income and economic security, offer high-quality services and support, create safe environments for children, and enforce laws against child abuse. The WHO, in collaboration with numerous partners, provides technical and programmatic guidance on preventing child abuse based on evidence; advocates for increased international funding for evidence-based child abuse prevention; and provides technical assistance to evidence-based child abuse prevention programs in low- and middle-income countries.

translations.source: Inajumuisha aina mbalimbali za vitendo kutoka kwa uonevu na mapigano ya kimwili, hadi unyanyasaji mkali zaidi wa kingono na kimwili hadi mauaji. Vurugu za vijana huongeza kwa kiasi kikubwa gharama za afya, ustawi na haki ya jinai; hupunguza tija; na kwa ujumla hudhoofisha muundo wa jamii. Zaidi ya vifo, majeraha na madhara ya kisaikolojia, unyanyasaji wa vijana unaweza kusababisha kuongezeka kwa tabia hatari za kiafya kama vile kuvuta sigara, matumizi mabaya ya dawa za kulevya, ngono isiyo salama na vurugu zaidi. Haya nayo yanahusishwa na magonjwa sugu ya kupumua, saratani, magonjwa ya moyo na mishipa, kisukari, mimba za utotoni na magonjwa ya zinaa. Mikakati madhubuti ya kuzuia na kukabiliana nayo ni pamoja na ile inayokuza uzazi na maendeleo ya utotoni; programu za kuzuia uonevu shuleni, kitaaluma, na maisha na ujuzi wa kijamii; mbinu za kimatibabu na watu walio katika hatari kubwa, na mbinu za ngazi ya jamii na jamii kama vile kupunguza ufikiaji na matumizi mabaya ya pombe na silaha za moto, maeneo yenye matatizo makubwa na polisi wenye matatizo, uboreshaji wa miji na kupunguza umaskini. WHO na washirika hupunguza unyanyasaji wa vijana kupitia mipango inayosaidia kutambua, kuhesabu na kukabiliana na tatizo, hizi ni pamoja na: kuandaa kifurushi cha programu za kuzuia unyanyasaji shuleni; kuzingatia ukubwa wa vurugu za vijana na haja ya kuzuia;
translations.reference: It includes a range of acts from bullying and physical fighting, to more severe sexual and physical assault to homicide. Youth violence dramatically increases health, welfare and criminal justice costs; reduces productivity; and generally undermines the fabric of society. Beyond deaths, injuries and psychological harm, youth violence can lead to increased health risk behaviours such as smoking, substance abuse, unsafe sex, and further violence. These in turn are associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, early pregnancy and sexually transmitted diseases. Effective prevention and response strategies include those that promote parenting and early childhood development; school-based bullying prevention, academic, and life and social skills development programmes; therapeutic approaches with high-risk individuals, and community- and society-level approaches like reducing access to and misuse of alcohol and firearms, hotspots and problem-oriented policing, urban upgrading, and poverty de-concentration. WHO and partners decrease youth violence through initiatives that help to identify, quantify and respond to the problem, these include: developing a package for schools-based violence prevention programmes; drawing attention to the magnitude of youth violence and the need for prevention;
translations.hypothesis: It encompasses a wide range of behaviors, from physical and sexual violence to severe emotional and psychological abuse, and even homicide. Youth violence significantly increases the costs associated with health, well-being, and the criminal justice system; it reduces productivity; and generally undermines the fabric of society. In addition to deaths, injuries, and psychological trauma, youth violence can also lead to increased risk behaviors such as smoking, drug use, unsafe sexual practices, and further violence. These behaviors are also associated with chronic respiratory diseases, cancer, heart disease, diabetes, and sexually transmitted infections. Effective prevention and intervention strategies include those that promote healthy parenting and child development; school-based, community-based, and life skills programs; medical and mental health services for those at risk; and community-level interventions such as reducing access to alcohol and firearms, addressing violence in high-risk areas, and improving law enforcement. WHO and its partners are working to reduce youth violence through programs that aim to identify, measure, and respond to the problem, including: developing a toolkit of school-based violence prevention programs; addressing the prevalence of youth violence and the need for prevention;

translations.source: kujenga ushahidi juu ya upeo na aina za vurugu katika mazingira tofauti; kuendeleza miongozo kwa Nchi Wanachama na sekta zote zinazohusika ili kuzuia vurugu za vijana na kuimarisha majibu yake; kuunga mkono juhudi za kitaifa za kuzuia ghasia za vijana; na kushirikiana na mashirika na mashirika ya kimataifa ili kuzuia unyanyasaji wa vijana duniani kote.
translations.reference: building evidence on the scope and types of violence in different settings; developing guidance for Member States and all relevant sectors to prevent youth violence and strengthen responses to it; supporting national efforts to prevent youth violence; and collaborating with international agencies and organizations to prevent youth violence globally.
translations.hypothesis: building evidence-based strategies to address different forms of violence in various contexts; developing guidelines for member states and all relevant sectors to prevent youth violence and strengthen responses; supporting national efforts to prevent youth violence; and collaborating with national and international organizations to combat youth violence worldwide.

translations.source: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake Muhtasari: Ukatili dhidi ya wanawake - hasa unyanyasaji wa mpenzi wa karibu na unyanyasaji wa kijinsia - ni tatizo kubwa la afya ya umma na kiafya na ukiukaji wa haki za binadamu za wanawake. Umejikita katika na kuendeleza ukosefu wa usawa wa kijinsia.Ulimwenguni 1 kati ya wanawake 3 hupata unyanyasaji wa kimwili na/au kingono maishani mwao, hasa na mwenzi wa karibu. Hiki ni ukumbusho kamili wa ukubwa wa usawa wa kijinsia na ubaguzi dhidi ya wanawake. Umoja wa Mataifa unafafanua unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake kama "kitendo chochote cha unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ambacho husababisha, au kinaweza kusababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono, au kiakili au mateso kwa wanawake, ikiwa ni pamoja na vitisho vya vitendo kama hivyo, kulazimishwa au kunyimwa uhuru kiholela, iwe unafanyika hadharani au katika maisha ya kibinafsi. Vurugu za mpenzi wa karibu hurejelea tabia ya mshirika wa karibu au mpenzi wa zamani ambayo husababisha madhara ya kimwili, kingono au kisaikolojia, ikiwa ni pamoja na unyanyasaji wa kimwili, kulazimishwa kingono, unyanyasaji wa kisaikolojia na kudhibiti tabia. Unyanyasaji wa kijinsia ni "tendo lolote la ngono, jaribio la kupata tendo la ngono, au kitendo kingine kinachoelekezwa dhidi ya ujinsia wa mtu kwa kulazimishwa, na mtu yeyote bila kujali uhusiano wake na mwathiriwa, katika mazingira yoyote. Inajumuisha ubakaji, unaofafanuliwa kama kupenya kwa kulazimishwa au vinginevyo kwa lazima kwa uke au mkundu kwa kutumia uume, sehemu nyingine ya mwili au kitu." Athari: Unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake ni tatizo la kimataifa la uwiano wa janga. Inasababisha uharibifu, madhara kwa maisha ya wanawake na ya watoto wao.
translations.reference: Violence against women Overview: Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public and clinical health problem and a violation of women's human rights. It is rooted in and perpetuates gender inequalities.Globally 1 in 3 women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, mostly by an intimate partner. This is a stark reminder of the scale of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of gender-based violence that results in, or is likely to result in, physical, sexual, or mental harm or suffering to women, including threats of such acts, coercion or arbitrary deprivation of liberty, whether occurring in public or in private life. Intimate partner violence refers to behaviour by an intimate partner or ex-partner that causes physical, sexual or psychological harm, including physical aggression, sexual coercion, psychological abuse and controlling behaviours. Sexual violence is "any sexual act, attempt to obtain a sexual act, or other act directed against a person’s sexuality using coercion, by any person regardless of their relationship to the victim, in any setting. It includes rape, defined as the physically forced or otherwise coerced penetration of the vulva or anus with a penis, other body part or object." Impact: Violence against women is a global problem of pandemic proportions. It causes devastating, harm to women’s lives and that of their children.
translations.hypothesis: Violence against women: Violence against women – particularly intimate partner violence and sexual violence – is a major public health and human rights issue. It perpetuates gender inequality and contributes to a range of negative outcomes for women. Globally, one in three women experience physical and/or sexual violence in their lifetime, most often by an intimate partner. This highlights the extent of gender inequality and discrimination against women. The United Nations defines violence against women as "any act of physical, sexual, or psychological violence, including gender-based violence that results in, or may lead to, physical, sexual, psychological or other forms of injury or death." Intimate partner violence refers to any act of physical, sexual, or psychological violence perpetrated by a current or former intimate partner, including domestic violence, sexual coercion, and emotional abuse. Sexual violence is defined as "any act of sexual penetration, however achieved, or any attempt to achieve sexual penetration, or any other act that is directed at the sexual function of a person and is unwelcome by the person, regardless of the nature of the relationship between the perpetrator and the victim, and in any setting." Consequences: Violence against women is a global public health issue. It has a range of negative consequences for women, including physical injury, mental health problems, and increased risk of death.

translations.source: Pia inadhuru afya ya kiuchumi na kijamii ya familia, jumuiya na nchi zao. Wakati baadhi ya wanawake wako katika hatari zaidi kuliko wengine, unyanyasaji unaweza kutokea kwa mwanamke yeyote, katika nchi yoyote - bila kujali tamaduni, dini au hali ya kiuchumi. Ukosefu wa usawa wa kijinsia ikiwa ni pamoja na kanuni hatari za kijinsia ni vichochezi muhimu vya unyanyasaji dhidi ya wanawake. Ukatili unaweza kuathiri vibaya afya ya wanawake kimwili, kiakili, kingono na uzazi. Inahusishwa na hatari kubwa ya majeraha, huzuni, matatizo ya wasiwasi, mimba zisizopangwa, magonjwa ya zinaa, VVU na matatizo mengine mengi ya afya. Watoto wanaolelewa katika familia ambazo kuna jeuri wanaweza kukumbwa na misukosuko mbalimbali ya kitabia na kihisia. Haya yanaweza kusababisha matatizo ya kiakili na ukuaji na pia kuhusishwa na kufanya au kukumbana na ukatili baadaye maishani.
