translations.source: Kuhimiza mvuto, mafunzo, kuajiri na kubaki kwa watu wa kiasili kama wahudumu wa afya kwa kuzingatia maarifa na desturi za jadi. Hati inayohusiana: A76/A/ CONF./ 1- Afya ya watu wa kiasili Azimio la kwanza kabisa la kuharakisha hatua za kuzuia kuzama kwa maji Wajumbe katika mkutano wa Sabini na sita wa Baraza la Afya Ulimwenguni walikubaliana Azimio la kuharakisha hatua za kuzuia kuzama kwa maji Duniani. Azimio hilo linaziomba nchi wanachama kutathmini hali yao ya kitaifa ya kuzama majini na kuendeleza kutekeleza mipango ya sekta mbalimbali ya kuzuia kuzama maji. Kuzama husababisha vifo 236,000 kila mwaka. Ni sababu kuu ya kimataifa ya vifo vya watoto vinavyohusiana na majeraha. Katika muongo mmoja uliopita, watu milioni 2.5 walikufa kutokana na kufa maji, na zaidi ya 90% ya hao walitokea katika nchi za kipato cha chini na cha kati. Kwa mualiko wa Baraza kuu la umoja wa mataifa (UN), WHO itaratibu hatua ndani ya mfumo wa umoja wa mataifa kuhusu kuzuia kuzama na kuwezesha maadhimisho ya siku kuzuia kuzama Duniani tarehe 25 Julai kila mwaka. Pia WHO itaanzisha ushirikiano wa kimataifa wa kuzuia kuzama na mashirika ya mfumo wa umoja wa mataifa, washirika wa maendeleo wa kimataifa na mashirika yasiyo ya kiserikali. Ili kuelewa vyema mzigo na kweli wa athari za kuzama, Azimio hilo linaiomba zaidi WHO kuandaa ripoti ya hali ya kimataifa kuhusu kuzuia kuzama.
translations.reference: encourage the attraction, training, recruitment and retention of Indigenous Peoples as health workers taking into account the traditional knowledge and practices. Related document: A76/A/CONF./1 - Health of Indigenous Peoples First-ever resolution to accelerate action on drowning preventionThe delegates at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly agreed a resolution to accelerate action on global drowning prevention. The resolution requests Member States to assess their national drowning situation and to develop and implement multisectoral drowning prevention programmes. Drowning causes 236 000 deaths every year. It is a leading global cause of injury-related child deaths. Over the past decade, 2.5 million people died from drowning, and over 90% of those occurred in low- and middle-income countries. At the invitation of the United Nations (UN) General Assembly, WHO will coordinate actions within the UN system on drowning prevention and facilitate the observance of World Drowning Prevention Day on 25 July each year. WHO will also set up a Global Alliance for Drowning Prevention with organizations of the UN system, international development partners and nongovernmental organizations. To better understand the true burden and impact of drowning, the resolution further requests WHO to prepare a global status report on drowning prevention.
translations.hypothesis: Promoting attraction, training, employment, and retention of indigenous people as healthcare providers, while respecting their knowledge and traditional practices. Relevant document: A76/A/ CONF./ 1 - Health of Indigenous Peoples. The first resolution to accelerate measures to prevent drowning. Delegates at the 76th World Health Assembly agreed on a resolution to accelerate measures to prevent drowning globally. The resolution calls on member states to assess their national drowning rates and to develop and implement multi-sectoral drowning prevention plans. Drowning causes 236,000 deaths each year. It is a major global cause of child mortality related to injury. Over the past decade, 2.5 million people have died from drowning, with over 90% of these deaths occurring in low- and middle-income countries. In response to a call from the United Nations General Assembly, WHO will coordinate action within the UN system on drowning prevention and will facilitate the observance of World Drowning Day on 25 July each year. WHO will also establish a global drowning prevention partnership with UN system agencies, international development partners, and non-governmental organizations. To better understand the burden and reality of drowning, the resolution calls on WHO to prepare a global status report on drowning.

translations.source: Hati inayohusiana: EB 152/CONF./2- Kuharakisha hatua ya kuzuia kuzama Duniani Soma zaidi kuhusu Azimio hilo Siku ya kuzuia kuzama Duniani 2023 Nchi wanachama zinahamasishana nyuma ya Azimio la kukabilina na kemikali, taka na uchafuzi wa mazingira Nchi wanachama zilikaribisha Azimio linaloshughulikia viashiria vya mzingira, ikiwa ni pamoja na usimamizi wa kemkali na taka. Jitihada hizo zinaweza kusaidia kuzuia hadi moja ya tano ya vifo vyote vinavyohusiana na kujiua kutokana na viuatilifu hatari. Nchi wanachama zilihimizwa kuimarisha utendaji wa mikakati iliyopo ya WHO ikijumuisha utekelezaji wa muongozo wa kemikali wa WHO unaoainisha majukumu muhimu ya sekta ya afya katika kutekeleza mkakati wa kudhibiti kemikali za kimataifa. Pia Azimio linahimiza wizara za afya kushiriki katika juhudi za kuandaa mapendekezo ya jopo la sera za sayansi kati ya serikali na mazungumzo ya makataba wa kukomesha uchafuzi wa plastiki. Azimio hilo lilimtaka mkurugenzi mkuu kuchukua hatua kadhaa, zikiwemo: Kuchapisha ripoti juu ya athari za kiafya za kemikali, takataka na uchafuzi wa mazingira kutoka kwa mtazamo wa "Afya Moja"; Kusasisha waraka wa 2012 kuhusu hali ya sayansi ya Endocrine kuharibu kemikali kwa pamoja na mpango wa mazingira wa umoja wa mataifa; na
translations.reference: Related document: EB152/CONF./2 - Accelerating action on global drowning prevention Read more about the resolution World Drowning Prevention Day 2023 Member States mobilize behind resolution to tackle chemicals, waste and pollution Member States welcomed the resolution addressing environmental determinants, including management of chemicals and waste. The efforts can help prevent up to one fifth of all suicide-related deaths from highly hazardous pesticides. Member States were urged to bolster the implementation of existing WHO strategies including implementation of the WHO Chemicals Roadmap which outlines key roles of the health sector in implementing the Strategic Approach to International Chemicals Management. The resolution also encourages the Ministries of Health to engage in efforts to prepare proposals for an intergovernmental science policy panel and negotiations for a treaty to end plastic pollution. The resolution called upon the Director-General to undertake several actions, including: publishing a report on the health implications of chemicals, waste and pollution from a "One Health" perspective; updating the 2012 document on the State of the Science of Endocrine Disrupting Chemicals jointly with the United Nations Environment Programme; and
translations.hypothesis: Document related: EB 152/CONF./2 - Accelerating the Implementation of the Global Coral Reef Monitoring System. Read more about the Resolution on World Coral Reef Day 2023. Member states endorse a resolution to address chemical, waste, and environmental pollution. Member states welcome a resolution that addresses environmental indicators, including chemical and waste management. These efforts could help prevent up to one in five deaths related to suicide due to environmental factors. Member states are encouraged to strengthen the implementation of the WHO's existing strategy, including the implementation of the WHO's chemical management plan, which outlines key roles for the health sector in implementing the international chemical control strategy. The resolution also encourages health ministries to participate in efforts to develop recommendations from the science policy interface between governments and negotiations on agreements to eliminate plastic pollution. The resolution requested the Director-General to take several steps, including: Publishing a report on the health impacts of chemicals, waste, and environmental pollution from a "One Health" perspective; Updating the 2012 report on the state of endocrine-disrupting chemicals, in collaboration with the United Nations Environment Programme; and

translations.source: Kusaidia nchi katika kuendeleza programu za kitaifa au kikanda za uchunguzi wa binadamu kwa kemikali zinazohusika. Hati inayohusiana: A76/A/ CONF./ 2 - Athari za kemikali, takataka na uchafuziwa mazingira kwa Afya ya Binadamu WHO inafanya kazi juu ya usalama wa kemikali na afya kufikia ustawi: Nchi wanachama za mfumo wa kimataifa zilikubali kupitisha "mfumo wa kitaifa wa kuunganisha ustawi katika afya ya umma kwa kutumia mbinu ya kukuza afya", ambayo inajitahidi kuwawezesha watu wote kustawi na kufikia uwezo wao kamili wa afya ya kimwili na kiakili katika maisha yao yote na katika vizazi vyote. Mfumo wa kimataifa unapendekeza maelekezo sita muhimu ya kimkakati ambayo yanazingatia: Huduma ya afya kwa wote, Uchumi sawa, mifumo ya kidijitali kuwezesha afya, na kupima na kufuatilia ustawi. Mfumo unapendekeza ushirikiano wa karibu na sekta zilizo nje ya afya ili kukuza na kulind afya. Inatumika kama muongozo kwa washikadau wote kushiriki kwa njia iliyounganishwa na ilivyoratibiwa kwa madhumuni ya pamoja: kukuza afya ya watu na sayari kwa njia endelevu na ya usawa. Kukuza afya ya watu na sayari kwa njia endelevu na yenye usawa. Baraza linamtaka mkurugenzi mkuu kuripoti kkuhusu utekelezaji wa mfumo wa kimataifa wa mwaka 2024, 2026 na 2031. Hati inayohusiana: A76/ 7 Add.2 - Ustawi na kukuza Afya
translations.reference: supporting countries in developing national or regional human biomonitoring programmes for chemicals of concern. Related document: A76/A/CONF./2 - The impact of chemicals, waste and pollution on human health WHO’s work on chemical safety and health Achieving well-being: a global frameworkMember States agreed to adopt the "Global framework for integrating well-being into public health utilizing a health promotion approach", which strives to enable all people to flourish and achieve their full physical and mental health potential throughout their lives and across generations. The Global framework recommends six key strategic directions that focus on: universal health coverage, equitable economies, protecting the planet, social protection systems, digital systems to enable health, and measuring and monitoring well-being. The framework proposes close collaboration with sectors outside the health sector to promote and protect health. It serves as a guide for all stakeholders to engage in a coherent and coordinated manner around a common purpose: promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. promoting the health of people and planet in a sustainable and equitable manner. The Assembly requests the Director-General to report on implementation of the global framework in 2024, 2026 and 2031. Related document: A76/7 Add.2 - Well-being and health promotion
translations.hypothesis: Assisting countries in developing national or regional chemical safety programs, including those related to human exposure. Relevant document: A76/A/CONF./2 - Chemical hazards, waste, and environmental pollution and their impact on human health. WHO is working to promote chemical safety and health to achieve well-being: Member states of the international system have agreed to adopt a "national framework for integrating well-being into public health," which aims to enable all people to thrive and reach their full physical and mental health potential throughout their lives and across generations. The international system proposes six key strategic priorities, which include: universal health coverage, equitable health systems, digital technologies to support health, and monitoring and evaluation of well-being. The system also recommends close collaboration with sectors outside of health to promote and protect health. It serves as a guide for all stakeholders to participate in a coordinated and planned manner for the common goal of promoting the health of people and the planet in a sustainable and equitable way. Promoting the health of people and the planet in a sustainable and equitable way. The Council requests the Director-General to report on the implementation of the international framework in 2024, 2026 and 2031. Relevant document: A76/7 Add.2 - Well-being and health promotion

translations.source: Azimio jipya la kuharakisha juhudi za urutubishaji wa virutubisho vya chakula Wajumbe waliidhinisha Azimio la kuongeza kasi ya juhudi za kuzuia upungufu wa virutubisho kupitia urutubishaji wa chakula. Upungufu katika hali ya vitamini na madini,, hasa ya folate, chuma, vitamini A, na Zinki huathiri 50% ya watoto wote wenye umri wa shule ya awali na 67% ya wanawake wote wa umri wa uzazi Duniani kote. Upungufu wa virutubisho unaweza kuwa na madhara makubwa, ikiwemo uti wa mgongo na Kasoro nyingine za mirija ya Neva. Urutubishaji wa chakula kwa kiwango kikubwa ni sehemu ya suluhisho. Kwa kuongezea vitamini na madini muhimu katika vyakula na vitoweo, kama vile unga wa ngano na mahindi, mchele, mafuta ya kupikia na chumvi kwa mujibu wa mifumo ya matumizi ya kitaifa na upungufu, nchi zinaweza kurekebisha na kuzuia zaidi upungufu wa virutubisho unaonekana. Azimio hilo linahimiza nchi wanachma kubuni sera kuhusu urutubishaji wa chakula kwa virutubishi vidogo na/ au kuongeza, na kuzingatia njia za kuimarisha mifumo ya ufadhili na ufuatiliaji. Azimio hilo lilikubaliwa chini ya mwamvuli wa ripoti ya muongo wa utekelezaji wa umoja wa mataifa kuhusu Lishe (2016-2025). Hati inayohusiana: EB 152/CONF./5 Kuongeza kasi ya juhudi za kuzuia upungufu wa virutubisho na madhara yake, ikiwemo uti wa mgongo na kasoro nyingine za mirija ya Neva kupitia urutubishaji wa chakula salama na bora Viunganishi vinavyohusiana
translations.reference: New resolution to accelerate efforts on food micronutrient fortification The delegates approved a resolution on accelerating efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through safe and effective food fortification. Deficiencies in vitamin and mineral status, particularly of folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all preschool aged children and 67% of all women of reproductive age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects.Large scale food fortification is part of the solution. By adding essential vitamins and minerals to staple foods and condiments, such as wheat and maize flours, rice, cooking oil and salt in accordance with national consumption patterns and deficiencies, countries can correct and further prevent a demonstrated micronutrient deficiency. The resolution urges Member States to develop policies on food fortification with micronutrients and/or supplementation, and to consider ways of strengthening financing and monitoring mechanisms. The resolution was agreed under the umbrella of the United Nations Decade of Action on Nutrition (2016-2025) report. Related document: EB152/CONF./5 Accelerating efforts for preventing micronutrient deficiencies and their consequences, including spina bifida and other neural tube defects, through safe and effective food fortification Related links
translations.hypothesis: A new resolution has been adopted to accelerate efforts to fortify food with essential nutrients. The resolution aims to increase efforts to prevent micronutrient deficiencies through food fortification. Micronutrient deficiencies, particularly in folate, iron, vitamin A, and zinc, affect 50% of all children in primary school and 67% of all women of childbearing age worldwide. Micronutrient deficiencies can have serious consequences, including stunted growth and other neurological problems. Food fortification is a key part of the solution. By adding essential vitamins and minerals to foods such as wheat flour, rice, cooking oil, and salt, countries can help to correct and prevent micronutrient deficiencies. The resolution encourages countries to develop policies on food fortification, with a focus on small and/or vulnerable populations, and to strengthen monitoring and evaluation systems. The resolution was adopted under the umbrella of the United Nations' Global Action Plan on Nutrition (2016-2025). Relevant document: EB 152/CONF./5.

translations.source: Soma zaidi kuhusu Azimio hilo WHO inafaya kazi ya kuimarisha chakula Nchi wanachama zimeidhinisha hatua za kukabiliana na dawa zisizo na viwango na zilizounganishwa Tarehe 27, Mei, Bunge la Sabini na sita la Afya Duniani liliidhinisha Azimio la kufanya mapitio huru ya mfumo wa nchi wanachama (MSM) kwa matukio, Dawa Duni, na zilizoghushiwa, na kutilia mkazo wito wa mkurugenzi mkuu wa Januari 2023 wa kuongeza umakini na hatua katika eneo hili muhimu. Uhakiki utaanzishwa katika kikao kijacho cha kamati ya uongozi ya MSM baadae mwaka 2023. Baraza lilianzisha MSM mwaka 2012 ili kukabiliana na bidhaa za matibabu duni na potofu kutoka kwa mtazamo wa afya ya umma na kwa njia ya uwazi na inayojumuisha wote. Lengo la MSM ni kulinda afya ya umma na kukuza upatikanaji wa bidhaa za matibabu za bei nafuu, salama, zinazofaa na bora. Zikibainisha matukio ya hivi karibuni ya dawa zilizochafuliwa zilizopatikana na hiyo zilisisitiza usaidizi unaohitajika ili kuboresha uwezo wao wa kuzuia, kugundua na kukubaliana na bidhaa duni na potofu kupitia upatikanaji wa teknolojia zinazofaa, ikiwemo vifaa vya kupimia maabara, pamoja na uangalizi ulioboresha kwenye masoko na utangazaji usio rasmi, na uuzaji wa mtandaoni wa bidhaa za matibabu. Nchi wanachama zilitoa wito wa kukumbatia na kutekeleza teknolojia ya kufuatilia na kugundua kwa ajili ya kudhibiti na ufuatiliaji wa soko, vilevile hitaji la ushirikiano rasmi na washirika husika wa ndani na kimataifa kama vile utekelezaji wa sheria na tawala za forodha. Nchi wanachama zilihimizwa kupatana na mikataba inayolenga uboreshaji wa sheria na uwekaji vikwazo vizuizi kwa kushughulika kimakosa katika bidhaa za kimatibabu zilizo duni na zilizoghushiwa.
translations.reference: Read more about the resolution WHO's work on food fortification Member States approve actions to counter substandard and falsified medicines On 27 May, the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly approved a resolution to conduct an independent review of the Member State Mechanism (MSM) for incidents, substandard and falsified medicines, reinforcing the Director-General’s January 2023 call for increased vigilance and action in this important area. The review will be initiated at the next MSM steering committee meeting later in 2023. The Assembly established the MSM in 2012 to tackle substandard and falsified medical products from a public health perspective and in a transparent and inclusive way. The goal of the MSM is to protect public health and promote access to affordable, safe, efficacious and quality medical products. Noting recent events of contaminated medicines found on various markets and related preventable deaths, Member States emphasized the support needed to improve their capacity to prevent, detect and respond to substandard and falsified medical products through access to appropriate technologies, including laboratory testing facilities, along with improved oversight on informal markets and advertising, and the online sale of medical products. Member States called for embracing and implementing track and trace technologies for market control and surveillance, as well as the need for formal collaboration by relevant local and global partners such as law enforcement and customs administrations. Member States were encouraged to align with conventions aimed at improvement of legislation and imposition of prohibitive sanctions for wrongful dealing in substandard and falsified medical products.
translations.hypothesis: Read more about this WHO resolution, which aims to strengthen food safety. Member states have endorsed measures to address substandard and falsified medicines. On May 27, the seventy-sixth World Health Assembly adopted a resolution to conduct a comprehensive review of the World Health Organization's (WHO) activities in the area of substandard and falsified medicines, and to call on the Director-General to give greater attention and resources to this important area. The review will be conducted at the next meeting of the WHO's Executive Board in late 2023. The WHO established the International Regulatory Cooperation Expert Group (IRCEG) in 2012 to address the issue of substandard and falsified medicines from a public health perspective, in a transparent and inclusive manner. The goal of IRCEG is to protect public health and to promote access to affordable, safe, effective, and quality medicines. In a recent report, IRCEG highlighted the need for increased efforts to improve the detection and prevention of substandard and falsified medicines, through the use of appropriate technologies, such as laboratory testing equipment, as well as improved surveillance in markets and online sales. Member states called for the adoption and implementation of technologies for market surveillance and traceability, as well as formal partnerships with relevant national and international stakeholders, including the enforcement of laws and regulations related to the import and export of medicines. Member states were also urged to comply with agreements aimed at strengthening legal frameworks and imposing sanctions on those involved in the production and distribution of substandard and falsified medicines.

translations.source: Sekretarieti inaombwa kutoa taarifa ya maendeleo katika eneo hili kwa halmashauri kuu mwaka 2025. Hati inayohusiana: EB 152/ 9 - Bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na za kughushi Viunganishi vinavyohusiana Uratibu wa nchi mwanachama wa WHO kuhusu matukio, bidhaa za matibabu zisizo na viwango na za kughushi Kazi ya WHO juu ya bidhaa za matibabu duni na za kughushi Azimio la kwanza kabisa juu ya sayansi ya tabia kwa afya bora limepitishwa Leo nchi wanachama zimepitisha sayansi ya tabia kwa ajili ya utatuzi bora wa afya huku nchi wanachama zikionyesha maafikiano mapana kuhusu hitaji la kuunganisha kwa utaratibu nadharia ya asayansi ya tabia, njia na mbinu katika mada zote za afya na kazi za afya ya umma. Azimio hilo linahimiza nchi wanachama kutambua jukumu la sayansi ya tabia katika kufikia matokeo bora ya afya, kutambua fursa za kuongezeka kwa matumizi na, kuanzisha kazi na vitengo vya kuzalisha na kitafsiri ushahidi ili kufahamisha sera na programu. Pia linamuomba mkurugenzi mkuu kujumuisha matumizi ya sayansi ya tabia ndani ya shirika na kutoa usaidizi kwa nchi wanachama kupitia uundaji wa muongozo na utoaji wa usaidizi wa kiufundi. Nchi wanachama zilitambua mafanikio ya mpango wa sayansi ya tabia kwa afya bora unaoongozwa na sekretarieti, ziliipongeza Malaysia, mdhamini wa Azimio hilo, vilevile nchi nyingine 19 zilizojiunga kama ufadhili wenza, na kumshukuru mkurugenzi mkuu kwa ripoti hiyo.
translations.reference: The Secretariat is requested to report on progress in this area to the Executive Board in 2025. Related document: EB152/9 - Substandard and falsified medical products Related links WHO Member State Mechanism on incidents, substandard and falsified medical products WHO's work on substandard and falsified medical products First ever resolution on behavioural sciences for better health is adopted Today Member States adopted the behavioural sciences for better health resolution with Member States showing broad consensus towards the need for integrating systematically behavioural science theory, methods and approaches across health topics and public health functions. The resolution urges Member States to acknowledge the role of behavioural science in achieving better health outcomes, to identify opportunities for increased use and, to establish functions and units for generating and translating evidence to inform policies and programmes. It also requests the Director-General to mainstream the use of behavioural science within the organization and to provide support to Member States through the development of guidance and the provision of technical assistance. Member States recognized the achievement of the Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative led by the secretariat, congratulated Malaysia, sponsor of the resolution, as well as the other 19 countries that joined as co-sponsors, and thanked the Director-General for the report.
translations.hypothesis: The Secretariat is requested to provide a report on progress in this area to the World Health Assembly in 2025. Relevant documents: EB 152/9 - Counterfeit and substandard medical products Relevant organizations WHO coordination mechanism for counterfeit and substandard medical products WHO work on substandard and counterfeit medical products First resolution on behavioral science for better health has been passed Today, member states have adopted behavioral science for better health, with broad agreement on the need to integrate behavioral science theory, methods, and approaches across all health and public health areas. The resolution encourages member states to recognize the role of behavioral science in achieving better health outcomes, to identify opportunities for scaling up, and to establish research and evidence-generation activities. It also requests the Director-General to integrate the use of behavioral science into the organization and to provide support to member states through the development of guidelines and technical assistance. Member states recognized the success of the Secretariat-led behavioral science for better health program, praised Malaysia, the sponsor of the resolution, as well as the other 19 countries that joined as co-sponsors, and thanked the Director-General for the report.

translations.source: Walisisitiza umuhimu wa kujenga uwezo katika eneo hili, hususani katika ofisi za kikanda, na kuunda hifadhi ya ushahidi na ushirikiano kati ya sekta, zikiwemo vyuo na sekta binafsi. Hati zinazohusiana: EB 152 (23) - sayansi ya tabia kwa afya bora. EB 152/ 25 - Sayansi ya tabia kwa afya bora, ripoti ya mkurugenzi mkuu. Sayansi ya tabia kwa mpango wa afya bora vipengee vilivyotajwa hapo juu vilijadiliwa kama sehemu ya waraka A76/7 Rev.1 - Ripoti iliyounganishwa na Mkurugenzi Mkuu.
translations.reference: They stressed the importance of building capacity in this area, particularly in regional offices, and of creating a repository of evidence and synergies between sectors, including with academia and the private sector. Related documents: EB152(23) - Behavioural sciences for better health EB152/25 - Behavioural sciences for better health, Report by the Director-General Behavioural Sciences for Better Health Initiative The above items were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General.
translations.hypothesis: We emphasized the importance of building capacity in this area, particularly in regional offices, and creating a platform for evidence and collaboration between the public and private sectors, including universities. Relevant documents: EB 152 (23) - Behavioral science for better health. EB 152/ 25 - Behavioral science for better health, report by the Director-General. The above-mentioned elements of behavioral science for better health were discussed as part of document A76/7 Rev.1 - Report prepared by the Director-General.

translations.source: Mkutano wa Sabini na sita wa afya Duniani - Taarifa ya kila siku: 27 Mei 2023. Mkakati wa kwanza wa kimataifa kuhusu kuzuia na kudhibiti maambukizi, mkutano wa afya Ulimwenguni umekubaliana leo kuhusu mkakati wa kwanza kabisa wa kimataifa wa kuzuia na kudhibiti maambukizi (IPC), ambao unatokana karibu miongo miwili ya juhudi zinazoongozwa na WHO na washirika. Mkakati huo unazipa nchi wanachama maelekezo ya kimkakati ya kupunguza kwa kiasi kikubwa hatari inayoendelea ya magonjwa yanayohusiana na huduma ya afya (HAIs), ikiwemo yale ambayo yanaonyesha ukinzani wa viua vijasumu. HAIs ni miongoni mwa matukio mabaya ya mara kwa mara yanatokea katika muktadha wa utoaji wa huduma za afya. Janga la UVIKO-19 na milipuko ya hivi karibuni ya magonjwa kama vile virusi vya Ebola, ugonjwa wa kupumua huko mashariki ya kati na virusi vya sudani vimefichua dhahiri mapungufu yaliyopo katika programu za IPC katika nchi zote. Mkakati unaweka dira wazi: Ifikapo 2030, kila mtu anaepata au kutoa huduma ya afya yupo salama kutokana na maambukizi yanayohusiana. Malengo yake makuu matatu ni: Kuzuia maambukizi katika huduma ya afya; kuchukua hatua ili kuhakikisha mipango ya IPC ipo na inatekelezwa; na kuratibu shughuli za IPC na maeneo na sekta nyinginezo. Mkakati unalenga katika mazingira yoyote ambapo huduma ya afya inatolewa, katika mfumo wa afya; inatokana na kanuni ya utunzaji safi na salama kama sehemu ya msingi ya haki ya afya, ambayo inaendeshwa na usawa, na ambayo inapaswa kuhakikisha uwajibikaji na uendelevu. Mkakati wa kimataifa wa IPC utakamilishwa na, na kutumiwa pamoja na, mpango wa utekelezaji wa kimataifa unaohusishwa na mfumo wa ufuatiliaji, ambao utatengenezwa mwaka 2023- 2024. Hati inayohusiana EB 152/9- Rasimu ya mkakati wa kimataifa juu ya kuzuia na kudhibiti maambukizi, muhtasari wa mtendaji ripoti ya mkurugenzi mkuu
translations.reference: Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly – Daily update: 27 May 2023 First global strategy on infection prevention and controlThe World Health Assembly agreed today on the first-ever global strategy on infection prevention and control (IPC), which builds on almost two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides Member States with strategic directions to substantially reduce the ongoing risk of health care-associated infections (HAIs), including those that exhibit antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most frequent adverse events occurring in the context of health service delivery. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent major disease outbreaks such as Ebola virus disease, the Middle East respiratory syndrome and the Sudan virus disease have clearly exposed the existing gaps in IPC programmes in all countries. The strategy sets a clear vision: by 2030, everyone accessing or providing health care is safe from associated infections. Its three key objectives are: to prevent infection in health care; act to ensure IPC programmes are in place and implemented; and coordinate IPC activities with other areas and sectors. The strategy is focused on any setting where health care is delivered, across the health system; it is based on the principle of clean and safe care as a fundamental component of the right to health, which is equity driven, and which should ensure accountability and sustainability. The global IPC strategy will be complemented by, and used in conjunction with, an associated global action plan and monitoring framework, that will be developed in 2023–2024. Related document EB152/9 - Draft global strategy on infection prevention and control, Executive summary Report by the Director-General
translations.hypothesis: Sixty-Seventh World Health Assembly - Daily Briefing: May 27, 2023. Today, the World Health Assembly has agreed on the first ever global strategy to prevent and control health-associated infections (HAIs), which builds on nearly two decades of efforts led by WHO and partners. The strategy provides member states with strategic guidance to significantly reduce the ongoing risk of HAIs, including those caused by antimicrobial resistance. HAIs are among the most common and serious adverse events in healthcare settings. The COVID-19 pandemic and recent outbreaks of diseases such as the Eastern Mediterranean respiratory virus and the Sudanese virus have highlighted the existing gaps in IPC programs in all countries. The strategy sets a clear direction: By 2030, everyone who receives or provides healthcare should be free from HAIs. Its three main goals are: to prevent HAIs in healthcare settings; to take action to ensure that IPC plans are in place and implemented; and to coordinate IPC activities with other areas and sectors. The strategy applies in any setting where healthcare is provided, within the health system; it is based on the principles of clean and safe care as a fundamental component of the right to health, which is equitable and sustainable. The global IPC strategy will be completed and implemented alongside an international implementation plan, which will be developed in 2023-2024. Document related to EB 152/9 - Draft of a global strategy on the prevention and control of health-associated infections, executive summary of the Director-General's report.

translations.source: Kazi ya WHO kuhusu kuzuia na kudhibiti maambukizi Azimio la kihistoria linatayarisha njia ya kuimarisha urekebishaji katika mifumo ya afya leo. Baraza la afya la ulimwenguni limekubali Azimio muhimu kuhusu kuimarisha urekebishaji katika mifumo ya afya. Huduma za urekebishaji zina jukumu muhimu katika kuhakikisha kufurahia haki za binadamu ikiwa ni pamoja na kiwango cha juu kinachoweza kufikiwa cha afya ya kimwili na kiakili. Pia inakuza afya ya uzazi na jinsia, na inatambua haki ya kufanya kazi na haki ya kupata elimu. Azimio hili muhimu linalenga kutatua changamoto katika ukarabati kama vile haja ya: Kuongeza ufahamu wa ukarabati wakati wa kuweka vipaumbele vya afya na ajenda za utafiti, ugawaji wa rasilimali, kukuza ushirikiano na kuwezesha uhamisho wa teknolojia.; Kuhakikisha nchi zina vifaa vya kutosha kukabiliana na ongezeko la ghafla la mahitaji ya ukarabati ikijumuisha teknolojia ya usaidizi kutokana na dharura za kiafya.; Kuhakikisha watu walio katika mazingira magumu na walio katika mazingira hatarishi wanapata huduma nafuu, bora na zinazofaa na ukarabati ikiwa ni pamoja na teknolojia saidizi; Kuepuka gharama kubwa za nje kwa watu kupata huduma za ukarabati na teknolojia saidizi ambayo inaweza kusababisha ugumu wa kifedha; na Kushughulikia kiwango cha sasa kisichotosha cha wafanyakazi wa ukarabati kuhudumia mahitaji ya watu. Azimio hilo linaorodhesha anuwai ya hatua zinazopaswa kuchukuliwa na sekretarieti ya WHO kama vile:
translations.reference: WHO's work on infection prevention and control Historic resolution paves the way for strengthening rehabilitation in health systemsToday the World Health Assembly agreed a landmark resolution on strengthening rehabilitation in health systems. Rehabilitation services play a key role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights including the highest attainable standard of physical and mental health. It also promotes sexual and reproductive health, and recognizes the right to work and the right to education. This landmark resolution aims to address the challenges in rehabilitation such as the need to: increase awareness of rehabilitation when setting health priorities and research agendas, allocating resources, promoting cooperation and enabling technology transfer; ensure countries are better equipped to respond to the sudden increase in rehabilitation needs including assistive technology due to health emergencies; ensure persons in marginalized and vulnerable situations have access to affordable, quality and appropriate rehabilitation services including assistive technology; avoid high out-of-pocket costs for people to access rehabilitation services and assistive technology that can cause financial hardships; and address the current insufficient level of rehabilitation workforce to serve the needs of the population. The resolution lists a range of actions to be taken by the WHO Secretariat such as:
translations.hypothesis: WHO's work on preventing and controlling infections: A historic resolution paves the way for strengthening health systems today. The World Health Assembly has adopted a crucial resolution on strengthening health systems. Health systems play a vital role in ensuring the enjoyment of human rights, including a high level of physical and mental health. They also promote reproductive and gender health, and recognize the right to work and the right to education. This important resolution addresses challenges in health system strengthening, such as: increasing awareness of health system strengthening during health priorities and research agendas, resource allocation, promoting collaboration, and facilitating technology transfer; ensuring that countries have sufficient resources to respond to a sudden increase in health system needs, including emergency technologies; ensuring that vulnerable populations and those in high-risk settings have access to quality, appropriate, and timely health system services, including emergency technologies; avoiding costly external costs for people to access health system services and technologies; and addressing the current shortage of health workers to meet the needs of the population. The resolution outlines a range of actions that the WHO Secretariat should take, such as:

translations.source: Kuchapisha ripoti ya msingi kufikia mwishoni mwa 2026 yenye taarifa juu ya uwezo wa nchi wanachama kujibu mahitaji ya urekebishaji; Kuendeleza shabaha na viashirio kwa ajili ya utoaji wa huduma bora za ukarabati ifikapo 2030; Kuhakikisha rasilimali zinazofaa zimelengwa katika WHO kusaidia nchi wanachama katika kutekeleza muongozo wa kiufundi na rasilimali; na kusaidia nchi wanachama kujumuisha urekebishaji na teknolojia ya usaidizi katika maandalizi yao ya dharura na mpango wa kukabiliana. Sekretarieti ya WHO itatoa taarifa kuhusu maendeleo katika utekelezaji wa Azimio hili kwa Baraza la Afya mwaka 2026, 2028 na 2030. Hati inayohusiana: EB 152/ 8 - Kuimarisha ukarabati katika mfumo ya afya, ripoti ya mkurugenzi mkuu. Kiunganishi kinachohusiana Kazi ya WHO kuhusu ukarabati Mambo yaliyopo hapo juu yalijadiliwa kama sehemu ya waraka A76/7 Rev.1 - Ripoti jumuishi ya mkurugenzi mkuu. Azimio la kuimarisha uwezo wa uchunguzi
translations.reference: publishing a baseline report by the end of 2026 with information on the capacity of Member States to respond to rehabilitation needs; developing targets and indicators for effective coverage of rehabilitation services by 2030; ensuring appropriate resources are allocated at WHO to support Member States in implementing technical guidance and resources; and supporting Member States to integrate rehabilitation and assistive technology in their emergency preparedness and response plan. The WHO Secretariat will report on progress in the implementation of this resolution to the Health Assembly in 2026, 2028 and 2030. Related document: EB152/8 - Strengthening rehabilitation in health systems, Report by the Director-General Related link WHO's work on rehabilitation The items above were discussed as part of the document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report by the Director-General. Resolution on strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis: Publishing a foundational report by the end of 2026 containing information on the capacity of member countries to meet the needs for rehabilitation; Developing benchmarks and indicators for the provision of high-quality rehabilitation services by 2030; Ensuring that appropriate resources are allocated to WHO to support member countries in implementing technical guidance and resources; and assisting member countries in integrating rehabilitation and assistive technology into their emergency preparedness and response plans. The WHO Secretariat will provide reports on progress in the implementation of this resolution to the Health Assembly in 2026, 2028, and 2030. Relevant documents: EB 152/8 - Strengthening rehabilitation in the health system, report of the Director-General. Related documents: WHO work on rehabilitation. The above points were discussed as part of document A76/7 Rev.1 - Consolidated report of the Director-General. Resolution on strengthening diagnostic capacity.

translations.source: Tarehe 26 Mei nchi wanachama ziliidhinisha Azimio la kuimarisha uwezo wa uchunguzi katika nchi na kuboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za uchunguzi. Azimio pana linatambua kuwa huduma za uchunguzi ni muhimu kwa ajili ya kuzuia, ufuatiliaji na uchunguzi, udhibiti wa visa, ufuatiliaji na matibabu ya magonjwa ya kuambukiza, yasiyo ya kuambukiza, yaliyopuuzwa na ya kawaida, majeraha na ulemavu. Uchunguzi huruhusu utambuzi sahihi wa magonjwa, na hivyo kuanzishwa kwa matibabu sahihi kwa matokeo bora ya kiafya kwa wakati unaofaa. Azimio hilo linazingatia wigo kamili wa "Uchunguzi", hivyo kujumuisha vipimo vya maabara vya "in vitro" m.f vipimo vya haraka vya uchunguzi na mmenyuko wa mnyororo wa Polymerase (PCR) na uchunguzi wa "non in vitro" m.f picha au vifaa vya kupima shinikizo la damu. Linashughulikia hatua za utafiti na maendeleo, utengenezaji (ikijumuishi uzalishaji wa ndani na uhamishaji wa teknolojia), udhibiti, uteuzi na ununuzi, uhamishaji, utetezi na kushughulikia vizuizi vya ufikiaji kwa ujumla. Utekelezaji wa Azimio hilo utajenga na kupanua kasi ya awali na ya sasa katika ngazi tatu za WHO ili kuzisaidia nchi kuboresha upatikanaji wa huduma za uchunguzi. Sekretarieti imeombwa kutoa taarifa ya maendeleo ya utekelezaji mwaka 2025. Hati inayohusiana EB 152/6 Kuimarisha uwezo wa uchunguzi
translations.reference: On 26 May Member States endorsed a resolution to strengthen diagnostics capacity in countres and to improve access to diagnostic services. The broad ranging resolution recognizes that diagnostic services are vital for the prevention, surveillance, diagnosis, case management, monitoring and treatment of communicable, noncommunicable, neglected tropical and rare diseases, injuries, and disabilities. Diagnostics allow for the precise identification of diseases, and therefore the timely initiation of the correct treatments for better health outcomes. The resolution considers the full spectrum of “diagnostics”, thus including both “in vitro” laboratory tests e.g. rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR) and “non in vitro” diagnostics e.g. imaging or blood pressure measurement devices. It covers actions for research and development, manufacturing (including local production and technology transfer), regulation, selection and procurement, awareness, advocacy and addressing access barriers in general. The implementation of the resolution will build and expand on previous and current work at three levels of WHO to help countries improve access to diagnostic services. The Secretariat is requested to report on implementation progress in 2025. Related document EB152/6 Strengthening diagnostics capacity
translations.hypothesis: On May 26, member states endorsed a resolution to strengthen diagnostic capabilities within countries and improve access to diagnostic services. The resolution recognizes that diagnostic services are essential for preventing, detecting, and managing diseases, including infectious, non-communicable, neglected, and common diseases, as well as injuries and disabilities. Accurate diagnosis through effective diagnostic services enables appropriate treatment, leading to better health outcomes in a timely manner. The resolution encompasses the full spectrum of "diagnostics," including both "in vitro" and "in vivo" tests, such as rapid diagnostic tests and polymerase chain reaction (PCR), as well as "in vivo" tests such as imaging and blood pressure monitoring devices. It addresses research and development, manufacturing (including local production and technology transfer), quality assurance, procurement, distribution, advocacy, and addressing barriers to access. The implementation of this resolution will build on and expand existing efforts within the three WHO pillars to help countries improve access to diagnostic services. The Secretariat is requested to provide a progress report on the implementation of the resolution by 2025. Related document: EB 152/6 Strengthening diagnostic capacity

translations.source: Tuzo za michango bora kwa afya ya umma ilitolewa wakati wa mkutano wa sabini na Sita wa afya Duniani. Wakati wa hafla ya kusisimua katika mkutano wa Sabini na Sita wa afya Ulimwenguni huko Jeniva, tuzo zilitolewa kwa watu na taasisi kutoka kote Ulimwenguni kwa michango yao bora kwa afya ya umma. "Zawadi na Tuzo za heshima za afya ya umma za 2023 zinaadhimisha watu ambao wametoa mchango mkubwa kwa afya ya kimataifa, na ambazo kazi yao imeenea zaidi ya wito wa kawaida", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Wakati ambapo unakabiliwa na changamoto nyingi, kila mmoja ni msukumo na ukumbusho wa maendeleo ambayo yanaweza kufanywa kuboresha afya na ustawi kwa wote" Wito wa uteuzi wa wagombeaji kwa kila zawadi hutumwa kila mwaka baada ya kufungwa kwa mkutano wa afya Duniani. Uteuzi unaweza kufanywa na tawala za kitaifa za afya za nchi mwanachama wa WHO na mpokeaji yeyote wa zamani wa zawadi. Katika kikao chake cha 152 mnamo Januari 2023, halmashauri kuu iliteua washindi wa 2023 wa Tuzo, kwa msingi wa mapendekezo yaliyotolewa na jopo maalumu la utetezi kwa kila Tuzo. Washindi saba wamechaguliwa kusherekea jukumu na mchango wao wa kipekee kwa afya ya umma kwa nchi zao na kimataifa. Wanatoka kanda nne za WHO. Amerika, Mashariki mwa Mediterania, Asia ya kusini- Mashariki na Pasifiki Magharibi. Washindi watano ni watu binafsi na watatu kati yao ni wanawake.
translations.reference: Awards for outstanding contributions to public health presented during the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly During a moving ceremony at the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly in Geneva, awards were presented to persons and institutions from around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. “The 2023 public health prizes and awards celebrate people who have made a significant contribution to global health, and whose work has extended far beyond the call of normal duty,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “At a time when the world faces many challenges, each is an inspiration and a reminder of the progress that can be made to improve health and wellbeing for all.” The call for nominations of candidates for each prize is sent out each year after closure of the World Health Assembly. Nominations can be made by national health administrations of a WHO Member State and by any former recipient of the prizes. At its 152nd session in January 2023, the Executive Board designated the 2023 winners of the prizes, on the basis of proposals made by the dedicated selection panels for each prize. The seven laureates have been selected to celebrate their unique role and contribution for public health in their countries and globally. They come from four WHO Regions – for the Americas, the Eastern-Mediterranean, South-East Asia and the Western Pacific. Five winners are individuals, and three among them are women.
translations.hypothesis: The awards for outstanding contributions to public health were presented at the 76th World Health Assembly. During the exciting event at the 76th World Health Assembly in Geneva, the awards were given to individuals and organizations from around the world for their outstanding contributions to public health. "The 2023 World Health Organization's Global Health and Development Awards recognize individuals who have made a significant contribution to global health, and whose work has gone beyond the ordinary," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. "In a time of many challenges, these awards are a reminder of the progress that can be made to improve health and well-being for all." Nominations for each award are sent out annually after the World Health Assembly closes. Nominations can be submitted by national health authorities in WHO member states, or by any previous recipient of the award. At its 152nd session in January 2023, the Executive Board selected the 2023 award winners, based on recommendations from a special panel for each award. Seven winners were selected to recognize their unique contributions to public health in their respective countries and globally. They come from four WHO regions: the Americas, Eastern Mediterranean, South-East Asia, and Western Pacific. Five of the winners are individuals, and three of them are women.

translations.source: Aidha, taasisi hizo mbili ambazo zimepokea tuzo ya 2023 zinaongozwa nawanawake. Washindi wa Tuzo za 2023 za Sasakawa Health Prize washindi wa 2023: Taasisi ya Nick Simons kutoka Nepal, na profesa Vichai Tienthavorn kutoka Thailand Taasisi ya Nick Simons ni shirika lisilo la kiserikali linalolenga kuboresha huduma za afya katika maeneo ya vijijini ya Nepali. Ikifanya kazi kwa karibu na wizara ya afya na idadi ya watu ya Nepali, taasisi imetoa mafunzo kwa wataalamu wa matibabu 7000 (kama vile wakunga, na wasaidizi wa ganzi) katika maeneo yote ya vijijini, ambao sasa wanatoa huduma zinazohitajika zaidi kwa jamii za wenyeji bila kukosekana kwa kundi la kutosha la jumla la watendaji na wataalamu. Taasisi imetoa vifaa muhimu na kutoa watumishi wa ziada wa afya ili kupunguza ukosefu wa wataalamu wa matibabu katika hospitali 44, na kuunga mkono uundaji wa mpango kazi wa huduma bora zaidi katika hospitali zaidi ya 100 za vijijini. Pia taasisi hufanya shughuli za utafiti na utetezi ili kuunda mitazamo na kuathiri sera zinazoathiri wahudumu wa afya vijijini nchini Nepali. Profes Vichai Tienthavorn ni Rais wa taasisi ya Praboromarajchanok katika wizara ya afya ya umma ya Thailand. Moja wapo ya mipango ya kitaifa ambayo ameoiongoza ni sera bunifu ya kukuza afya ili kusaidia kushughulikia thalassemia, na kazi yake ikisababishwa kupitishwa kwa sera madhubuti ya kitaifa ya kuzuia na kudhibiti thelassemia. Pia alibuni chombo madhubuti cha mawasiliano na njia ya uchunguzi ili kuwasaidia wananchi kurekebisha tabia zao ili kuzuia na kudhibiti kisukari na shinikizo la damu. Kama matokeo ya kazi hii, sera ya kitaifa ilipitishwa yenye matokeo chanya kwa mzigo wa magonjwa nchini.
translations.reference: In addition, the two institutions that have received a 2023 award are led by women. Winners of the 2023 prizes Sasakawa Health PrizeThe 2023 laureates: The Nick Simons Institute from Nepal, and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand The Nick Simons Institute is a nongovernmental organization aiming to improve health care in the rural areas of Nepal. Working closely with the Ministry of Health and Population of Nepal, the Institute has trained 7000 medical professionals (such as midwives and anaesthetic assistants) in rural areas, who now provide most-needed services to local communities in the absence of a sufficient pool of general practitioners and specialists. The Institute has supplied essential equipment and provided additional health personnel to alleviate the lack of medical professionals in 44 hospitals, and supported the design of an action plan for better quality services in more than 100 rural hospitals. The Institute also conducts research and advocacy activities to shape perceptions and influence policies that affect rural health workers in Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is President of the Praboromarajchanok Institute at the Ministry of Public Health of Thailand. One of the nation-wide initiatives he has led is an innovative health promotion policy to help address thalassemia, with his work resulting in the adoption of an effective national policy for thalassemia prevention and control. He also designed an effective communications tool and screening method to help citizens to adapt their behaviours in order to prevent and control diabetes and hypertension. As a result of this work, a national policy was adopted with significant positive impact on the country’s disease burden.
translations.hypothesis: Furthermore, the two organizations that received the 2023 award are led by women. The 2023 Sasakawa Health Prize winners are: the Nick Simons Foundation from Nepal, and Professor Vichai Tienthavorn from Thailand. The Nick Simons Foundation is a non-governmental organization dedicated to improving healthcare services in rural areas of Nepal. Working closely with the Nepali Ministry of Health and other organizations, the foundation has provided training to 7,000 healthcare professionals (including nurses and community health workers) in all rural areas, who are now providing essential services to local communities without adequate staffing. The foundation has also provided essential equipment and additional healthcare personnel to reduce the shortage of healthcare professionals in 44 hospitals, and has supported the development of a comprehensive program for improved healthcare in over 100 rural hospitals. The foundation also conducts research and advocacy to shape policies that affect healthcare providers in rural Nepal. Professor Vichai Tienthavorn is the Director of the Praboromarajchanok Institute in the Thai Ministry of Public Health. One of the national initiatives he has led is a groundbreaking program to promote health to help address thalassemia, and his work has contributed to the passage of a strong national policy to prevent and control thalassemia. He has also designed a comprehensive communication tool and a screening program to help citizens change their behavior to prevent and control diabetes and high blood pressure. As a result of this work, a national policy was passed with positive outcomes for the health of the population.

translations.source: Profesa Tienthavorn pia alichukua jukumu muhimu katika kushughulikia changamoto ya usambazaji usio sawa wa wahudumu wa afya, hususani maeneo ya mipakani na vijijini ya Thailand. Aliongoza kuanzishwa kwa ushirikiano na shule za vijijini ili kuajiri wanafunzi wa shule za upili, kuwafundisha na kuwasaidia kutulia ndani baada ya kukamilisha mtaala wa matibabu ulioharakishwa. Umoja wa Falme za Kiarabu Health Foundation Tuzo ya 2023 mshindi wa Tuzo: Dk Maria Asuncion Silvestre kutoka Ufilipino pamoja na kuwa daktari na mtafiti mashuhuri, Dk Maria Asuncion Silvetre ni mtetezi mwenye shauku ya unyonyeshaji wa maziwa ya mama pekee na mwanzilishi wa Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Afya ya Mama na Mtoto) - shirika lisilo la kiserikali la Ufilipino. DK Silvestre ametumia uzoefu wake kama mtendaji wa jamii kufanya utafiti na kubuni itifaki ya kusaidia kuboresha afya ya mama na watoto wachanga. Itifaki ya uanzilishi aliyounda inaeleza seti rahisi ya hatua zinazopaswa kufanywa na wahudumu wa afya wanaomhudumia mama wakati wa kujifungua na mtoto wake mchanga baada ya kuzaliwa. Itifaki hii, ambayo inatumika katika mipangilio yote ya rasilimali, inasaidia kuziba pengo la usawa wa afya katika utunzaji wa mama na mtoto. Kwa ushirikiano na serikali ya kitaifa ya Ufilipino pamoja na nchi 17 katika Kanda ya Pasifiki ya Magharibi ya WHO, amefanya jukumu muhimu katika kuongeza kampeni ya Kukumbatia kwanza kwa utunzaji muhimu wa waoto Wachanga Mapema- seti ya hatua rahisi zinazopendekezwa na WHO na za gharama nafuu. Muadhama Sheikh Sabah Al- Ahmad Al-Jaber Al- Sabah Tuzo ya utafiti kwa huduma za afya kwa wazee na ukuzaji wa afya Washindi wa tuzo za 2023: Kituo cha kitaifa cha kudhibiti na kuzuia magonjwa ya muda mrefu na yasiyoambukiza kutoka China; Dk Abla Mehio Sibai kutoka Lebanoni Kituo cha kitaifa cha kudhibiti na kuzuia magonjwa ya muda mrefu na yasiyoambukiza, ambacho ni sehemu ya kituo cha kudhibiti na kuzuia magonjwa cha China (CDC cha China), kimeandaa idara inayoangazia afya ya wazee tangu 2013.
translations.reference: Professor Tienthavorn also played an essential role in addressing the challenge of uneven distribution of the health workforce, especially in border and rural areas of Thailand. He led the establishment of partnerships with village schools to recruit high-school students, train them and help them settle back locally after having accomplished an accelerated medical curriculum. United Arab Emirates Health Foundation Prize2023 award laureate: Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre from the PhilippinesIn addition to being a renowned clinician and researcher, Dr Maria Asuncion Silvestre is a passionate advocate for exclusive breastfeeding and the founder of Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Health of Mother and Child) – a Philippine non-governmental organization. Dr Silvestre has leveraged her experience as a community practitioner to conduct research and design a protocol to help improve the health of mothers and newborns. The pioneering protocol she created describes a simple set of actions to be undertaken by health workers attending a mother during delivery and her newborn after birth. This protocol, which is applicable in all resource settings, helps to bridge the health equity gap in mother-and-child care. In partnership with the national government of the Philippines as well as 17 countries in the WHO Western Pacific Region, she has played a key role in scaling up the First Embrace campaign for Early Essential Newborn Care – a set of simple WHO-recommended and cost-effective interventions. His Highness Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah Prize for Research in Health Care for the Elderly and in Health Promotion 2023 award laureates: The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention from China; and Dr Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon The National Center for Chronic and Noncommunicable Disease Control and Prevention, part of the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), has hosted a department focusing on the health of older people since 2013.
translations.hypothesis: Professor Tienthavorn also played a crucial role in addressing the challenge of unequal access to healthcare, particularly in rural and remote areas of Thailand. He led the establishment of partnerships with rural schools to recruit high school students, train them in basic healthcare, and support them in pursuing careers in the field. The Arab Health Foundation Award 2023 Winner: Dr. Maria Asuncion Silvestre from the Philippines, in addition to being a renowned doctor and researcher, Dr. Maria Asuncion Silvestre is a passionate advocate for exclusive breastfeeding and the founder of Kalusugan ng Mag-Ina (Mother and Child Health) - a non-governmental organization in the Philippines. Dr. Silvestre has used her experience as a community leader to conduct research and develop guidelines to help improve the health of mothers and newborns. The guidelines she developed outline a simple set of steps that healthcare providers should follow when caring for a mother and her newborn after delivery. This guideline, which is applicable in all settings, helps to address the gap in healthcare equity for mothers and newborns. In collaboration with the Philippine national government and 17 other countries in the WHO Western Pacific region, she played a key role in promoting the WHO's "Early Steps" initiative - a set of simple, low-cost steps recommended by WHO. Sheikh Sabah Al- Ahmad Al-Jaber Al- Sabah Research Award for Health Services to the Elderly and Healthy Aging 2023 Winner: The National Center for the Control and Prevention of Chronic and Non-Communicable Diseases from China; Dr. Abla Mehio Sibai from Lebanon, The National Center for the Control and Prevention of Chronic and Non-Communicable Diseases, a part of the Chinese Center for Disease Control and Prevention (China CDC), has been developing a department focused on the health of the elderly since 2013.

translations.source: Inatoa mipango na shughuli mbalimbali zinazoendeleza uimarishaji wa afya kwa wazee, kuanzia kuelewa vyema zaidi sababu za magonjwa na vifo kupitia uchunguzi wa kitaifa wa magonjwa yasiyoambukiza, tafiti, sajili za vifo na utafiti, hadi programu maalamu za kukuza ujuzi wa kiafya naushahidi- sera za msingi. Mipango ni pamoja na zana maalumu kwa ajili ya wazee, kujenga uwezo wa wafanyakazi 2000 wa huduma ya msingi, wiki ya utetezi wa afya inayotolewa kwa wazee, na mradi wa ubunifu wa kuboresha afya ya akili na uchunguzi ya wazee zaidi ya nusu milioni kwa maeneo yote ya mijini na vijijini. Dk Abla Mehio Sibai ni profesa wa epidemiolojia na mkuu wa kitivo cha sayansi ya afya katika chuo kikuu cha Marekani Beiruti nchini Lebanoni. Ametoa mchango mkubwa kwa afya ya wazee nchini Lebanoni na kanda, akiendeleza mbinu ya kukuza uzee wenye afya. Aliongoza mpango wa ubunifu kwa kuandikisha karibu wazee 600 kila mwaka katika chuo kikuu cha wazee ndani ya chuo kikuu cha Marekani cha Beiruti, pamoja na kutoa mafunzo kwa wataalamu wa afya ya umma. Aliongoza utafiti wa mzigo wa magonjwa ulimwenguni wa WHO nchini Lebanoni (2000) na kufanya utafiti wa kwanza wa kitaifa kuhusu magonjwa yasiyoambukiza na mambo hatarishi, kuarifu utetezi, utungaji sera na upangaji wa programu kwa ajili ya kukuza afya. Kwa kuzingatia mafanikio yake mengi, Dk Sibai ameandaa "mkakati wa kitaifa kwa wazee" nchini Lebanoni (2020-2023). Dk Sibai pia aliunda mtandao wa wazee wa kiarabu na kituo cha mafunzo ya uzeeni ili kuunda uhusiano kati ya utafiti na sera katika ngazi ya kitaifa, kikanda na kimataifa. Dk LEE Jong-Wook tuzo ya ukumbusho ya afya ya umma Mshindi wa tuzo ya 2023: Dk Jorge Francisco Meneses kutoka Guatemala.
translations.reference: It delivers a broad range of programmes and activities that advance health promotion for older people, ranging from better understanding the causes of morbidity and mortality through national surveillance for noncommunicable diseases, surveys, death registries and research, to tailored programmes to promote health literacy and evidence-based policies. Initiatives include a dedicated toolkit for older people, capacity‑building of 2000 primary care staff, a health advocacy week dedicated to older people, and an innovative project to improve the mental and cognitive health of more than half a million older persons in both urban and rural areas. Dr Abla Mehio Sibai is Professor of Epidemiology and Dean of the Faculty of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made an outstanding contribution to older people’s health in Lebanon and the region, developing a holistic approach to the promotion of healthy ageing. She led the innovative initiative of enrolling close to 600 older people every year at the University for Seniors at the American University of Beirut, in addition to training public health professionals. She co-led the landmark WHO Global Burden of Disease Study in Lebanon (2000) and conducted the first national survey on noncommunicable diseases and risk factors, informing advocacy, policy-making and programme planning for health promotion. Building on her numerous achievements, Dr Sibai has developed the “National Strategy for Older Persons” in Lebanon (2020–2030). Dr Sibai also created the Arab Ageing Network and the Centre for Studies on Ageing to create links between research and policy at the national, regional and international levels. Dr LEE Jong-wook Memorial Prize for Public Health 2023 award laureate: Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses from Guatemala
translations.hypothesis: The program offers a variety of initiatives aimed at promoting healthy aging, ranging from improved understanding of the causes and risk factors of non-communicable diseases through national disease surveillance and research, to specialized programs to enhance health literacy and evidence-based policies. The initiatives include tailored tools for older adults, capacity building for 2,000 primary healthcare workers, a health promotion week for older adults, and a groundbreaking project to improve the mental health of over half a million older adults in urban and rural areas. Dr. Abla Mehio Sibai is a professor of epidemiology and director of the Department of Health Sciences at the American University of Beirut in Lebanon. She has made significant contributions to the health of older adults in Lebanon and the region, developing innovative strategies for healthy aging. She led a pioneering program to register approximately 600 older adults each year at the American University of Beirut, as well as providing training for public health professionals. She led the WHO's global burden of disease study in Lebanon (2000) and conducted the first national survey on non-communicable diseases and risk factors, informing policy development and program implementation for health promotion. Building on her many achievements, Dr. Sibai developed "a national strategy for older adults" in Lebanon (2020-2023). Dr. Sibai also created a network of Arab older adults and a center for aging research to foster collaboration between research and policy at the national, regional, and international levels. Dr. LEE Jong-Wook Public Health Prize: Winner 2023: Dr. Jorge Francisco Meneses from Guatemala.

translations.source: Baada ya kukuza ushiriki wa jamii katika huduma za hospitali kwa zaidi ya miaka 20, Dk Jorge Francisco Meneses alianzisha kamati za kijamii zinazozingatia usalama na kuridhika kwa wagonjwa, familia zao na jamii. kutokana na matokeo chanya ya kazi yake katika kuboresha ubora wa huduma kwa wagonjwa, kielelezo cha utunzaji wa kimaadili na mgonjwa alioubuni ulikuzwa katika ngazi ya kitaifa. Siku hizi, akiwa kama msimamizi wa kitaifa wa hospitali za Guatemala, amebuni mpango wa kuboresha ubora wa huduma kwa wagonjwa wa hospitlini na familia zao. Dk Meneses hivi karibuni ameratibu mradi wa majaribio wa kibunifu katika eneo la afya la Guatemala kusini ili kujenga mtandao wa kwanza wa kitaifa wa huduma ya afya, kwa kuzingatia kipaumbele kwa jamii ambazo hazijahudumiwa. Kazi yake ilisababisha kupitishwa kwa mkakati wa kitaifa wa mitandao ya huduma ya Afya jumuishi, ya kwanza huko Amerika ya kati. Tuzo ya Nelson mandela ya kukuza afya Mshindi wa tuzo ya 2023: Dk Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma kutoka Bahrain Pamoja na kuwa afisa mtendaji mkuu wa mamlaka ya kitaifa ya udhibiti wa afya ya Bahrain, Dk Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma ameshika nyadhifa mbalimbali za uongozi katika serikali na taasisi za kitaaluma nchini mwake. Dk Al Jalahma amekuwa na mchango mkubwa katika ukuzaji wa mikakati na mipango mingi ya kitaifa na kikanda katika nyanja ya afya ya msingi, afya ya watoto na vijana, magonjwa sugu na uachaji wa uvutaji sigara. Katika nafasi yake ya sasa, Dk A Jalahma ameongoza mipango mikuu yenye mafanikio, kama vile mfumo wa kitaifa wa ithibati kwa taasisi za afya, na mipango kadhaa inayosababisha kuongezeka kwa fursa na ubora katika sekta ya afya.
translations.reference: Having promoted community participation in hospital care for over 20 years, Dr Jorge Francisco Meneses set up community-based committees focusing on the safety and satisfaction of patients, their families and the community. Given the positive impact of his work in improving the quality of patient care, the model of ethical and patient-centred care that he designed was scaled up at the national level. Nowadays, as the national supervisor for hospitals of Guatemala, he has designed a programme to improve the quality of services for hospital patients and their families. Dr Meneses has recently coordinated an innovative pilot project in the Guatemala South Health Area to build the first national integrated network of health services, with a priority focus on underserved communities. His work led to the adoption of a national strategy of integrated health services networks, the first in Central America. Nelson Mandela Award for Health Promotion 2023 award laureate: Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma from Bahrain In addition to being the Chief Executive Officer of the National Health Regulatory Authority of Bahrain, Dr Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma has held various leadership positions in government and academic institutions in her country. Dr Al Jalahma has played a major role in the development of numerous national and region-wide strategies and plans in the field of primary healthcare, child and adolescent health, chronic diseases and antismoking. In her current role, Dr Al Jalahma has led major successful initiatives, such as the national accreditation system for health institutions, the digital transformation of the National Health Regulatory Authority, and several initiatives leading to increased efficiency and excellence in the health sector.
translations.hypothesis: After more than 20 years of fostering community involvement in hospital services, Dr. Jorge Francisco Meneses established community committees focused on patient and family safety and satisfaction. Due to the positive results of his work in improving patient care, a model of ethical and patient-centered care was adopted at the national level. Today, as the national director of Guatemalan hospitals, he has developed a plan to improve the quality of care for patients and their families. Recently, Dr. Meneses has overseen a groundbreaking pilot project in southern Guatemala to create the first national network of health services, with a focus on underserved communities. His work has led to the adoption of the first national strategy for integrated health service networks in Central America. Nelson Mandela Award for Improving Health: 2023 Winner: Dr. Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma from Bahrain. In addition to being the Chief Executive Officer of the national health regulatory authority in Bahrain, Dr. Mariam Athbi Al Jalahma has held various leadership positions in government and academic institutions in her country. Dr. Al Jalahma has made significant contributions to the development of numerous national and regional strategies and programs in the fields of primary health care, child and adolescent health, chronic diseases, and tobacco control. In her current role, Dr. Al Jalahma has led successful initiatives, such as the national accreditation system for health facilities, and several programs that have led to increased opportunities and quality in the health sector.

translations.source: Pia yeye ni mtu maarufu kwa ajili kukuza afya katika njia mbalimbali katika nchi zote za Baraza la ushirikiano la Ghuba.
translations.reference: She is also a prominent voice for health promotion across various channels throughout all countries of the Gulf Cooperation Council.

translations.source: WHO na Jamhuri ya watu wa Korea zatia saini makubaliano ya kihistoria ili kuongeza uwezo wa utengenezaji wa Viumbe hai WHO na Jamhuri ya watu wa korea zimetia saini mkataba wa maelewano ili kuanzisha kitovu cha mafunzo cha kimataifa katika utengenezaji wa viumbe hai. Kituo hiki cha mafunzo cha kimataifa kitahudumia nchi zote za kipato cha chini na cha kati (LMICs) kuzalisha Biolojia kama vile Insulin, kinga za monokloni na dawa za saratani. Janga la UVIKO-19 liliangazia hitaji la kupanua uzalishaji wa ndani na wa kikanda wa chanjo na bidhaa nyingine muhimu za afya katika LMICs ili kuhakikisha ufikiaji sawa. Ili kuzipa nchi ujuzi wa kuzalisha Chanjo za mRNA kwa haraka, WHO, kwa kushirikiana na washirika, ilianzisha mpango wa uhawilishaji Teknolojia wa mRNA, na uzinduzi rasmi wa kituo cha Hub nchini Afrika Kusini mwezi Aprili 2023. Juhudi hizi za kimataifa na mipango mingine ya hivi karibuni ya uzalishaji wa ndani inataka mikakati kabambe ya maendeleo ya wafanyakazi ili kuajiri, kuongeza ujuzi na kuhifadhi wafanyakazi wanaofanya kazi katia vituo hivi,na umahiri wa kipekee unaohitajika katika uzalishaji wa viumbe hai. Ili kukabiliana na pengo hili, WHO na Jamhuri ya korea zimeshirikiana kwa karibu kwa zaidi ya mwaka mmoja na sasa zinarasimisha dhamira yao ya kuanzishwa kwa kitovu cha mafunzo cha kimataifa katika utengenezaji wa viumbe hai. "Tunayo furaha kutia saini mkataba huu wa makubaliano ili kuinua ushirikiano wetu hadi ngazi nyengine na kushirikiana katika utayarishaji wa mtaala mpana wa mafunzo. Tunatarajia kitovu hiki cha mafunzo cha hadhi ya kimataifa kitasaidia kujenga nguvu kazi yenye ujuzi na kuhakikisha bidhaa bora za afya zinaweza kutengenezwa Duniani kote", alisema DK Yukiko Nakatani, mkurugenzi mkuu msaidizi wa WHO, upatikanaji wa dawa na bidhaa za afya. Serikali ya Jamhuri ya Korea inatoa anuwai kubwa ya vifaa vya mafunzo ya kitaifa na programu zinazohusu wigo kamili wa utengenezaji wa kibiolojia na inapanua shughuli zake ili kuchukua wanafunzi zaidi kutoka nchi nyingine kwa ujenzi wa kampasi ya elimu ya viumbe hai ya kimataifa itakayozinduliwa mnamo 2025.
translations.reference: WHO and Republic of Korea sign landmark agreement to boost biomanufacturing capacity WHO and the Republic of Korea have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to establish a global training hub in biomanufacturing. This global training centre will serve all low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) to produce biologicals, such as vaccines, insulin, monoclonal antibodies, and cancer medicines. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need to expand the local and regional production of vaccines and other essential health products in LMICs to ensure equitable access. To provide countries with the know-how to rapidly produce mRNA vaccines, WHO, in collaboration with partners, established the mRNA Technology Transfer programme, with the formal inauguration of the Hub facility in South Africa in April 2023. This multilateral effort and other recent local production initiatives call for ambitious workforce development strategies to recruit, upskill and retain the staff working at these facilities, with unique competencies required in bioproduction. To address this gap, WHO and the Republic of Korea have engaged in closed collaboration for over a year and are now formalizing their commitment towards the establishment of a global training hub in biomanufacturing. “We are delighted to sign this Memorandum of Understanding to elevate our partnership to the next level and collaborate in the development of a comprehensive training curriculum. We hope this world-class training hub will help to build a skilled workforce and ensure quality health products can be manufactured around the world,” said Dr Yukiko Nakatani, WHO Assistant Director-General, Access to Medicines and Health Products. The Government of the Republic of Korea is offering a large range of national training facilities and programmes covering the full spectrum of biological manufacturing and is expanding its operations to accommodate more trainees from other countries with the construction of a Global Bio Education Campus to be launched in 2025.
translations.hypothesis: WHO and the Republic of Korea have signed a historic agreement to enhance the capacity for the production of biological products. This international training center will serve all low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) in the production of biologics such as insulin, monoclonal antibodies, and cancer drugs. The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need to expand local and regional production of vaccines and other essential health products in LMICs to ensure equitable access. To help countries develop the capacity to rapidly produce mRNA vaccines, WHO, in partnership with other organizations, launched the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub, with its official launch in South Africa in April 2023. These international efforts and recent initiatives to promote local production aim to create sustainable development strategies for the workforce, including recruitment, training, and retention of workers in these facilities, as well as the specialized skills needed in biological product manufacturing. To address this gap, WHO and the Republic of Korea have been working closely together for over a year and are now formalizing their commitment to establish an international training center for biological product manufacturing. "We are pleased to sign this agreement to further strengthen our collaboration and work together to develop a comprehensive training program. We believe that this internationally recognized training center will help build a skilled workforce and ensure that high-quality health products can be manufactured worldwide," said DK Yukiko Nakatani, Deputy Director-General of WHO, responsible for access to medicines and health products. The government of the Republic of Korea offers a wide range of national training facilities and programs covering the full spectrum of biological manufacturing, and is expanding its activities to accommodate more students from other countries in the development of a global biological product training campus, which will be launched in 2025.

translations.source: "Ninatumai mkataba huu wa maelewano utaimarisha ushirikiano kati ya Jamhuri ya Korea na WHO ili kuimarisha uwezo na utengenezaji wa viumbe hai wa LMICs", alisema Bw Hwang Seunghyun mkurugenzi mkuu, ofisi za kitovu cha chanjo cha kimataifa katika wizara ya afya na ustawi kutoka Jamhuri ya Korea. Sambamba na hilo, WHO inaimarisha uimarishaji wa mfumo wa udhibiti ili kuhakikisha ubora na usalama wa bidhaa za afya. Mashirika yenye nguvu zaidi ya udhibiti pia yataongeza imani katika bidhaa zinazozalishwa nchini na kukabiliana na taarifa potofu na upatikanaji wa dawa zisizo na viwango, zisizo salama au zilizoghushiwa.
translations.reference: “I hope this Memorandum of Understanding will enhance cooperation between the Republic of Korea and WHO to strengthen biomanufacturing capacities of LMICs,” said Mr Hwang Seunghyun Director General, Global Vaccine Hub Office at the Ministry of Health and Welfare from Republic of Korea. In parallel, WHO is intensifying regulatory system strengthening to ensure the quality and safety of health products. Stronger regulatory agencies will also enhance confidence in locally produced products and counter misinformation and the availability of unsafe substandard or falsified medicines.
translations.hypothesis: "I hope this agreement will strengthen the partnership between the Republic of Korea and WHO in enhancing the production and manufacturing of vaccines for LMICs," said Mr. Hwang Seunghyun, Director-General, International Vaccine Center, Ministry of Health and Welfare, from the Republic of Korea. In addition, WHO is working to strengthen the regulatory system to ensure the quality and safety of health products. Stronger regulatory bodies will also increase confidence in domestically produced products and help to combat counterfeit and substandard medicines.

translations.source: WHO yazitaka serikali kuacha kutoa ruzuku kwa mazao ya Tumbaku yanayohatarisha maisha Katika siku ya kuzuia Tumbaku Duniani, WHO inazitaka serikali kuacha kutoa ruzuku kwa kilimo cha Tumbaku na kuunga mkono mazao endelevu ambayo yanaweza kulisha mamilioni ya watu. "Tumbaku inahusika na vifo milioni 8 kwa mwaka, serikali kote ulimwenguni hutumia mamilioni kusaidia mashamba ya Tumbaku", alisema Dk Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, mkurugenzi mkuu wa WHO. "Kwa kuchagua kulima mazao ya chakula badala ya Tumbaku, tunatanguliza afya, kuhifadhi mifumo ya ikolojia, na kuimarisha usalama wa chakula kwa wote" Zaidi ya watu milioni 300 Duniani wanakabiliwa na uhaba wa chakula. Wakati huohuo zaidi ya hekta milioni 3 za ardhi katika zaidi ya 120 zinatumiwa kulima Tumbaku hatari, hata nchi ambazo watu wana njaa. Ripoti mpya ya WHO, "Lima chakula, si Tumbaku", inaangazia matatizo ya ukuzaji wa Tumbaku na faida za kubadili mazao ya chakula endelevu kwa wakulima, jamii, uchumi, mazingira na Dunia kwa ujumla. Pia ripoti hiyo ilifichua sekta ya Tumbaku kwa kuwanasa wakulima katika msululu mbaya wa madeni, kueneza kilimo cha Tumbaku kwa kuzidisha faida zake za kiuchumi na ushawishi kupitia vikundi vya mwanzo vya wakulima. Kilimo cha Tumbaku kinasababisha magonjwa kwa wakulima wenyewe na zaidi ya vibarua milioni 1 wanakadiriwa kufanya kazi kwenye mashamba ya Tumbaku, wakikosa fursa ya kupata elimu. "Tumbaku sio tu ni tishio kwa uhaba wa chakula, lakini afya kwa ujumla, ikiwa ni pamoja na afya ya wakulima wa Tumbaku.
